T = exp{-2 pi^2^ sum~i~[sum~j~(U^ij^ h~i~ h~j~ a*~i~ a*~j~)]}

h  = the Miller indices a* = the reciprocal space cell lengths

These matrix elements may appear with atomic coordinates in the ATOM_SITE category, or they may appear in the separate ATOM_SITE_ANISOTROP category, but they may not appear in both places. Similarly, anisotropic displacements may appear as either B's or U's, but not as both.

The unique elements of the real symmetric matrix are entered by row.

_atom_site.aniso_U[2][3]_esd.name: _atom_site.aniso_U[2][3]_esd
_atom_site.aniso_U[2][3]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.aniso_U[2][3].

_atom_site.aniso_U[3][3].name: _atom_site.aniso_U[3][3]
_atom_site.aniso_U[3][3].description:
The [3][3] element of the standard anisotropic atomic displacement matrix U, which appears in the structure-factor term as:

T = exp{-2 pi^2^ sum~i~[sum~j~(U^ij^ h~i~ h~j~ a*~i~ a*~j~)]}

h  = the Miller indices a* = the reciprocal space cell lengths

These matrix elements may appear with atomic coordinates in the ATOM_SITE category, or they may appear in the separate ATOM_SITE_ANISOTROP category, but they may not appear in both places. Similarly, anisotropic displacements may appear as either B's or U's, but not as both.

The unique elements of the real symmetric matrix are entered by row.

_atom_site.aniso_U[3][3]_esd.name: _atom_site.aniso_U[3][3]_esd
_atom_site.aniso_U[3][3]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.aniso_U[3][3].

_atom_site.attached_hydrogens.name: _atom_site.attached_hydrogens
_atom_site.attached_hydrogens.description: The number of hydrogen atoms attached to the atom at this site excluding any hydrogen atoms for which coordinates (measured or calculated) are given.

_atom_site.auth_asym_id.name: _atom_site.auth_asym_id
_atom_site.auth_asym_id.description: An alternative identifier for _atom_site.label_asym_id that may be provided by an author in order to match the identification used in the publication that describes the structure.

_atom_site.auth_atom_id.name: _atom_site.auth_atom_id
_atom_site.auth_atom_id.description: An alternative identifier for _atom_site.label_atom_id that may be provided by an author in order to match the identification used in the publication that describes the structure.

_atom_site.auth_comp_id.name: _atom_site.auth_comp_id
_atom_site.auth_comp_id.description: An alternative identifier for _atom_site.label_comp_id that may be provided by an author in order to match the identification used in the publication that describes the structure.

_atom_site.auth_seq_id.name: _atom_site.auth_seq_id
_atom_site.auth_seq_id.description:
An alternative identifier for _atom_site.label_seq_id that may be provided by an author in order to match the identification used in the publication that describes the structure.

Note that this is not necessarily a number, that the values do not have to be positive, and that the value does not have to correspond to the value of _atom_site.label_seq_id. The value of _atom_site.label_seq_id is required to be a sequential list of positive integers.

The author may assign values to _atom_site.auth_seq_id in any desired way. For instance, the values may be used to relate this structure to a numbering scheme in a homologous structure, including sequence gaps or insertion codes. Alternatively, a scheme may be used for a truncated polymer that maintains the numbering scheme of the full length polymer. In all cases, the scheme used here must match the scheme used in the publication that describes the structure.

_atom_site.B_equiv_geom_mean.name: _atom_site.B_equiv_geom_mean
_atom_site.B_equiv_geom_mean.description:
Equivalent isotropic atomic displacement parameter, B~eq~, in angstroms squared, calculated as the geometric mean of the anisotropic atomic displacement parameters.

B~eq~ = (B~i~ B~j~ B~k~)^1/3^

B~n~ = the principal components of the orthogonalized B^ij^

The IUCr Commission on Nomenclature recommends against the use of B for reporting atomic displacement parameters. U, being directly proportional to B, is preferred.

_atom_site.B_equiv_geom_mean_esd.name: _atom_site.B_equiv_geom_mean_esd
_atom_site.B_equiv_geom_mean_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.B_equiv_geom_mean.

_atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv.name: _atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv
_atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv.description:
Isotropic atomic displacement parameter, or equivalent isotropic atomic displacement parameter, B~eq~, calculated from the anisotropic displacement parameters.

B~eq~ = (1/3) sum~i~[sum~j~(B^ij^ A~i~ A~j~ a*~i~ a*~j~)]

A     = the real space cell lengths a*    = the reciprocal space cell lengths B^ij^ = 8 pi^2^ U^ij^

Ref: Fischer, R. X. & Tillmanns, E. (1988). Acta Cryst. C44, 775-776.

The IUCr Commission on Nomenclature recommends against the use of B for reporting atomic displacement parameters. U, being directly proportional to B, is preferred.

Note -

The particular type of ADP stored in this item is qualified by item _refine.pdbx_adp_type.

_atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv_esd.name: _atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv_esd
_atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv.

_atom_site.calc_attached_atom.name: _atom_site.calc_attached_atom
_atom_site.calc_attached_atom.description: The _atom_site.id of the atom site to which the 'geometry-calculated' atom site is attached.

_atom_site.calc_flag.name: _atom_site.calc_flag
_atom_site.calc_flag.description: A standard code to signal whether the site coordinates have been determined from the intensities or calculated from the geometry of surrounding sites, or have been assigned dummy values. The abbreviation 'c' may be used in place of 'calc'.

_atom_site.Cartn_x.name: _atom_site.Cartn_x
_atom_site.Cartn_x.description: The x atom-site coordinate in angstroms specified according to a set of orthogonal Cartesian axes related to the cell axes as specified by the description given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_atom_site.Cartn_x_esd.name: _atom_site.Cartn_x_esd
_atom_site.Cartn_x_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.Cartn_x.

_atom_site.Cartn_y.name: _atom_site.Cartn_y
_atom_site.Cartn_y.description: The y atom-site coordinate in angstroms specified according to a set of orthogonal Cartesian axes related to the cell axes as specified by the description given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_atom_site.Cartn_y_esd.name: _atom_site.Cartn_y_esd
_atom_site.Cartn_y_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.Cartn_y.

_atom_site.Cartn_z.name: _atom_site.Cartn_z
_atom_site.Cartn_z.description: The z atom-site coordinate in angstroms specified according to a set of orthogonal Cartesian axes related to the cell axes as specified by the description given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_atom_site.Cartn_z_esd.name: _atom_site.Cartn_z_esd
_atom_site.Cartn_z_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.Cartn_z.

_atom_site.chemical_conn_number.name: _atom_site.chemical_conn_number
_atom_site.chemical_conn_number.description: This data item is a pointer to _chemical_conn_atom.number in the CHEMICAL_CONN_ATOM category.

_atom_site.constraints.name: _atom_site.constraints
_atom_site.constraints.description: A description of the constraints applied to parameters at this site during refinement. See also _atom_site.refinement_flags and _refine.ls_number_constraints.

_atom_site.details.name: _atom_site.details
_atom_site.details.description: A description of special aspects of this site. See also _atom_site.refinement_flags.

_atom_site.disorder_assembly.name: _atom_site.disorder_assembly
_atom_site.disorder_assembly.description:
A code which identifies a cluster of atoms that show long-range positional disorder but are locally ordered. Within each such cluster of atoms, _atom_site.disorder_group is used to identify the sites that are simultaneously occupied. This field is only needed if there is more than one cluster of disordered atoms showing independent local order.

*** This data item would not in general be used in a macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_site.disorder_group.name: _atom_site.disorder_group
_atom_site.disorder_group.description:
A code which identifies a group of positionally disordered atom sites that are locally simultaneously occupied. Atoms that are positionally disordered over two or more sites (e.g. the hydrogen atoms of a methyl group that exists in two orientations) can be assigned to two or more groups. Sites belonging to the same group are simultaneously occupied, but those belonging to different groups are not. A minus prefix (e.g. '-1') is used to indicate sites disordered about a special position.

*** This data item would not in general be used in a macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_site.footnote_id.name: _atom_site.footnote_id
_atom_site.footnote_id.description: The value of _atom_site.footnote_id must match an ID specified by _atom_sites_footnote.id in the ATOM_SITES_FOOTNOTE list.

_atom_site.fract_x.name: _atom_site.fract_x
_atom_site.fract_x.description: The x coordinate of the atom-site position specified as a fraction of _cell.length_a.

_atom_site.fract_x_esd.name: _atom_site.fract_x_esd
_atom_site.fract_x_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.fract_x.

_atom_site.fract_y.name: _atom_site.fract_y
_atom_site.fract_y.description: The y coordinate of the atom-site position specified as a fraction of _cell.length_b.

_atom_site.fract_y_esd.name: _atom_site.fract_y_esd
_atom_site.fract_y_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.fract_y.

_atom_site.fract_z.name: _atom_site.fract_z
_atom_site.fract_z.description: The z coordinate of the atom-site position specified as a fraction of _cell.length_c.

_atom_site.fract_z_esd.name: _atom_site.fract_z_esd
_atom_site.fract_z_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.fract_z.

_atom_site.group_PDB.name: _atom_site.group_PDB
_atom_site.group_PDB.description: The group of atoms to which the atom site belongs. This data item is provided for compatibility with the original Protein Data Bank format, and only for that purpose.

_atom_site.id.name: _atom_site.id
_atom_site.id.description:
The value of _atom_site.id must uniquely identify a record in the ATOM_SITE list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

This data item was introduced to provide compatibility between small-molecule and macromolecular CIFs. In a small-molecule CIF, _atom_site_label is the identifier for the atom. In a macromolecular CIF, the atom identifier is the aggregate of _atom_site.label_alt_id, _atom_site.label_asym_id, _atom_site.label_atom_id, _atom_site.label_comp_id and _atom_site.label_seq_id. For the two types of files to be compatible, a formal identifier for the category had to be introduced that was independent of the different modes of identifying the atoms. For compatibility with older CIFs, _atom_site_label is aliased to _atom_site.id.

In general, this aggregate identifier does not uniquely identify an atom site as for non-polymers _atom_site.label_seq_id is '.'.

_atom_site.label_alt_id.name: _atom_site.label_alt_id
_atom_site.label_alt_id.description: A place holder to indicate alternate conformation. The alternate conformation can be an entire polymer chain, or several residues or partial residue (several atoms within one residue). If an atom is provided in more than one position, then a non-blank alternate location indicator must be used for each of the atomic positions.

_atom_site.label_asym_id.name: _atom_site.label_asym_id
_atom_site.label_asym_id.description:
A component of the identifier for this atom site. For further details, see the definition of the STRUCT_ASYM category.

This data item is a pointer to _struct_asym.id in the STRUCT_ASYM category.

_atom_site.label_atom_id.name: _atom_site.label_atom_id
_atom_site.label_atom_id.description:
A component of the identifier for this atom site.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_atom_site.label_comp_id.name: _atom_site.label_comp_id
_atom_site.label_comp_id.description:
A component of the identifier for this atom site.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_atom_site.label_entity_id.name: _atom_site.label_entity_id
_atom_site.label_entity_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entity.id in the ENTITY category.

_atom_site.label_seq_id.name: _atom_site.label_seq_id
_atom_site.label_seq_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entity_poly_seq.num in the ENTITY_POLY_SEQ category.

_atom_site.occupancy.name: _atom_site.occupancy
_atom_site.occupancy.description: The fraction of the atom type present at this site. The sum of the occupancies of all the atom types at this site may not exceed 1.0 unless it is a dummy site.

_atom_site.occupancy_esd.name: _atom_site.occupancy_esd
_atom_site.occupancy_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.occupancy.

_atom_site.restraints.name: _atom_site.restraints
_atom_site.restraints.description: A description of restraints applied to specific parameters at this site during refinement. See also _atom_site.refinement_flags and _refine.ls_number_restraints.

_atom_site.symmetry_multiplicity.name: _atom_site.symmetry_multiplicity
_atom_site.symmetry_multiplicity.description: The multiplicity of a site due to the space-group symmetry as is given in International Tables for Crystallography Vol. A (2002).

_atom_site.thermal_displace_type.name: _atom_site.thermal_displace_type
_atom_site.thermal_displace_type.description: A standard code used to describe the type of atomic displacement parameters used for the site.

_atom_site.type_symbol.name: _atom_site.type_symbol
_atom_site.type_symbol.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_type.symbol in the ATOM_TYPE category.

_atom_site.U_equiv_geom_mean.name: _atom_site.U_equiv_geom_mean
_atom_site.U_equiv_geom_mean.description:
Equivalent isotropic atomic displacement parameter, U~eq~, in angstroms squared, calculated as the geometric mean of the anisotropic atomic displacement parameters.

U~eq~ = (U~i~ U~j~ U~k~)^1/3^

U~n~ = the principal components of the orthogonalized U^ij^

_atom_site.U_equiv_geom_mean_esd.name: _atom_site.U_equiv_geom_mean_esd
_atom_site.U_equiv_geom_mean_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.U_equiv_geom_mean.

_atom_site.U_iso_or_equiv.name: _atom_site.U_iso_or_equiv
_atom_site.U_iso_or_equiv.description:
Isotropic atomic displacement parameter, or equivalent isotropic atomic  displacement parameter, U~eq~, calculated from anisotropic atomic displacement  parameters.

U~eq~ = (1/3) sum~i~[sum~j~(U^ij^ A~i~ A~j~ a*~i~ a*~j~)]

A  = the real space cell lengths a* = the reciprocal space cell lengths

Ref: Fischer, R. X. & Tillmanns, E. (1988). Acta Cryst. C44, 775-776.

_atom_site.U_iso_or_equiv_esd.name: _atom_site.U_iso_or_equiv_esd
_atom_site.U_iso_or_equiv_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site.U_iso_or_equiv.

_atom_site.Wyckoff_symbol.name: _atom_site.Wyckoff_symbol
_atom_site.Wyckoff_symbol.description: The Wyckoff symbol (letter) as listed in the space-group tables of International Tables for Crystallography, Vol. A (2002).

_atom_site.pdbx_atom_ambiguity.name: _atom_site.pdbx_atom_ambiguity
_atom_site.pdbx_atom_ambiguity.description: The optional value of _atom_site.pdbx_atom_ambiguity atoms that differ only by stereochemistry but are not stereospecifically assigned by the experiment.

_atom_site.adp_type.name: _atom_site.adp_type
_atom_site.adp_type.description: A standard code used to describe the type of atomic displacement parameters used for the site.

_atom_site.refinement_flags.name: _atom_site.refinement_flags
_atom_site.refinement_flags.description: A concatenated series of single-letter codes which indicate the refinement restraints or constraints applied to this site.  This item should not be used.  It has been replaced by _atom_site.refinement_flags_posn, *_adp and *_occupancy. It is retained in this dictionary only to provide compatibility with old CIFs.
enums: distance or angle restraint on site
riding-atom site attached to non-riding atom

_atom_site.refinement_flags_adp.name: _atom_site.refinement_flags_adp
_atom_site.refinement_flags_adp.description: A code which indicates the refinement restraints or constraints applied to the atomic displacement parameters of this site.
enums: special-position constraints on atomic displacement parameters

_atom_site.refinement_flags_occupancy.name: _atom_site.refinement_flags_occupancy
_atom_site.refinement_flags_occupancy.description: A code which indicates that refinement restraints or constraints were applied to the occupancy of this site.

_atom_site.refinement_flags_posn.name: _atom_site.refinement_flags_posn
_atom_site.refinement_flags_posn.description: A code which indicates the refinement restraints or constraints applied to the positional coordinates of this site.
enums: distance or angle restraint on positional coordinates
riding-atom site attached to non-riding atom

_atom_site.pdbx_formal_charge.name: _atom_site.pdbx_formal_charge
_atom_site.pdbx_formal_charge.description: The net integer charge assigned to this atom. This is the formal charge assignment normally found in chemical diagrams.

_atom_site.pdbx_tls_group_id.name: _atom_site.pdbx_tls_group_id
_atom_site.pdbx_tls_group_id.description:
The TLS group to which the atom position is assigned.

The TLS group is defined in category pdbx_refine_tls. This item is a reference to _pdbx_refine_tls.id.

_atom_site.pdbx_ncs_dom_id.name: _atom_site.pdbx_ncs_dom_id
_atom_site.pdbx_ncs_dom_id.description:
The NCS domain to which the atom position is assigned.

The NCS group is defined in category struct_ncs_dom. This item is a reference to _struct_ncs_dom.id.

_atom_site.pdbx_struct_group_id.name: _atom_site.pdbx_struct_group_id
_atom_site.pdbx_struct_group_id.description:
The value of _atom_site.pdbx_struct_group_id identifies the group or groups assigned to this atom.    This is a reference to the identifier for group definition in category PDBX_STRUCT_GROUP_LIST.

Multiple groups identifiers are encoded as a comma separated list.

_atom_site.pdbx_group_NDB.name: _atom_site.pdbx_group_NDB
_atom_site.pdbx_group_NDB.description: The ATOM group code used by the NDB.

_atom_site.pdbx_atom_group.name: _atom_site.pdbx_atom_group
_atom_site.pdbx_atom_group.description: The ATOM group code used by the NDB.

_atom_site.pdbx_label_seq_num.name: _atom_site.pdbx_label_seq_num
_atom_site.pdbx_label_seq_num.description: Sequential residue number used by NDB.

_atom_site.pdbx_not_in_asym.name: _atom_site.pdbx_not_in_asym
_atom_site.pdbx_not_in_asym.description: Will identify with a 'Y' that this strand got generated.

_atom_site.pdbx_label_index.name: _atom_site.pdbx_label_index
_atom_site.pdbx_label_index.description: This data item is an ordinal which identifies distinct chemical components in the atom_site category, both polymeric and non-polymeric.

_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_name.name: _atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_name
_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_name.description: The name of additional external databases with residue level mapping.

_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_acc.name: _atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_acc
_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_acc.description: The accession code related to the additional external database entry.

_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_num.name: _atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_num
_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_num.description: The sequence position of the external database entry that corresponds to the residue mapping defined by the SIFTS process.

_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_res.name: _atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_res
_atom_site.pdbx_sifts_xref_db_res.description: Describes the residue type of the given UniProt match

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][1].name: _atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][1]
_atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][1].description:
The [1][1] element of the anisotropic atomic displacement matrix B, which appears in the structure-factor term as:

T = exp{-1/4 sum~i~[sum~j~(B^ij^ h~i~ h~j~ a*~i~ a*~j~)]}

h  = the Miller indices a* = the reciprocal space cell lengths

These matrix elements may appear with atomic coordinates in the ATOM_SITE category, or they may appear in the separate ATOM_SITE_ANISOTROP category, but they may not appear in both places. Similarly, anisotropic displacements may appear as either B's or U's, but not as both.

The unique elements of the real symmetric matrix are entered by row.

The IUCr Commission on Nomenclature recommends against the use of B for reporting atomic displacement parameters. U, being directly proportional to B, is preferred.

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][1]_esd.name: _atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][1]_esd
_atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][1]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][1].

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][2]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][2].

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][3]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site_anisotrop.B[1][3].

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][2]_esd.name: _atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][2]_esd
_atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][2]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][2].

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][3].description:

T = exp{-1/4 sum~i~[sum~j~(B^ij^ h~i~ h~j~ a*~i~ a*~j~)]}

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][3]_esd.name: _atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][3]_esd
_atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][3]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site_anisotrop.B[2][3].

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[3][3].name: _atom_site_anisotrop.B[3][3]
_atom_site_anisotrop.B[3][3].description:
The [3][3] element of the anisotropic atomic displacement matrix B, which appears in the structure-factor term as:

T = exp{-1/4 sum~i~[sum~j~(B^ij^ h~i~ h~j~ a*~i~ a*~j~)]}

h  = the Miller indices a* = the reciprocal space cell lengths

These matrix elements may appear with atomic coordinates in the ATOM_SITE category, or they may appear in the separate ATOM_SITE_ANISOTROP category, but they may not appear in both places. Similarly, anisotropic displacements may appear as either B's or U's, but not as both.

The unique elements of the real symmetric matrix are entered by row.

The IUCr Commission on Nomenclature recommends against the use of B for reporting atomic displacement parameters. U, being directly proportional to B, is preferred.

_atom_site_anisotrop.B[3][3]_esd.name: _atom_site_anisotrop.B[3][3]_esd
_atom_site_anisotrop.B[3][3]_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _atom_site_anisotrop.B[3][3].

_atom_site_anisotrop.ratio.name: _atom_site_anisotrop.ratio
_atom_site_anisotrop.ratio.description: Ratio of the maximum to minimum principal axes of displacement (thermal) ellipsoids.

_atom_sites.Cartn_transf_matrix[1][1].name: _atom_sites.Cartn_transf_matrix[1][1]
_atom_sites.Cartn_transf_matrix[1][1].description:
The [1][1] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform fractional coordinates in the ATOM_SITE category to Cartesian coordinates in the same category. The axial alignments of this transformation are described in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes. The 3x1 translation is defined in _atom_sites.Cartn_transf_vector[].

|x'|              |11 12 13| |x|               |1| |y'|~Cartesian~ = |21 22 23| |y|~fractional~ + |2| |z'|              |31 32 33| |z|               |3|

_atom_sites.solution_primary.name: _atom_sites.solution_primary
_atom_sites.solution_primary.description:
This code identifies the method used to locate the initial atom sites.

*** This data item would not in general be used in a macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_sites.solution_secondary.name: _atom_sites.solution_secondary
_atom_sites.solution_secondary.description:
This code identifies the method used to locate the non-hydrogen-atom sites not found by _atom_sites.solution_primary.

*** This data item would not in general be used in a macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_sites.solution_hydrogens.name: _atom_sites.solution_hydrogens
_atom_sites.solution_hydrogens.description:
This code identifies the method used to locate the hydrogen atoms.

*** This data item would not in general be used in a macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_sites.special_details.name: _atom_sites.special_details
_atom_sites.special_details.description: Additional information about the atomic coordinates not coded elsewhere in the CIF.

_atom_sites_alt.details.name: _atom_sites_alt.details
_atom_sites_alt.details.description: A description of special aspects of the modelling of atoms in alternative conformations.

_atom_sites_alt.id.name: _atom_sites_alt.id
_atom_sites_alt.id.description:
The value of _atom_sites_alt.id must uniquely identify a record in the ATOM_SITES_ALT list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_atom_sites_alt_ens.details.name: _atom_sites_alt_ens.details
_atom_sites_alt_ens.details.description: A description of special aspects of the ensemble structure generated from atoms with various alternative IDs.

_atom_sites_alt_ens.id.name: _atom_sites_alt_ens.id
_atom_sites_alt_ens.id.description:
The value of _atom_sites_alt_ens.id must uniquely identify a record in the ATOM_SITES_ALT_ENS list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_atom_sites_alt_gen.alt_id.name: _atom_sites_alt_gen.alt_id
_atom_sites_alt_gen.alt_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_sites_alt.id in the ATOM_SITES_ALT category.

_atom_sites_alt_gen.ens_id.name: _atom_sites_alt_gen.ens_id
_atom_sites_alt_gen.ens_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_sites_alt_ens.id in the ATOM_SITES_ALT_ENS category.

_atom_sites_footnote.id.name: _atom_sites_footnote.id
_atom_sites_footnote.id.description: A code that identifies the footnote.

_atom_sites_footnote.text.name: _atom_sites_footnote.text
_atom_sites_footnote.text.description:
The text of the footnote. Footnotes are used to describe an atom site or a group of atom sites in the ATOM_SITE list.

For example, footnotes may be used to indicate atoms for which the electron density is very weak, or atoms for which static disorder has been modelled.

_atom_type.analytical_mass_percent.name: _atom_type.analytical_mass_percent
_atom_type.analytical_mass_percent.description: Mass percentage of this atom type derived from chemical analysis.

_atom_type.description.name: _atom_type.description
_atom_type.description.description: A description of the atom(s) designated by this atom type. In most cases, this is the element name and oxidation state of a single atom species. For disordered or nonstoichiometric structures it will describe a combination of atom species.

_atom_type.number_in_cell.name: _atom_type.number_in_cell
_atom_type.number_in_cell.description: Total number of atoms of this atom type in the unit cell.

_atom_type.oxidation_number.name: _atom_type.oxidation_number
_atom_type.oxidation_number.description: Formal oxidation state of this atom type in the structure.

_atom_type.radius_bond.name: _atom_type.radius_bond
_atom_type.radius_bond.description: The effective intramolecular bonding radius in angstroms of this atom type.

_atom_type.radius_contact.name: _atom_type.radius_contact
_atom_type.radius_contact.description: The effective intermolecular bonding radius in angstroms of this atom type.

_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_a1.name: _atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_a1
_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_a1.description:
The Cromer-Mann scattering-factor coefficient a1 used to calculate the scattering factors for this atom type.

Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974). Vol. IV, Table 2.2B or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C, Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_c.description:

_atom_type.scat_dispersion_imag.name: _atom_type.scat_dispersion_imag
_atom_type.scat_dispersion_imag.description: The imaginary component of the anomalous-dispersion scattering factor, f'', in electrons for this atom type and the radiation identified by _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id.

_atom_type.scat_dispersion_real.name: _atom_type.scat_dispersion_real
_atom_type.scat_dispersion_real.description: The real component of the anomalous-dispersion scattering factor, f', in electrons for this atom type and the radiation identified by _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id.

_atom_type.scat_length_neutron.name: _atom_type.scat_length_neutron
_atom_type.scat_length_neutron.description: The bound coherent scattering length in femtometres for the atom type at the isotopic composition used for the diffraction experiment.

_atom_type.scat_source.name: _atom_type.scat_source
_atom_type.scat_source.description: Reference to the source of the scattering factors or scattering lengths used for this atom type.

_atom_type.scat_versus_stol_list.name: _atom_type.scat_versus_stol_list
_atom_type.scat_versus_stol_list.description: A table of scattering factors as a function of sin theta over lambda. This table should be well commented to indicate the items present. Regularly formatted lists are strongly recommended.

_atom_type.symbol.name: _atom_type.symbol
_atom_type.symbol.description: The code used to identify the atom species (singular or plural) representing this atom type. Normally this code is the element symbol. The code may be composed of any character except an underscore with the additional proviso that digits designate an oxidation state and must be followed by a + or - character.

_atom_type.scat_dispersion_source.name: _atom_type.scat_dispersion_source
_atom_type.scat_dispersion_source.description: Reference to the source of the real and imaginary dispersion corrections for scattering factors used for this atom type.

_atom_type.pdbx_scat_Cromer_Mann_a5.name: _atom_type.pdbx_scat_Cromer_Mann_a5
_atom_type.pdbx_scat_Cromer_Mann_a5.description:
Scattering-factor coefficient a5, used to calculate electron elastic atomic scattering factors for the defined atom type.

Electron Elastic Scattering Factors Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (2006). Vol. C, Table 4.3.2.2, pp. 282-283.

Cromer_Mann equation Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974). Vol. IV, Table 2.2B or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C, Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.pdbx_scat_Cromer_Mann_b5.description:

Electron Elastic Scattering Factors Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (2006). Vol. C, Table 4.3.2.2, pp. 282-283.

Cromer_Mann equation Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974). Vol. IV, Table 2.2B or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C, Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.pdbx_scat_Z.name: _atom_type.pdbx_scat_Z
_atom_type.pdbx_scat_Z.description: Atomic number of atom in scattering amplitude.

_atom_type.pdbx_N_electrons.name: _atom_type.pdbx_N_electrons
_atom_type.pdbx_N_electrons.description: Number of electrons in atom used in scattering factor

_audit.creation_date.name: _audit.creation_date
_audit.creation_date.description: A date that the data block was created. The date format is yyyy-mm-dd.

_audit.creation_method.name: _audit.creation_method
_audit.creation_method.description: A description of how data were entered into the data block.

_audit.revision_id.name: _audit.revision_id
_audit.revision_id.description: The value of _audit.revision_id must uniquely identify a record in the AUDIT list.

_audit.update_record.name: _audit.update_record
_audit.update_record.description: A record of any changes to the data block. The update format is a date (yyyy-mm-dd) followed by a description of the changes. The latest update entry is added to the bottom of this record.

_audit_author.address.name: _audit_author.address
_audit_author.address.description: The address of an author of this data block. If there are multiple authors, _audit_author.address is looped with _audit_author.name.
examples:
Department
 Institute
 Street
 City and postcode
 COUNTRY

_audit_author.name.name: _audit_author.name
_audit_author.name.description: The name of an author of this data block. If there are multiple authors, _audit_author.name is looped with _audit_author.address. The family name(s), followed by a comma and including any dynastic components, precedes the first name(s) or initial(s).

_audit_author.pdbx_ordinal.name: _audit_author.pdbx_ordinal
_audit_author.pdbx_ordinal.description: This data item defines the order of the author's name in the list of audit authors.

_audit_author.identifier_ORCID.name: _audit_author.identifier_ORCID
_audit_author.identifier_ORCID.description: The Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCID).

_audit_conform.dict_location.name: _audit_conform.dict_location
_audit_conform.dict_location.description: A file name or uniform resource locator (URL) for the dictionary to which the current data block conforms.

_audit_conform.dict_name.name: _audit_conform.dict_name
_audit_conform.dict_name.description: The string identifying the highest-level dictionary defining data names used in this file.

_audit_conform.dict_version.name: _audit_conform.dict_version
_audit_conform.dict_version.description: The version number of the dictionary to which the current data block conforms.

_audit_contact_author.address.name: _audit_contact_author.address
_audit_contact_author.address.description: The mailing address of the author of the data block to whom correspondence should be addressed.
examples:
Department
 Institute
 Street
 City and postcode
 COUNTRY

_audit_contact_author.email.name: _audit_contact_author.email
_audit_contact_author.email.description: The electronic mail address of the author of the data block to whom correspondence should be addressed, in a form recognizable to international networks. The format of e-mail addresses is given in Section 3.4, Address Specification, of Internet Message Format, RFC 2822, P. Resnick (Editor), Network Standards Group, April 2001.

_audit_contact_author.fax.name: _audit_contact_author.fax
_audit_contact_author.fax.description:
The facsimile telephone number of the author of the data block to whom correspondence should be addressed.

The recommended style starts with the international dialing prefix, followed by the area code in parentheses, followed by the local number with no spaces.

_audit_contact_author.name.name: _audit_contact_author.name
_audit_contact_author.name.description:
The name of the author of the data block to whom correspondence should be addressed.

The family name(s), followed by a comma and including any dynastic components, precedes the first name(s) or initial(s).

_audit_contact_author.phone.name: _audit_contact_author.phone
_audit_contact_author.phone.description:
The telephone number of the author of the data block to whom correspondence should be addressed.

The recommended style starts with the international dialing prefix, followed by the area code in parentheses, followed by the local number and any extension number prefixed by 'x', with no spaces.

_audit_link.block_code.name: _audit_link.block_code
_audit_link.block_code.description: The value of _audit_block.code associated with a data block in the current file related to the current data block. The special value '.' may be used to refer to the current data block for completeness.

_audit_link.block_description.name: _audit_link.block_description
_audit_link.block_description.description: A textual description of the relationship of the referenced data block to the current one.

_cell.angle_alpha.name: _cell.angle_alpha
_cell.angle_alpha.description: Unit-cell angle alpha of the reported structure in degrees.

_cell.angle_alpha_esd.name: _cell.angle_alpha_esd
_cell.angle_alpha_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell.angle_alpha.

_cell.angle_beta.name: _cell.angle_beta
_cell.angle_beta.description: Unit-cell angle beta of the reported structure in degrees.

_cell.angle_beta_esd.name: _cell.angle_beta_esd
_cell.angle_beta_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell.angle_beta.

_cell.angle_gamma.name: _cell.angle_gamma
_cell.angle_gamma.description: Unit-cell angle gamma of the reported structure in degrees.

_cell.angle_gamma_esd.name: _cell.angle_gamma_esd
_cell.angle_gamma_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell.angle_gamma.

_cell.entry_id.name: _cell.entry_id
_cell.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_cell.details.name: _cell.details
_cell.details.description: A description of special aspects of the cell choice, noting possible alternative settings.
examples: standard setting from 45 deg rotation around c

_cell.formula_units_Z.name: _cell.formula_units_Z
_cell.formula_units_Z.description: The number of the formula units in the unit cell as specified by _chemical_formula.structural, _chemical_formula.moiety or _chemical_formula.sum.

_cell.length_a.name: _cell.length_a
_cell.length_a.description: Unit-cell length a corresponding to the structure reported in angstroms.

_cell.length_a_esd.name: _cell.length_a_esd
_cell.length_a_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell.length_a.

_cell.length_b.name: _cell.length_b
_cell.length_b.description: Unit-cell length b corresponding to the structure reported in angstroms.

_cell.length_b_esd.name: _cell.length_b_esd
_cell.length_b_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell.length_b.

_cell.length_c.name: _cell.length_c
_cell.length_c.description: Unit-cell length c corresponding to the structure reported in angstroms.

_cell.length_c_esd.name: _cell.length_c_esd
_cell.length_c_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell.length_c.

_cell.volume.name: _cell.volume
_cell.volume.description:
Cell volume V in angstroms cubed.

V = a b c (1 - cos^2^~alpha~ - cos^2^~beta~ - cos^2^~gamma~ + 2 cos~alpha~ cos~beta~ cos~gamma~)^1/2^

a     = _cell.length_a b     = _cell.length_b c     = _cell.length_c alpha = _cell.angle_alpha beta  = _cell.angle_beta gamma = _cell.angle_gamma

_cell.volume_esd.name: _cell.volume_esd
_cell.volume_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell.volume.

_cell.Z_PDB.name: _cell.Z_PDB
_cell.Z_PDB.description:
The number of the polymeric chains in a unit cell. In the case of heteropolymers, Z is the number of occurrences of the most populous chain.

This data item is provided for compatibility with the original Protein Data Bank format, and only for that purpose.

_cell.reciprocal_angle_alpha.name: _cell.reciprocal_angle_alpha
_cell.reciprocal_angle_alpha.description:
The angle (recip-alpha) defining the reciprocal cell in degrees. (recip-alpha), (recip-alpha) and (recip-alpha) related to the angles in the real cell by:

cos(recip-alpha) = [cos(beta)*cos(gamma) - cos(alpha)]/[sin(beta)*sin(gamma)]

cos(recip-beta) = [cos(gamma)*cos(alpha) - cos(beta)]/[sin(gamma)*sin(alpha)]

cos(recip-gamma) = [cos(alpha)*cos(beta) - cos(gamma)]/[sin(alpha)*sin(beta)]

Ref: Buerger, M. J. (1942). X-ray Crystallography, p. 360. New York: John Wiley & Sons Inc.

_cell.reciprocal_angle_beta.name: _cell.reciprocal_angle_beta
_cell.reciprocal_angle_beta.description:
The angle (recip-beta) defining the reciprocal cell in degrees. (recip-alpha), (recip-alpha) and (recip-alpha) related to the angles in the real cell by:

cos(recip-alpha) = [cos(beta)*cos(gamma) - cos(alpha)]/[sin(beta)*sin(gamma)]

cos(recip-beta) = [cos(gamma)*cos(alpha) - cos(beta)]/[sin(gamma)*sin(alpha)]

cos(recip-gamma) = [cos(alpha)*cos(beta) - cos(gamma)]/[sin(alpha)*sin(beta)]

Ref: Buerger, M. J. (1942). X-ray Crystallography, p. 360. New York: John Wiley & Sons Inc.

_cell.reciprocal_angle_gamma.name: _cell.reciprocal_angle_gamma
_cell.reciprocal_angle_gamma.description:
The angle (recip-gamma) defining the reciprocal cell in degrees. (recip-alpha), (recip-alpha) and (recip-alpha) related to the angles in the real cell by:

cos(recip-alpha) = [cos(beta)*cos(gamma) - cos(alpha)]/[sin(beta)*sin(gamma)]

cos(recip-beta) = [cos(gamma)*cos(alpha) - cos(beta)]/[sin(gamma)*sin(alpha)]

cos(recip-gamma) = [cos(alpha)*cos(beta) - cos(gamma)]/[sin(alpha)*sin(beta)]

Ref: Buerger, M. J. (1942). X-ray Crystallography, p. 360. New York: John Wiley & Sons Inc.

_cell.reciprocal_length_a.name: _cell.reciprocal_length_a
_cell.reciprocal_length_a.description:
The reciprocal cell length (recip-a) in inverse angstroms. (recip-a), (recip-b) and (recip-c) are related to the real cell by the following equation:

recip-a = b*c*sin(alpha)/V

recip-b = c*a*sin(beta)/V

recip-c = a*b*sin(gamma)/V

where V is the cell volume.

Ref: Buerger, M. J. (1942). X-ray Crystallography, p. 360. New York: John Wiley & Sons Inc.

_cell.reciprocal_length_b.name: _cell.reciprocal_length_b
_cell.reciprocal_length_b.description:
The reciprocal cell length (recip-b) in inverse angstroms. (recip-a), (recip-b) and (recip-c) are related to the real cell by the following equation:

recip-a = b*c*sin(alpha)/V

recip-b = c*a*sin(beta)/V

recip-c = a*b*sin(gamma)/V

where V is the cell volume.

Ref: Buerger, M. J. (1942). X-ray Crystallography, p. 360. New York: John Wiley & Sons Inc.

_cell.reciprocal_length_c.name: _cell.reciprocal_length_c
_cell.reciprocal_length_c.description:
The reciprocal cell length (recip-c) in inverse angstroms. (recip-a), (recip-b) and (recip-c) are related to the real cell by the following equation:

recip-a = b*c*sin(alpha)/V

recip-b = c*a*sin(beta)/V

recip-c = a*b*sin(gamma)/V

where V is the cell volume.

Ref: Buerger, M. J. (1942). X-ray Crystallography, p. 360. New York: John Wiley & Sons Inc.

_cell.pdbx_unique_axis.name: _cell.pdbx_unique_axis
_cell.pdbx_unique_axis.description: To further identify unique axis if necessary.  E.g., P 21 with an unique C axis will have 'C' in this field.

_cell.pdbx_esd_method.name: _cell.pdbx_esd_method
_cell.pdbx_esd_method.description: How the estimated standard deviation was determined.
enums: Standard deviation from a population of many crystals, for example from serial crystallography
Errors are derived from a refined fit of the cell parameters to the data and represent the precision of that fit
Error using a scan varying restraint across the crystal as a whole
ESD from measuring the unit cell using a moving window across many frames

_cell_measurement.entry_id.name: _cell_measurement.entry_id
_cell_measurement.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_cell_measurement.pressure.name: _cell_measurement.pressure
_cell_measurement.pressure.description: The pressure in kilopascals at which the unit-cell parameters were measured (not the pressure at which the sample was synthesized).

_cell_measurement.pressure_esd.name: _cell_measurement.pressure_esd
_cell_measurement.pressure_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell_measurement.pressure.

_cell_measurement.radiation.name: _cell_measurement.radiation
_cell_measurement.radiation.description: Description of the radiation used to measure the unit-cell data. See also _cell_measurement.wavelength.

_cell_measurement.reflns_used.name: _cell_measurement.reflns_used
_cell_measurement.reflns_used.description: The total number of reflections used to determine the unit cell. These reflections may be specified as CELL_MEASUREMENT_REFLN data items.

_cell_measurement.temp.name: _cell_measurement.temp
_cell_measurement.temp.description: The temperature in kelvins at which the unit-cell parameters were measured (not the temperature of synthesis).

_cell_measurement.temp_esd.name: _cell_measurement.temp_esd
_cell_measurement.temp_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _cell_measurement.temp.

_cell_measurement.theta_max.name: _cell_measurement.theta_max
_cell_measurement.theta_max.description: The maximum theta angle of reflections used to measure the unit cell in degrees.

_cell_measurement.theta_min.name: _cell_measurement.theta_min
_cell_measurement.theta_min.description: The minimum theta angle of reflections used to measure the unit cell in degrees.

_cell_measurement.wavelength.name: _cell_measurement.wavelength
_cell_measurement.wavelength.description: The wavelength in angstroms of the radiation used to measure the unit cell. If this is not specified, the wavelength is assumed to be that specified in the category DIFFRN_RADIATION_WAVELENGTH.

_cell_measurement_refln.index_h.name: _cell_measurement_refln.index_h
_cell_measurement_refln.index_h.description: Miller index h of a reflection used for measurement of the unit cell.

_cell_measurement_refln.index_k.name: _cell_measurement_refln.index_k
_cell_measurement_refln.index_k.description: Miller index k of a reflection used for measurement of the unit cell.

_cell_measurement_refln.index_l.name: _cell_measurement_refln.index_l
_cell_measurement_refln.index_l.description: Miller index l of a reflection used for measurement of the unit cell.

_cell_measurement_refln.theta.name: _cell_measurement_refln.theta
_cell_measurement_refln.theta.description: Theta angle for a reflection used for measurement of the unit cell in degrees.

_chem_comp.formula.name: _chem_comp.formula
_chem_comp.formula.description:
The formula for the chemical component. Formulae are written according to the following rules:

(1) Only recognized element symbols may be used.

(2) Each element symbol is followed by a 'count' number. A count of '1' may be omitted.

(3) A space or parenthesis must separate each cluster of (element symbol + count), but in general parentheses are not used.

(4) The order of elements depends on whether carbon is present or not. If carbon is present, the order should be: C, then H, then the other elements in alphabetical order of their symbol. If carbon is not present, the elements are listed purely in alphabetic order of their symbol. This is the 'Hill' system used by Chemical Abstracts.

_chem_comp.formula_weight.name: _chem_comp.formula_weight
_chem_comp.formula_weight.description: Formula mass in daltons of the chemical component.

_chem_comp.id.name: _chem_comp.id
_chem_comp.id.description:
The value of _chem_comp.id must uniquely identify each item in the CHEM_COMP list.

For protein polymer entities, this is the three-letter code for the amino acid.

For nucleic acid polymer entities, this is the one-letter code for the base.

_chem_comp.model_details.name: _chem_comp.model_details
_chem_comp.model_details.description: A description of special aspects of the generation of the coordinates for the model of the component.

_chem_comp.model_erf.name: _chem_comp.model_erf
_chem_comp.model_erf.description: A pointer to an external reference file from which the atomic description of the component is taken.

_chem_comp.model_source.name: _chem_comp.model_source
_chem_comp.model_source.description: The source of the coordinates for the model of the component.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_class.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_class
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_class.description: A description of the class of a nonstandard monomer if the nonstandard monomer represents a modification of a standard monomer.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_details.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_details
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_details.description: A description of special details of a nonstandard monomer.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_flag.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_flag
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_flag.description: 'yes' indicates that this is a 'standard' monomer, 'no' indicates that it is 'nonstandard'. Nonstandard monomers should be described in more detail using the _chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent, _chem_comp.mon_nstd_class and _chem_comp.mon_nstd_details data items.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent.description: The name of the parent monomer of the nonstandard monomer, if the nonstandard monomer represents a modification of a standard monomer.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent_comp_id.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent_comp_id
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent_comp_id.description:
The identifier for the parent component of the nonstandard component. May be be a comma separated list if this component is derived from multiple components.

Items in this indirectly point to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp.name.name: _chem_comp.name
_chem_comp.name.description: The full name of the component.

_chem_comp.number_atoms_all.name: _chem_comp.number_atoms_all
_chem_comp.number_atoms_all.description: The total number of atoms in the component.

_chem_comp.number_atoms_nh.name: _chem_comp.number_atoms_nh
_chem_comp.number_atoms_nh.description: The number of non-hydrogen atoms in the component.

_chem_comp.one_letter_code.name: _chem_comp.one_letter_code
_chem_comp.one_letter_code.description:
For standard polymer components, the one-letter code for the component.   For non-standard polymer components, the one-letter code for parent component if this exists; otherwise, the one-letter code should be given as 'X'.

Components that derived from multiple parents components are described by a sequence of one-letter-codes.

_chem_comp.three_letter_code.name: _chem_comp.three_letter_code
_chem_comp.three_letter_code.description:
For standard polymer components, the common three-letter code for the component.   Non-standard polymer components and non-polymer components are also assigned three-letter-codes.

For ambiguous polymer components three-letter code should be given as 'UNK'.  Ambiguous ions are assigned the code 'UNX'. Ambiguous non-polymer components are assigned the code 'UNL'.

_chem_comp.type.name: _chem_comp.type
_chem_comp.type.description: For standard polymer components, the type of the monomer. Note that monomers that will form polymers are of three types: linking monomers, monomers with some type of N-terminal (or 5') cap and monomers with some type of C-terminal (or 3') cap.

_chem_comp.pdbx_modification_details.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_modification_details
_chem_comp.pdbx_modification_details.description: For nonstandard components a text description of modification of the parent component.

_chem_comp.pdbx_component_no.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_component_no
_chem_comp.pdbx_component_no.description: A serial number used by PDB in the FORMUL record.

_chem_comp.pdbx_type.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_type
_chem_comp.pdbx_type.description: A preliminary classification used by PDB.

_chem_comp.pdbx_ambiguous_flag.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_ambiguous_flag
_chem_comp.pdbx_ambiguous_flag.description: A preliminary classification used by PDB to indicate that the chemistry of this component while described as clearly as possible is still ambiguous.  Software tools may not be able to process this component definition.

_chem_comp.pdbx_replaced_by.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_replaced_by
_chem_comp.pdbx_replaced_by.description: Identifies the _chem_comp.id of the component that has replaced this component.

_chem_comp.pdbx_replaces.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_replaces
_chem_comp.pdbx_replaces.description: Identifies the _chem_comp.id's of the components which have been replaced by this component. Multiple id codes should be separated by commas.

_chem_comp.pdbx_formal_charge.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_formal_charge
_chem_comp.pdbx_formal_charge.description: The net integer charge assigned to this component. This is the formal charge assignment normally found in chemical diagrams.

_chem_comp.pdbx_subcomponent_list.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_subcomponent_list
_chem_comp.pdbx_subcomponent_list.description: The list of subcomponents contained in this component.

_chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_detail.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_details
_chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_detail.description: This data item provides additional details about the model coordinates in the component definition.

_chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_db_cod.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_db_code
_chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_db_cod.description: This data item identifies the PDB database code from which the heavy atom model coordinates were obtained.

_chem_comp.pdbx_ideal_coordinates_detail.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_ideal_coordinates_details
_chem_comp.pdbx_ideal_coordinates_detail.description: This data item identifies the source of the ideal coordinates in the component definition.

_chem_comp.pdbx_ideal_coordinates_missin.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_ideal_coordinates_missing_flag
_chem_comp.pdbx_ideal_coordinates_missin.description: This data item identifies if ideal coordinates are missing in this definition.
enums: ideal coordinates are not provided or incomplete

_chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_missin.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_missing_flag
_chem_comp.pdbx_model_coordinates_missin.description: This data item identifies if model coordinates are missing in this definition.
enums: model coordinates are not provided or incomplete

_chem_comp.pdbx_initial_date.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_initial_date
_chem_comp.pdbx_initial_date.description: Date component was added to database.

_chem_comp.pdbx_release_status.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_release_status
_chem_comp.pdbx_release_status.description: This data item holds the current release status for the component.
enums: Component defintion has been obsoleted and replaced by another entry
Component definition is provided for reference only and will not be used in released entries.

_chem_comp.pdbx_processing_site.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_processing_site
_chem_comp.pdbx_processing_site.description: This data item identifies the deposition site that processed this chemical component defintion.

_chem_comp.pdbx_number_subcomponents.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_number_subcomponents
_chem_comp.pdbx_number_subcomponents.description: The number of subcomponents represented in this component.

_chem_comp.pdbx_class_1.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_class_1
_chem_comp.pdbx_class_1.description: Internal classifier used to organize ligand dictionary (broad chemical class).

_chem_comp.pdbx_class_2.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_class_2
_chem_comp.pdbx_class_2.description: Internal classifier used to organize ligand dictionary (notable chemical features).

_chem_comp.pdbx_comp_type.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_comp_type
_chem_comp.pdbx_comp_type.description: A type classification of this chemical component.

_chem_comp.pdbx_reserved_name.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_reserved_name
_chem_comp.pdbx_reserved_name.description: Previous chemical name used for this component if a name correction has been made.

_chem_comp.pdbx_pcm.name: _chem_comp.pdbx_pcm
_chem_comp.pdbx_pcm.description: A flag to indicate if the CCD can be used to represent a protein modification.
enums: No - Should never be used to represent a protein modification
Yes - Can be used to represent a protein modification

_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1.name: _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1
_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the three atoms that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_2.name: _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_2
_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the three atoms that define the angle. The second atom is taken to be the apex of the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.name: _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3
_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.description:
The ID of the third of the three atoms that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_angle.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_angle.comp_id
_chem_comp_angle.comp_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_angle.value_angle.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_angle
_chem_comp_angle.value_angle.description: The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed in degrees.

_chem_comp_angle.value_angle_esd.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_angle_esd
_chem_comp_angle.value_angle_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_angle.value_angle.

_chem_comp_angle.value_dist.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_dist
_chem_comp_angle.value_dist.description: The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed as the distance between the atoms specified by _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1 and _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.

_chem_comp_angle.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_dist_esd
_chem_comp_angle.value_dist_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_angle.value_dist.

_chem_comp_atom.alt_atom_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.alt_atom_id
_chem_comp_atom.alt_atom_id.description: An alternative identifier for the atom. This data item would be used in cases where alternative nomenclatures exist for labelling atoms in a group.

_chem_comp_atom.atom_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.atom_id
_chem_comp_atom.atom_id.description:
The value of _chem_comp_atom.atom_id must uniquely identify each atom in each monomer in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM list.

The atom identifiers need not be unique over all atoms in the data block; they need only be unique for each atom in a component.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_chem_comp_atom.charge.name: _chem_comp_atom.charge
_chem_comp_atom.charge.description: The net integer charge assigned to this atom. This is the formal charge assignment normally found in chemical diagrams.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x.description:
The x component of the coordinates for this atom in this component specified as orthogonal angstroms. The choice of reference axis frame for the coordinates is arbitrary.

The set of coordinates input for the entity here is intended to correspond to the atomic model used to generate restraints for structure refinement, not to atom sites in the ATOM_SITE list.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x_esd.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x_esd
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y.description:
The y component of the coordinates for this atom in this component specified as orthogonal angstroms. The choice of reference axis frame for the coordinates is arbitrary.

The set of coordinates input for the entity here is intended to correspond to the atomic model used to generate restraints for structure refinement, not to atom sites in the ATOM_SITE list.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y_esd.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y_esd
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z.description:
The z component of the coordinates for this atom in this component specified as orthogonal angstroms. The choice of reference axis frame for the coordinates is arbitrary.

The set of coordinates input for the entity here is intended to correspond to the atomic model used to generate restraints for structure refinement, not to atom sites in the ATOM_SITE list.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z_esd.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z_esd
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z.

_chem_comp_atom.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.comp_id
_chem_comp_atom.comp_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_atom.partial_charge.name: _chem_comp_atom.partial_charge
_chem_comp_atom.partial_charge.description: The partial charge assigned to this atom.

_chem_comp_atom.substruct_code.name: _chem_comp_atom.substruct_code
_chem_comp_atom.substruct_code.description: This data item assigns the atom to a substructure of the component, if appropriate.
enums: main chain of an amino acid
side chain of an amino acid

_chem_comp_atom.type_symbol.name: _chem_comp_atom.type_symbol
_chem_comp_atom.type_symbol.description: The code used to identify the atom species representing this atom type. Normally this code is the element symbol.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_align.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_align
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_align.description: Atom name alignment offset in PDB atom field.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_ordinal.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_ordinal
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_ordinal.description: Ordinal index for the component atom list.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_atom_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_atom_id
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_atom_id.description: The atom identifier in the subcomponent where a larger component has been divided subcomponents.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_comp_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_comp_id
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_comp_id.description: The component identifier for the subcomponent where a larger component has been divided subcomponents.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_alt_atom_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_alt_atom_id
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_alt_atom_id.description: An alternative identifier for the atom. This data item would be used in cases where alternative nomenclatures exist for labelling atoms in a group.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_alt_comp_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_alt_comp_id
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_alt_comp_id.description: An alternative identifier for the atom. This data item would be used in cases where alternative nomenclatures exist for labelling atoms in a group.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_x_ideal.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_x_ideal
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_x_ideal.description: An alternative x component of the coordinates for this atom in this component specified as orthogonal angstroms.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_y_ideal.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_y_ideal
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_y_ideal.description: An alternative y component of the coordinates for this atom in this component specified as orthogonal angstroms.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_z_ideal.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_z_ideal
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_model_Cartn_z_ideal.description: An alternative z component of the coordinates for this atom in this component specified as orthogonal angstroms.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_stereo_config.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_stereo_config
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_stereo_config.description: The chiral configuration of the atom that is a chiral center.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_aromatic_flag.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_aromatic_flag
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_aromatic_flag.description: A flag indicating an aromatic atom.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_leaving_atom_flag.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_leaving_atom_flag
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_leaving_atom_flag.description: A flag indicating a leaving atom.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_residue_numbering.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_residue_numbering
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_residue_numbering.description: Preferred residue numbering in the BIRD definition.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_polymer_type.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_polymer_type
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_polymer_type.description: Is the atom in a polymer or non-polymer subcomponent in the BIRD definition.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_entity_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_entity_id
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_component_entity_id.description: A reference to entity identifier in data  category pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_entity_list.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_stnd_atom_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_stnd_atom_id
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_stnd_atom_id.description: A standard identifier for the atom. This data item is used when IUPAC/IUBMB nomenclature exists for labeling atoms.

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_backbone_atom_flag.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_backbone_atom_flag
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_backbone_atom_flag.description: A flag indicating the backbone atoms in polypeptide units.
enums: No  - not part of the polypeptide backbone
Yes - a part of the polypeptide backbone

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_n_terminal_atom_fla.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_n_terminal_atom_flag
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_n_terminal_atom_fla.description: A flag indicating the N-terminal group atoms in polypeptide units.
enums: No  - not part of the N-terminal group
Yes - a part of the N-terminal group

_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_c_terminal_atom_fla.name: _chem_comp_atom.pdbx_c_terminal_atom_flag
_chem_comp_atom.pdbx_c_terminal_atom_fla.description: A flag indicating the C-terminal group atoms in polypeptide units.
enums: No  - not part of the C-terminal group
Yes - a part of the C-terminal group

_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_1.name: _chem_comp_bond.atom_id_1
_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the two atoms that define the bond.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_2.name: _chem_comp_bond.atom_id_2
_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the two atoms that define the bond.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_bond.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_bond.comp_id
_chem_comp_bond.comp_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_bond.value_order.name: _chem_comp_bond.value_order
_chem_comp_bond.value_order.description: The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical bond associated with the specified atoms, expressed as a bond order.

_chem_comp_bond.value_dist.name: _chem_comp_bond.value_dist
_chem_comp_bond.value_dist.description: The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical bond associated with the specified atoms, expressed as a distance.

_chem_comp_bond.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_comp_bond.value_dist_esd
_chem_comp_bond.value_dist_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_bond.value_dist.

_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_ordinal.name: _chem_comp_bond.pdbx_ordinal
_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_ordinal.description: Ordinal index for the component bond list.

_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_stereo_config.name: _chem_comp_bond.pdbx_stereo_config
_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_stereo_config.description: Stereochemical configuration across a double bond.

_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_aromatic_flag.name: _chem_comp_bond.pdbx_aromatic_flag
_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_aromatic_flag.description: A flag indicating an aromatic bond.

_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_pi_flag.name: _chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_pi_flag
_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_pi_flag.description: This data item identifies if this is a bond between a metal and a pi system (alkene, arene, etc).

_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_coordination_.name: _chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_coordination_flag
_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_coordination_.description: This data item identifies if this is a bond between a metal and its coordinating group, otherwise referred to as a dative or coordinate covalent bond. This flag is used for all bonds between metals and non-metals, including metal-pi bonds and all other metal coordination bonds.

_chem_comp_chir.atom_id.name: _chem_comp_chir.atom_id
_chem_comp_chir.atom_id.description:
The ID of the atom that is a chiral centre.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_chir.atom_config.name: _chem_comp_chir.atom_config
_chem_comp_chir.atom_config.description: The chiral configuration of the atom that is a chiral centre.

_chem_comp_chir.id.name: _chem_comp_chir.id
_chem_comp_chir.id.description: The value of _chem_comp_chir.id must uniquely identify a record in the CHEM_COMP_CHIR list.

_chem_comp_chir.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_chir.comp_id
_chem_comp_chir.comp_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_all.name: _chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_all
_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_all.description: The total number of atoms bonded to the atom specified by _chem_comp_chir.atom_id.

_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_nh.name: _chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_nh
_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_nh.description: The number of non-hydrogen atoms bonded to the atom specified by _chem_comp_chir.atom_id.

_chem_comp_chir.volume_flag.name: _chem_comp_chir.volume_flag
_chem_comp_chir.volume_flag.description: A flag to indicate whether a chiral volume should match the standard value in both magnitude and sign, or in magnitude only.

_chem_comp_chir.volume_three.name: _chem_comp_chir.volume_three
_chem_comp_chir.volume_three.description:
The chiral volume, V~c~, for chiral centres that involve a chiral atom bonded to three non-hydrogen atoms and one hydrogen atom.

V~c~ = V1 * (V2 X V3)

V1 = the vector distance from the atom specified by _chem_comp_chir.atom_id to the first atom in the CHEM_COMP_CHIR_ATOM list V2 = the vector distance from the atom specified by _chem_comp_chir.atom_id to the second atom in the CHEM_COMP_CHIR_ATOM list V3 = the vector distance from the atom specified by _chem_comp_chir.atom_id to the third atom in the CHEM_COMP_CHIR_ATOM list *  = the vector dot product X  = the vector cross product

_chem_comp_chir.volume_three_esd.name: _chem_comp_chir.volume_three_esd
_chem_comp_chir.volume_three_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_chir.volume_three.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.atom_id.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.atom_id
_chem_comp_chir_atom.atom_id.description:
The ID of an atom bonded to the chiral atom.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.chir_id.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.chir_id
_chem_comp_chir_atom.chir_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_chir.id in the CHEM_COMP_CHIR category.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.comp_id
_chem_comp_chir_atom.comp_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.dev.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.dev
_chem_comp_chir_atom.dev.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of the position of this atom from the plane defined by all of the atoms in the plane.

_chem_comp_link.link_id.name: _chem_comp_link.link_id
_chem_comp_link.link_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the CHEM_LINK category.

_chem_comp_link.details.name: _chem_comp_link.details
_chem_comp_link.details.description: A description of special aspects of a link between chemical components in the structure.

_chem_comp_link.type_comp_1.name: _chem_comp_link.type_comp_1
_chem_comp_link.type_comp_1.description:
The type of the first of the two components joined by the link.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.type in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_link.type_comp_2.name: _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2
_chem_comp_link.type_comp_2.description:
The type of the second of the two components joined by the link.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.type in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_plane.id.name: _chem_comp_plane.id
_chem_comp_plane.id.description: The value of _chem_comp_plane.id must uniquely identify a record in the CHEM_COMP_PLANE list.

_chem_comp_plane.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_plane.comp_id
_chem_comp_plane.comp_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_all.name: _chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_all
_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_all.description: The total number of atoms in the plane.

_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_nh.name: _chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_nh
_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_nh.description: The number of non-hydrogen atoms in the plane.

_chem_comp_plane_atom.atom_id.name: _chem_comp_plane_atom.atom_id
_chem_comp_plane_atom.atom_id.description:
The ID of an atom involved in the plane.

This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_plane_atom.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_plane_atom.comp_id

Note that the torsion angle cannot be fully specified by a distance (for instance, a torsion angle of -60  degree will yield the same distance as a 60 degree angle). However, the distance specification can be useful for refinement in situations in which the angle is already close to the desired value.

_chem_link_angle.atom_1_comp_id.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_1_comp_id
_chem_link_angle.atom_1_comp_id.description: This data item indicates whether atom 1 is found in the first or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_angle.atom_2_comp_id.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_2_comp_id
_chem_link_angle.atom_2_comp_id.description: This data item indicates whether atom 2 is found in the first or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_angle.atom_3_comp_id.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_3_comp_id
_chem_link_angle.atom_3_comp_id.description: This data item indicates whether atom 3 is found in the first or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_angle.atom_id_1.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_id_1
_chem_link_angle.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the three atoms that define the angle.

An atom with this ID must exist in the component of the type specified by _chem_comp_link.type_comp_1 (or _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2, where the appropriate data item is indicated by the value of _chem_comp_angle.atom_1_comp_id).

_chem_link_angle.atom_id_2.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_id_2
_chem_link_angle.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the three atoms that define the angle. The second atom is taken to be the apex of the angle.

An atom with this ID must exist in the component of the type specified by _chem_comp_link.type_comp_1 (or _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2, where the appropriate data item is indicated by the value of _chem_comp_angle.atom_2_comp_id).

_chem_link_angle.atom_id_3.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_id_3
_chem_link_angle.atom_id_3.description:
The ID of the third of the three atoms that define the angle.

An atom with this ID must exist in the component of the type specified by _chem_comp_link.type_comp_1 (or _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2, where the appropriate data item is indicated by the value of _chem_comp_angle.atom_3_comp_id).

_chem_link_angle.link_id.name: _chem_link_angle.link_id
_chem_link_angle.link_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the CHEM_LINK category.

_chem_link_angle.value_angle.name: _chem_link_angle.value_angle
_chem_link_angle.value_angle.description: The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed in degrees.

_chem_link_angle.value_angle_esd.name: _chem_link_angle.value_angle_esd
_chem_link_angle.value_angle_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_link_angle.value_angle.

_chem_link_angle.value_dist.name: _chem_link_angle.value_dist
_chem_link_angle.value_dist.description: The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed as the distance between the atoms specified by _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1 and _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.

_chem_link_angle.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_link_angle.value_dist_esd
_chem_link_angle.value_dist_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_comp_angle.value_dist.

_chem_link_bond.atom_1_comp_id.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_1_comp_id
_chem_link_bond.atom_1_comp_id.description: This data item indicates whether atom 1 is found in the first or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_bond.atom_2_comp_id.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_2_comp_id
_chem_link_bond.atom_2_comp_id.description: This data item indicates whether atom 2 is found in the first or the second of the two chemical components connected by the link.

_chem_link_bond.atom_id_1.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_id_1
_chem_link_bond.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the two atoms that define the bond.

As this data item does not point to a specific atom in a specific chemical component, it is not a child in the linkage sense.

_chem_link_bond.atom_id_2.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_id_2
_chem_link_bond.atom_id_2.description:

_chem_link_bond.link_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the CHEM_LINK category.

_chem_link_bond.value_dist.name: _chem_link_bond.value_dist
_chem_link_bond.value_dist.description: The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical bond associated with the specified atoms, expressed as a distance.

_chem_link_bond.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_link_bond.value_dist_esd
_chem_link_bond.value_dist_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_link_bond.value_dist.

_chem_link_bond.value_order.name: _chem_link_bond.value_order
_chem_link_bond.value_order.description: The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical bond associated with the specified atoms, expressed as a bond order.
