_chem_link_chir.atom_comp_id.description: This data item indicates whether the chiral atom is found in the first or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_chir.atom_id.name: _chem_link_chir.atom_id
_chem_link_chir.atom_id.description:
The ID of the atom that is a chiral centre.

As this data item does not point to a specific atom in a specific chemical component, it is not a child in the linkage sense.

_chem_link_chir.atom_config.name: _chem_link_chir.atom_config
_chem_link_chir.atom_config.description: The chiral configuration of the atom that is a chiral centre.

_chem_link_chir.id.name: _chem_link_chir.id
_chem_link_chir.id.description: The value of _chem_link_chir.id must uniquely identify a record in the CHEM_LINK_CHIR list.

_chem_link_chir.link_id.name: _chem_link_chir.link_id
_chem_link_chir.link_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the CHEM_LINK category.

_chem_link_chir.number_atoms_all.name: _chem_link_chir.number_atoms_all
_chem_link_chir.number_atoms_all.description: The total number of atoms bonded to the atom specified by _chem_link_chir.atom_id.

_chem_link_chir.number_atoms_nh.name: _chem_link_chir.number_atoms_nh
_chem_link_chir.number_atoms_nh.description: The number of non-hydrogen atoms bonded to the atom specified by _chem_link_chir.atom_id.

_chem_link_chir.volume_flag.name: _chem_link_chir.volume_flag
_chem_link_chir.volume_flag.description: A flag to indicate whether a chiral volume should match the standard value in both magnitude and sign, or in magnitude only.

_chem_link_chir.volume_three.name: _chem_link_chir.volume_three
_chem_link_chir.volume_three.description:
The chiral volume, V(c), for chiral centres that involve a chiral atom bonded to three non-hydrogen atoms and one hydrogen atom.

V~c~ = V1 * (V2 X V3)

V1 = the vector distance from the atom specified by _chem_link_chir.atom_id to the first atom in the CHEM_LINK_CHIR_ATOM list V2 = the vector distance from the atom specified by _chem_link_chir.atom_id to the second atom in the CHEM_LINK_CHIR_ATOM list V3 = the vector distance from the atom specified by _chem_link_chir.atom_id to the third atom in the CHEM_LINK_CHIR_ATOM list *  = the vector dot product X  = the vector cross product

_chem_link_chir.volume_three_esd.name: _chem_link_chir.volume_three_esd
_chem_link_chir.volume_three_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _chem_link_chir.volume_three.

_chem_link_chir_atom.atom_comp_id.name: _chem_link_chir_atom.atom_comp_id
_chem_link_chir_atom.atom_comp_id.description: This data item indicates whether the atom bonded to a chiral atom is found in the first or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_chir_atom.atom_id.name: _chem_link_chir_atom.atom_id
_chem_link_chir_atom.atom_id.description:
The ID of an atom bonded to the chiral atom.

As this data item does not point to a specific atom in a specific chemical component, it is not a child in the linkage sense.

_chem_link_chir_atom.chir_id.name: _chem_link_chir_atom.chir_id

_chem_link_tor_value.tor_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _chem_link_tor.id in the CHEM_LINK_TOR category.

_chemical.entry_id.name: _chemical.entry_id

_chemical.compound_source.name: _chemical.compound_source
_chemical.compound_source.description: Description of the source of the compound under study, or of the parent  molecule if a simple derivative is studied. This includes the place of discovery for minerals or the actual source of a natural product.
examples: Extracted from the bark of Cinchona Naturalis

_chemical.melting_point.name: _chemical.melting_point
_chemical.melting_point.description: The temperature in kelvins at which the crystalline solid changes to a liquid.

_chemical.name_common.name: _chemical.name_common
_chemical.name_common.description: Trivial name by which the compound is commonly known.

_chemical.name_mineral.name: _chemical.name_mineral
_chemical.name_mineral.description: Mineral name accepted by the International Mineralogical Association. Use only for natural minerals. See also _chemical.compound_source.

_chemical.name_structure_type.name: _chemical.name_structure_type
_chemical.name_structure_type.description: Commonly used structure-type name. Usually only applied to minerals or inorganic compounds.

_chemical.name_systematic.name: _chemical.name_systematic
_chemical.name_systematic.description: IUPAC or Chemical Abstracts full name of the compound.

_chemical.absolute_configuration.name: _chemical.absolute_configuration
_chemical.absolute_configuration.description:
Necessary conditions for the assignment of _chemical.absolute_configuration are given by H. D. Flack and G. Bernardinelli (1999, 2000).

Ref: Flack, H. D. & Bernardinelli, G. (1999). Acta Cryst. A55, 908-915. (http://www.iucr.org/paper?sh0129) Flack, H. D. & Bernardinelli, G. (2000). J. Appl. Cryst. 33, 1143-1148. (http://www.iucr.org/paper?ks0021)
enums: absolute configuration established by anomalous-dispersion effects in diffraction measurements on the crystal
absolute configuration established by the structure determination of a compound containing a chiral reference molecule of known absolute configuration

absolute configuration established by the structure determination of a compound containing a chiral reference molecule of known absolute configuration and confirmed by anomalous-dispersion effects in diffraction measurements on the crystal

absolute configuration has not been established by anomalous-dispersion effects in diffraction measurements on the crystal. The enantiomer has been assigned by reference to an unchanging chiral centre in the synthetic procedure

absolute configuration is unknown, there being no firm chemical evidence for its assignment to hand and it having not been established by anomalous-dispersion effects in diffraction measurements on the crystal. An arbitrary choice of enantiomer has been made

_chemical.melting_point_gt.name: _chemical.melting_point_gt
_chemical.melting_point_gt.description:
A temperature in kelvins above which the melting point (the temperature at which the crystalline solid changes to a liquid) lies. _chemical.melting_point_gt and _chemical.melting_point_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_chemical.melting_point should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_chemical.melting_point_lt.name: _chemical.melting_point_lt
_chemical.melting_point_lt.description:
A temperature in kelvins below which the melting point (the temperature at which the crystalline solid changes to a liquid) lies. _chemical.melting_point_gt and _chemical.melting_point_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_chemical.melting_point should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_chemical.optical_rotation.name: _chemical.optical_rotation
_chemical.optical_rotation.description: The optical rotation in solution of the compound is specified in the following format: '[\a]^TEMP^~WAVE~ = SORT (c = CONC, SOLV)' where: TEMP is the temperature of the measurement in degrees Celsius, WAVE is an indication of the wavelength of the light used for the measurement, CONC is the concentration of the solution given as the mass of the substance in g in 100 ml of solution, SORT is the signed value (preceded by a + or a - sign) of 100.\a/(l.c), where \a is the signed optical rotation in degrees measured in a cell of length l in dm and c is the value of CONC as defined above, and SOLV is the chemical formula of the solvent.

_chemical.properties_biological.name: _chemical.properties_biological
_chemical.properties_biological.description: A free-text description of the biological properties of the material.
examples:
diverse biological activities including use as a
 laxative and strong antibacterial activity against
 S. aureus and weak activity against
 cyclooxygenase-1 (COX-1)

antibiotic activity against Bacillus subtilis
 (ATCC 6051) but no significant activity against
 Candida albicans (ATCC 14053), Aspergillus flavus
 (NRRL 6541) and Fusarium verticillioides (NRRL
 25457)

no influenza A virus sialidase inhibitory and
 plaque reduction activities

_chemical.properties_physical.name: _chemical.properties_physical
_chemical.properties_physical.description: A free-text description of the physical properties of the material.

_chemical.temperature_decomposition.name: _chemical.temperature_decomposition
_chemical.temperature_decomposition.description: The temperature in kelvins at which the solid decomposes.

_chemical.temperature_decomposition_gt.name: _chemical.temperature_decomposition_gt
_chemical.temperature_decomposition_gt.description:
A temperature in kelvins above which the solid is known to decompose. _chemical.temperature_decomposition_gt and _chemical.temperature_decomposition_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_chemical.temperature_decomposition should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_chemical.temperature_decomposition_lt.name: _chemical.temperature_decomposition_lt
_chemical.temperature_decomposition_lt.description:
A temperature in kelvins below which the solid is known to decompose. _chemical.temperature_decomposition_gt and _chemical.temperature_decomposition_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_chemical.temperature_decomposition should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_chemical.temperature_sublimation.name: _chemical.temperature_sublimation
_chemical.temperature_sublimation.description: The temperature in kelvins at which the solid sublimes.

_chemical.temperature_sublimation_gt.name: _chemical.temperature_sublimation_gt
_chemical.temperature_sublimation_gt.description:
A temperature in kelvins above which the solid is known to sublime. _chemical.temperature_sublimation_gt and _chemical.temperature_sublimation_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_chemical.temperature_sublimation should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_chemical.temperature_sublimation_lt.name: _chemical.temperature_sublimation_lt
_chemical.temperature_sublimation_lt.description:
A temperature in kelvins below which the solid is known to sublime. _chemical.temperature_sublimation_gt and _chemical.temperature_sublimation_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_chemical.temperature_sublimation should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_chemical_conn_atom.charge.name: _chemical_conn_atom.charge
_chemical_conn_atom.charge.description: The net integer charge assigned to this atom. This is the formal charge assignment normally found in chemical diagrams.

_chemical_conn_atom.display_x.name: _chemical_conn_atom.display_x
_chemical_conn_atom.display_x.description: The 2D Cartesian x coordinate of the position of this atom in a recognizable chemical diagram. The coordinate origin is at the lower left corner, the x axis is horizontal and the y axis is vertical. The coordinates must lie in the range 0.0 to 1.0. These coordinates can be obtained from projections of a suitable uncluttered view of the molecular structure.

_chemical_conn_atom.display_y.name: _chemical_conn_atom.display_y
_chemical_conn_atom.display_y.description: The 2D Cartesian y coordinate of the position of this atom in a recognizable chemical diagram. The coordinate origin is at the lower left corner, the x axis is horizontal and the y axis is vertical. The coordinates must lie in the range 0.0 to 1.0. These coordinates can be obtained from projections of a suitable uncluttered view of the molecular structure.

_chemical_conn_atom.NCA.name: _chemical_conn_atom.NCA
_chemical_conn_atom.NCA.description: The number of connected atoms excluding terminal hydrogen atoms.

_chemical_conn_atom.NH.name: _chemical_conn_atom.NH
_chemical_conn_atom.NH.description: The total number of hydrogen atoms attached to this atom, regardless of whether they are included in the refinement or the ATOM_SITE list. This number is the same as _atom_site.attached_hydrogens only if none of the hydrogen atoms appear in the ATOM_SITE list.

_chemical_conn_atom.number.name: _chemical_conn_atom.number
_chemical_conn_atom.number.description: The chemical sequence number to be associated with this atom. Within an ATOM_SITE list, this number must match one of the _atom_site.chemical_conn_number values.

_chemical_conn_atom.type_symbol.name: _chemical_conn_atom.type_symbol
_chemical_conn_atom.type_symbol.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_type.symbol in the ATOM_TYPE category.

_chemical_conn_bond.atom_1.name: _chemical_conn_bond.atom_1
_chemical_conn_bond.atom_1.description: This data item is a pointer to _chemical_conn_atom.number in the CHEMICAL_CONN_ATOM category.

_chemical_conn_bond.atom_2.name: _chemical_conn_bond.atom_2
_chemical_conn_bond.atom_2.description: This data item is a pointer to _chemical_conn_atom.number in the CHEMICAL_CONN_ATOM category.

_chemical_conn_bond.type.name: _chemical_conn_bond.type
_chemical_conn_bond.type.description: The chemical bond type associated with the connection between the two sites _chemical_conn_bond.atom_1 and _chemical_conn_bond.atom_2.

_chemical_formula.analytical.name: _chemical_formula.analytical
_chemical_formula.analytical.description: Formula determined by standard chemical analysis including trace elements. See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description for rules for writing chemical formulae. Parentheses are used only for standard uncertainties (estimated standard deviations).

_chemical_formula.entry_id.name: _chemical_formula.entry_id
_chemical_formula.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_chemical_formula.iupac.name: _chemical_formula.iupac
_chemical_formula.iupac.description:
Formula expressed in conformance with IUPAC rules for inorganic and metal-organic compounds where these conflict with the rules for any other CHEMICAL_FORMULA entries. Typically used for formatting a formula in accordance with journal rules. This should appear in the data block in addition to the most appropriate of the other CHEMICAL_FORMULA data names.

Ref: IUPAC (1990). Nomenclature of Inorganic Chemistry. Oxford: Blackwell Scientific Publications.
examples: [Co Re (C12 H22 P)2 (C O)6].0.5C H3 O H

_chemical_formula.moiety.name: _chemical_formula.moiety
_chemical_formula.moiety.description: Formula with each discrete bonded residue or ion shown as a separate moiety. See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description for rules for writing chemical formulae. In addition to the general formulae requirements, the following rules apply: (1) Moieties are separated by commas ','. (2) The order of elements within a moiety follows general rule (5) in the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description. (3) Parentheses are not used within moieties but may surround a moiety. Parentheses may not be nested. (4) Charges should be placed at the end of the moiety. The charge '+' or '-' may be preceded by a numerical multiplier and should be separated from the last (element symbol + count) by a space. Pre- or post-multipliers may be used for individual moieties.
examples: C7 H4 Cl Hg N O3 S

_chemical_formula.structural.name: _chemical_formula.structural
_chemical_formula.structural.description: See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description for the rules for writing chemical formulae for inorganics, organometallics, metal complexes etc., in which bonded groups are preserved as discrete entities within parentheses, with post-multipliers as required. The order of the elements should give as much information as possible about the chemical structure. Parentheses may be used and nested as required. This formula should correspond to the structure as actually reported, i.e. trace elements not included in atom-type and atom-site data should not be included in this formula (see also _chemical_formula.analytical).

_chemical_formula.sum.name: _chemical_formula.sum
_chemical_formula.sum.description: See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description for the rules for writing chemical formulae in which all discrete bonded residues and ions are summed over the constituent elements, following the ordering given in general rule (5) in the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description. Parentheses are not normally used.

_chemical_formula.weight.name: _chemical_formula.weight
_chemical_formula.weight.description: Formula mass in daltons. This mass should correspond to the formulae given under _chemical_formula.structural, _chemical_formula.moiety or _chemical_formula.sum and, together with the Z value and cell parameters, should yield the density given as _exptl_crystal.density_diffrn.

_chemical_formula.weight_meas.name: _chemical_formula.weight_meas
_chemical_formula.weight_meas.description: Formula mass in daltons measured by a non-diffraction experiment.

_citation.abstract.name: _citation.abstract
_citation.abstract.description: Abstract for the citation. This is used most when the citation is extracted from a bibliographic database that contains full text or abstract information.

_citation.abstract_id_CAS.name: _citation.abstract_id_CAS
_citation.abstract_id_CAS.description: The Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) abstract identifier; relevant for journal articles.

_citation.book_id_ISBN.name: _citation.book_id_ISBN
_citation.book_id_ISBN.description: The International Standard Book Number (ISBN) code assigned to the book cited; relevant for books or book chapters.

_citation.book_publisher.name: _citation.book_publisher

_citation.book_publisher_city.description: The location of the publisher of the citation; relevant for books or book chapters.

_citation.book_title.name: _citation.book_title
_citation.book_title.description: The title of the book in which the citation appeared; relevant for books or book chapters.

_citation.coordinate_linkage.name: _citation.coordinate_linkage
_citation.coordinate_linkage.description: _citation.coordinate_linkage states whether this citation is concerned with precisely the set of coordinates given in the data block. If, for instance, the publication described the same structure, but the coordinates had undergone further refinement prior to the creation of the data block, the value of this data item would be 'no'.

_citation.country.description: The country/region of publication; relevant for books and book chapters.

_citation.details.description: A description of special aspects of the relationship of the contents of the data block to the literature item cited.
examples:
citation relates to this precise
 coordinate set

citation relates to earlier low-resolution
 structure

citation relates to further refinement of
 structure reported in citation 2

_citation.id.name: _citation.id
_citation.id.description:
The value of _citation.id must uniquely identify a record in the CITATION list.

The _citation.id 'primary' should be used to indicate the citation that the author(s) consider to be the most pertinent to the contents of the data block.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_citation.page_first.description: The first page of the citation; relevant for journal articles, books and book chapters.

_citation.page_last.name: _citation.page_last
_citation.page_last.description: The last page of the citation; relevant for journal articles, books and book chapters.

examples:
Structure of diferric duck ovotransferrin
 at 2.35 Angstroms resolution.

_citation.year.description: The year of the citation; relevant for journal articles, books and book chapters.
