_database_PDB_tvect.id.name: _database_PDB_tvect.id
_database_PDB_tvect.id.description:
The value of _database_PDB_tvect.id must uniquely identify a record in the DATABASE_PDB_TVECT list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_database_PDB_tvect.vector[1].name: _database_PDB_tvect.vector[1]
_database_PDB_tvect.vector[1].description: The [1] element of the PDB TVECT vector.

_database_PDB_tvect.vector[2].name: _database_PDB_tvect.vector[2]
_database_PDB_tvect.vector[2].description: The [2] element of the PDB TVECT vector.

_database_PDB_tvect.vector[3].name: _database_PDB_tvect.vector[3]
_database_PDB_tvect.vector[3].description: The [3] element of the PDB TVECT vector.

_diffrn.ambient_environment.name: _diffrn.ambient_environment
_diffrn.ambient_environment.description: The gas or liquid surrounding the sample, if not air.

_diffrn.ambient_temp.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp
_diffrn.ambient_temp.description: The mean temperature in kelvins at which the intensities were measured.

_diffrn.ambient_temp_details.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp_details
_diffrn.ambient_temp_details.description: A description of special aspects of temperature control during data collection.

_diffrn.ambient_temp_esd.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp_esd
_diffrn.ambient_temp_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _diffrn.ambient_temp.

_diffrn.crystal_id.name: _diffrn.crystal_id
_diffrn.crystal_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_diffrn.crystal_support.name: _diffrn.crystal_support
_diffrn.crystal_support.description: The physical device used to support the crystal during data collection.

_diffrn.crystal_treatment.name: _diffrn.crystal_treatment
_diffrn.crystal_treatment.description: Remarks about how the crystal was treated prior to intensity measurement. Particularly relevant when intensities were measured at low temperature.
examples: slow cooled with direct air stream

_diffrn.details.name: _diffrn.details
_diffrn.details.description: Special details of the diffraction measurement process. Should include information about source instability, crystal motion, degradation and so on.

_diffrn.id.name: _diffrn.id
_diffrn.id.description: This data item uniquely identifies a set of diffraction data.

_diffrn.ambient_pressure.name: _diffrn.ambient_pressure
_diffrn.ambient_pressure.description: The mean hydrostatic pressure in kilopascals at which the intensities were measured.

_diffrn.ambient_pressure_gt.name: _diffrn.ambient_pressure_gt
_diffrn.ambient_pressure_gt.description:
The mean hydrostatic pressure in kilopascals above which the intensities were measured. _diffrn.ambient_pressure_gt and _diffrn.ambient_pressure_lt allow a pressure range to be given.

_diffrn.ambient_pressure should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_diffrn.ambient_pressure_lt.name: _diffrn.ambient_pressure_lt
_diffrn.ambient_pressure_lt.description:
The mean hydrostatic pressure in kilopascals below which the intensities were measured. _diffrn.ambient_pressure_gt and _diffrn.ambient_pressure_lt allow a pressure range to be given.

_diffrn.ambient_pressure should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_diffrn.ambient_temp_gt.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp_gt
_diffrn.ambient_temp_gt.description:
The mean temperature in kelvins above which the intensities were measured.  _diffrn.ambient_temp_gt and _diffrn.ambient_temp_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_diffrn.ambient_temp should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_diffrn.ambient_temp_lt.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp_lt
_diffrn.ambient_temp_lt.description:
The mean temperature in kelvins below which the intensities were measured. _diffrn.ambient_temp_gt and _diffrn.ambient_temp_lt allow a range of temperatures to be given.

_diffrn.ambient_temp should always be used in preference to these two items whenever possible.

_diffrn.pdbx_serial_crystal_experiment.name: _diffrn.pdbx_serial_crystal_experiment
_diffrn.pdbx_serial_crystal_experiment.description: Y/N if using serial crystallography experiment in which multiple crystals contribute to each diffraction frame in the experiment.

_diffrn_attenuator.code.name: _diffrn_attenuator.code
_diffrn_attenuator.code.description: A code associated with a particular attenuator setting. This code is referenced by the _diffrn_refln.attenuator_code which is stored with the diffraction data. See _diffrn_attenuator.scale.

_diffrn_attenuator.scale.name: _diffrn_attenuator.scale
_diffrn_attenuator.scale.description: The scale factor applied when an intensity measurement is reduced by an attenuator identified by _diffrn_attenuator.code. The measured intensity must be multiplied by this scale to convert it to the same scale as unattenuated intensities.

_diffrn_attenuator.material.name: _diffrn_attenuator.material
_diffrn_attenuator.material.description: Material from which the attenuator is made.

_diffrn_data_frame.array_id.name: _diffrn_data_frame.array_id
_diffrn_data_frame.array_id.description: This item is a pointer to _array_structure.id  in the ARRAY_STRUCTURE  category.

_diffrn_data_frame.array_section_id.name: _diffrn_data_frame.array_section_id
_diffrn_data_frame.array_section_id.description: This item is a pointer to _array_structure_list_section.id in the ARRAY_STRUCTURE_LIST_SECTION  category.

_diffrn_data_frame.binary_id.name: _diffrn_data_frame.binary_id
_diffrn_data_frame.binary_id.description: This item is a pointer to _array_data.binary_id  in the ARRAY_DATA  category.

_diffrn_data_frame.center_fast.name: _diffrn_data_frame.center_fast
_diffrn_data_frame.center_fast.description:
The value of _diffrn_data_frame.center_fast  is the fast index axis beam centre position relative to the detector element face in the units specified in the data item _diffrn_data_frame.center_units  along the fast axis of the detector from the centre of the first pixel to the point at which the Z axis (which should be collinear with the beam) intersects the face of the detector, if in fact it does. At the time of the measurement the current settings of the detector positioner for the given frame are used.

It is important to note that for measurements in mm, the sense of the axis is used, rather than the sign of the pixel-to-pixel increments.

_diffrn_data_frame.center_slow.name: _diffrn_data_frame.center_slow
_diffrn_data_frame.center_slow.description:
The value of _diffrn_data_frame.center_slow  is the slow index axis beam centre position relative to the detector element face in the units specified in the data item _diffrn_data_frame.center_units  along the slow axis of the detector from the centre of the first pixel to the point at which the Z axis (which should be collinear with the beam) intersects the face of the detector, if in fact it does. At the time of the measurement the current settings of the detector positioner for the given frame are used.

It is important to note that the sense of the axis is used, rather than the sign of the pixel-to-pixel increments.

_diffrn_data_frame.center_derived.name: _diffrn_data_frame.center_derived
_diffrn_data_frame.center_derived.description: The value of _diffrn_data_frame.center_derived is assumed to be 'yes', i.e. that values of _diffrn_data_frame.center_fast  and _diffrn_data_frame.center_slow are derived from the axis settings rather than measured.

_diffrn_data_frame.center_units.name: _diffrn_data_frame.center_units
_diffrn_data_frame.center_units.description:
The value of _diffrn_data_frame.center_units specifies the units in which the values of _diffrn_data_frame.center_fast  and _diffrn_data_frame.center_slow are presented.  The default is 'mm' for millimetres.  The alternatives are 'pixels' and 'bins'.  In all cases the centre distances are measured from the centre of the first pixel, i.e. in a 2x2 binning, the measuring origin is offset from the centres of the bins by one half pixel towards the first pixel.

If 'bins' is specified, the data in _array_intensities.pixel_fast_bin_size, _array_intensities.pixel_slow_bin_size, and _array_intensities.pixel_binning_method are used to define the binning scheme.

_diffrn_data_frame.detector_element_id.name: _diffrn_data_frame.detector_element_id
_diffrn_data_frame.detector_element_id.description: This item is a pointer to _diffrn_detector_element.id in the DIFFRN_DETECTOR_ELEMENT  category.

_diffrn_data_frame.id.name: _diffrn_data_frame.id
_diffrn_data_frame.id.description: The value of _diffrn_data_frame.id  must uniquely identify each complete frame of data.

_diffrn_data_frame.details.name: _diffrn_data_frame.details
_diffrn_data_frame.details.description:
The value of _diffrn_data_frame.details  should give a description of special aspects of each frame of data.

This is an appropriate location in which to record information from vendor headers as presented in those headers, but it should never be used as a substitute for providing the fully parsed information within the appropriate imgCIF/CBF categories.

Normally, when a conversion from a miniCBF has been done the data from _array_data.header_convention should be transferred to this data item and _array_data.header_convention should be removed.

_diffrn_detector.details.name: _diffrn_detector.details
_diffrn_detector.details.description: A description of special aspects of the radiation detector.

_diffrn_detector.detector.name: _diffrn_detector.detector
_diffrn_detector.detector.description: The general class of the radiation detector.

_diffrn_detector.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_detector.diffrn_id
_diffrn_detector.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_detector.type.name: _diffrn_detector.type
_diffrn_detector.type.description: The make, model or name of the detector device used.

_diffrn_detector.area_resol_mean.name: _diffrn_detector.area_resol_mean
_diffrn_detector.area_resol_mean.description: The resolution of an area detector, in pixels/mm.

_diffrn_detector.dtime.name: _diffrn_detector.dtime
_diffrn_detector.dtime.description: The deadtime in microseconds of the detector used to measure the diffraction intensities.

_diffrn_detector.pdbx_frames_total.name: _diffrn_detector.pdbx_frames_total
_diffrn_detector.pdbx_frames_total.description: The total number of data frames collected for this data set.

_diffrn_detector.pdbx_collection_time_to.name: _diffrn_detector.pdbx_collection_time_total
_diffrn_detector.pdbx_collection_time_to.description: The total number of seconds required to measure this data set.

_diffrn_detector.pdbx_frequency.name: _diffrn_detector.pdbx_frequency
_diffrn_detector.pdbx_frequency.description: The operating frequency of the detector (Hz) used in data collection.

_diffrn_detector.id.name: _diffrn_detector.id
_diffrn_detector.id.description:
The value of _diffrn_detector.id  must uniquely identify each detector used to collect each diffraction data set.

If the value of _diffrn_detector.id  is not given, it is implicitly equal to the value of _diffrn_detector.diffrn_id.

_diffrn_detector.number_of_axes.name: _diffrn_detector.number_of_axes
_diffrn_detector.number_of_axes.description:
The value of _diffrn_detector.number_of_axes  gives the number of axes of the positioner for the detector identified by _diffrn_detector.id.

The word 'positioner' is a general term used in instrumentation design for devices that are used to change the positions of portions of apparatus by linear translation, rotation or combinations of such motions.

Axes which are used to provide a coordinate system for the face of an area detector should not be counted for this data item.

The description of each axis should be provided by entries in DIFFRN_DETECTOR_AXIS.

_diffrn_detector_axis.axis_id.name: _diffrn_detector_axis.axis_id
_diffrn_detector_axis.axis_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _axis.id  in the AXIS category.

_diffrn_detector_axis.detector_id.name: _diffrn_detector_axis.detector_id
_diffrn_detector_axis.detector_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_detector.id  in the DIFFRN_DETECTOR  category.

This item was previously named _diffrn_detector_axis.id which is now a deprecated name.  The old name is provided as an alias but should not be used for new work.

_diffrn_detector_element.id.name: _diffrn_detector_element.id
_diffrn_detector_element.id.description: The value of _diffrn_detector_element.id  must uniquely identify each element of a detector.

_diffrn_detector_element.detector_id.name: _diffrn_detector_element.detector_id
_diffrn_detector_element.detector_id.description: This item is a pointer to _diffrn_detector.id in the DIFFRN_DETECTOR  category.

_diffrn_detector_element.reference_cente.name: _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_fast
_diffrn_detector_element.reference_cente.description:
The value of _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_fast  is the fast index axis beam centre position relative to the detector element face in the units specified in the data item _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_units  along the fast axis of the detector from the centre of the first pixel to the point at which the Z-axis (which should be collinear with the beam) intersects the face of the detector, if in fact it does. At the time of the measurement all settings of the detector positioner should be at their reference settings.  If more than one reference setting has been used the value given should be representative of the beam centre as determined from the ensemble of settings.

It is important to note that for measurements in mm, the sense of the axis is used, rather than the sign of the pixel-to-pixel increments.

_diffrn_detector_element.reference_cente.name: _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_slow
_diffrn_detector_element.reference_cente.description:
The value of _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_slow  is the slow index axis beam centre position relative to the detector element face in the units specified in the data item _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_units  along the slow axis of the detector from the centre of the first pixel to the point at which the Z-axis (which should be collinear with the beam) intersects the face of the detector, if in fact it does. At the time of the measurement all settings of the detector positioner should be at their reference settings.  If more than one reference setting has been used the value given should be representative of the beam centre as determined from the ensemble of settings.

It is important to note that the sense of the axis is used, rather than the sign of the pixel-to-pixel increments.

_diffrn_detector_element.reference_cente.name: _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_units
_diffrn_detector_element.reference_cente.description:
The value of _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_units specifies the units in which the values of _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_fast  and _diffrn_detector_element.reference_center_slow are presented.  The default is 'mm' for millimetres.  The alternatives are 'pixels' and 'bins'.  In all cases the centre distances are measured from the centre of the first pixel, i.e. in a 2x2 binning, the measuring origin is offset from the centres of the bins by one half pixel towards the first pixel.

If 'bins' is specified, the data in _array_intensities.pixel_fast_bin_size, _array_intensities.pixel_slow_bin_size, and _array_intensities.pixel_binning_method are used to define the binning scheme.

_diffrn_measurement.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_measurement.diffrn_id
_diffrn_measurement.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_measurement.details.name: _diffrn_measurement.details
_diffrn_measurement.details.description: A description of special aspects of the intensity measurement.
examples:
440 frames, 0.20 degrees, 150 sec, detector
 distance 12 cm, detector angle 22.5 degrees

_diffrn_measurement.device.name: _diffrn_measurement.device
_diffrn_measurement.device.description: The general class of  goniometer or device used to support and orient the specimen.

_diffrn_measurement.device_details.name: _diffrn_measurement.device_details
_diffrn_measurement.device_details.description: A description of special aspects of the device used to measure the diffraction intensities.
examples:
commercial goniometer modified locally to
allow for 90\% \t arc

_diffrn_measurement.device_type.name: _diffrn_measurement.device_type
_diffrn_measurement.device_type.description: The make, model or name of the measurement device (goniometer) used.

_diffrn_measurement.specimen_support.name: _diffrn_measurement.specimen_support
_diffrn_measurement.specimen_support.description: The physical device used to support the crystal during data collection.

_diffrn_measurement.pdbx_angle_start.name: _diffrn_measurement.pdbx_angle_start
_diffrn_measurement.pdbx_angle_start.description: The starting angle for crystal rotation.

_diffrn_measurement.pdbx_angle_end.name: _diffrn_measurement.pdbx_angle_end
_diffrn_measurement.pdbx_angle_end.description: The ending angle for crystal rotation.

_diffrn_measurement.pdbx_exposure_time_p.name: _diffrn_measurement.pdbx_exposure_time_per_image
_diffrn_measurement.pdbx_exposure_time_p.description: The exposure time per image for the crystal and detector/camera.

_diffrn_measurement.rotation_mode.name: _diffrn_measurement.rotation_mode
_diffrn_measurement.rotation_mode.description: Originated from IUCr Electron Diffraction CIF extension. Code describing the technique used to sample as completely as possible the accessible range of reciprocal space. Rotation mode indicates the detector records diffracted intensities continuously while the sample is rotated. Stepwise model indicates the detector records diffracted intensities while the sample is stationary.

_diffrn_measurement.method_precession.name: _diffrn_measurement.method_precession
_diffrn_measurement.method_precession.description: Originated from IUCr Electron Diffraction CIF extension. Flag indicating if the radiation beam illuminates the sample at an angle that is rotated about a precession axis.

_diffrn_measurement.sample_tracking.name: _diffrn_measurement.sample_tracking
_diffrn_measurement.sample_tracking.description: Originated from IUCr Electron Diffraction CIF extension. Flag indicating if a tracking method to follow the crystal was used in the data acquisition. Typically used to track very small crystals in electron diffraction experiments.

_diffrn_measurement.sample_tracking_meth.name: _diffrn_measurement.sample_tracking_method
_diffrn_measurement.sample_tracking_meth.description: Originated from IUCr Electron Diffraction CIF extension. Description of the method used to follow the crystal during data collection.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.diffrn_id
_diffrn_orient_matrix.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.type.name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.type
_diffrn_orient_matrix.type.description: A description of the orientation matrix type and how it should be applied to define the orientation of the crystal precisely with respect to the diffractometer axes.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][1].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][1]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][1].description: The [1][1] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][2].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][2]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][2].description: The [1][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][3].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][3]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][3].description: The [1][3] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][1].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][1]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][1].description: The [2][1] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][2].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][2]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][2].description: The [2][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][3].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][3]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][3].description: The [2][3] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][1].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][1]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][1].description: The [3][1] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][2].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][2]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][2].description: The [3][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][3].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][3]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][3].description: The [3][3] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the local  diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_chi.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_chi
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_chi.description: Diffractometer angle chi of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix in degrees. See _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_kappa.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_kappa
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_kappa.description: Diffractometer angle kappa of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix in degrees. See _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_omega.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_omega
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_omega.description: Diffractometer angle omega of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix in degrees. See _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_phi.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_phi
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_phi.description: Diffractometer angle phi of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix in degrees. See _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_psi.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_psi
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_psi.description: Diffractometer angle psi of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix in degrees. See _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_theta.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_theta
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_theta.description: Diffractometer angle theta of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix in degrees. See _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.diffrn_id
_diffrn_orient_refln.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.index_h.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.index_h
_diffrn_orient_refln.index_h.description: Miller index h of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix.

_diffrn_orient_refln.index_k.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.index_k
_diffrn_orient_refln.index_k.description: Miller index k of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix.

_diffrn_orient_refln.index_l.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.index_l
_diffrn_orient_refln.index_l.description: Miller index l of a reflection used to define the orientation matrix.

_diffrn_radiation.collimation.name: _diffrn_radiation.collimation
_diffrn_radiation.collimation.description: The collimation or focusing applied to the radiation.

_diffrn_radiation.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_radiation.diffrn_id
_diffrn_radiation.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_radiation.filter_edge.name: _diffrn_radiation.filter_edge
_diffrn_radiation.filter_edge.description: Absorption edge in angstroms of the radiation filter used.

_diffrn_radiation.inhomogeneity.name: _diffrn_radiation.inhomogeneity
_diffrn_radiation.inhomogeneity.description: Half-width in millimetres of the incident beam in the direction perpendicular to the diffraction plane.

_diffrn_radiation.monochromator.name: _diffrn_radiation.monochromator
_diffrn_radiation.monochromator.description: The method used to obtain monochromatic radiation. If a mono- chromator crystal is used, the material and the indices of the Bragg reflection are specified.

_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm.name: _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm
_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm.description: The angle in degrees, as viewed from the specimen, between the perpendicular component of the polarization and the diffraction plane. See _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio.

_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio.name: _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio
_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio.description: Polarization ratio of the diffraction beam incident on the crystal. This is the ratio of the perpendicularly polarized to the parallel-polarized component of the radiation. The perpendicular component forms an angle of _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm to the normal to the diffraction plane of the sample (i.e. the plane containing the incident and reflected beams).

_diffrn_radiation.probe.name: _diffrn_radiation.probe
_diffrn_radiation.probe.description: The nature of the radiation used (i.e. the name of the subatomic particle or the region of the electromagnetic spectrum). It is strongly recommended that this information is given, so that the probe radiation can be simply determined.

_diffrn_radiation.type.name: _diffrn_radiation.type
_diffrn_radiation.type.description: The nature of the radiation. This is typically a description of the X-ray wavelength in Siegbahn notation.

_diffrn_radiation.xray_symbol.name: _diffrn_radiation.xray_symbol
_diffrn_radiation.xray_symbol.description: The IUPAC symbol for the X-ray wavelength for the probe radiation.
enums: use where K-L~3~ and K-L~2~ are not resolved

_diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id.name: _diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id
_diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id in the DIFFRN_RADIATION_WAVELENGTH category.

_diffrn_radiation.pdbx_wavelength_list.name: _diffrn_radiation.pdbx_wavelength_list
_diffrn_radiation.pdbx_wavelength_list.description: Comma separated list of wavelengths or wavelength range.

_diffrn_radiation.pdbx_analyzer.name: _diffrn_radiation.pdbx_analyzer
_diffrn_radiation.pdbx_analyzer.description: Indicates the method used to obtain monochromatic radiation. _diffrn_radiation.monochromator describes the primary beam monochromator (pre-specimen monochromation). _diffrn_radiation.pdbx_analyzer specifies the post-diffraction analyser (post-specimen) monochromation. Note that monochromators may have either 'parallel' or 'antiparallel' orientation. It is assumed that the geometry is parallel unless specified otherwise. In a parallel geometry, the position of the monochromator allows the incident beam and the final post-specimen and post-monochromator beam to be as close to parallel as possible. In a parallel geometry, the diffracting planes in the specimen and monochromator will be parallel when 2*theta(monochromator) is equal to 2*theta (specimen). For further discussion see R. Jenkins and R. Snyder, Introduction to X-ray Powder Diffraction, Wiley (1996), pp. 164-5.

_diffrn_radiation.pdbx_scattering_type.name: _diffrn_radiation.pdbx_scattering_type
_diffrn_radiation.pdbx_scattering_type.description: The radiation scattering type for this diffraction data set.

_diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id.name: _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id
_diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id.description:
The code identifying each value of _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.wavelength. Items in the DIFFRN_RADIATION_WAVELENGTH category are looped when multiple wavelengths are used.

This code is used to link with the DIFFRN_REFLN category. The _diffrn_refln.wavelength_id codes must match one of the codes defined in this category.

_diffrn_radiation_wavelength.wt.name: _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.wt
_diffrn_radiation_wavelength.wt.description: The relative weight of a wavelength identified by the code _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id in the list of wavelengths.

_diffrn_refln.angle_chi.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_chi
_diffrn_refln.angle_chi.description: The diffractometer angle chi of a reflection in degrees. This angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_kappa.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_kappa
_diffrn_refln.angle_kappa.description: The diffractometer angle kappa of a reflection in degrees. This angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_omega.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_omega
_diffrn_refln.angle_omega.description: The diffractometer angle omega of a reflection in degrees. This angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_phi.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_phi
_diffrn_refln.angle_phi.description: The diffractometer angle phi of a reflection in degrees. This angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_psi.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_psi
_diffrn_refln.angle_psi.description: The diffractometer angle psi of a reflection in degrees. This angle corresponds  to the specified orientation matrix and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_theta.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_theta
_diffrn_refln.angle_theta.description: The diffractometer angle theta of a reflection in degrees. This angle corresponds  to the specified orientation matrix and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell transformations.

_diffrn_refln.attenuator_code.name: _diffrn_refln.attenuator_code
_diffrn_refln.attenuator_code.description: The code identifying the attenuator setting for this reflection. This code must match one of the _diffrn_attenuator.code values.

_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_1.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_bg_1
_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_1.description: The diffractometer counts for the measurement of the background before the peak.

_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_2.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_bg_2
_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_2.description: The diffractometer counts for the measurement of the background after the peak.

_diffrn_refln.counts_net.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_net
_diffrn_refln.counts_net.description: The diffractometer counts for the measurement of net counts after background removal.

_diffrn_refln.counts_peak.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_peak
_diffrn_refln.counts_peak.description: The diffractometer counts for the measurement of counts for the peak scan or position.

_diffrn_refln.counts_total.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_total
_diffrn_refln.counts_total.description: The diffractometer counts for the measurement of total counts (background plus peak).

_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_horiz.name: _diffrn_refln.detect_slit_horiz
_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_horiz.description: Total slit aperture in degrees in the diffraction plane.

_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_vert.name: _diffrn_refln.detect_slit_vert
_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_vert.description: Total slit aperture in degrees perpendicular to the diffraction plane.

_diffrn_refln.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_refln.diffrn_id
_diffrn_refln.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_refln.elapsed_time.name: _diffrn_refln.elapsed_time
_diffrn_refln.elapsed_time.description: Elapsed time in minutes from the start of the diffraction experiment to the measurement of this intensity.

_diffrn_refln.id.name: _diffrn_refln.id
_diffrn_refln.id.description:
The value of _diffrn_refln.id must uniquely identify the reflection in the data set identified by the item _diffrn_refln.diffrn_id.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_diffrn_refln.index_h.name: _diffrn_refln.index_h
_diffrn_refln.index_h.description: Miller index h of a reflection. The values of the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category need not match the values of the Miller indices in the REFLN category if a transformation of the original measured cell has taken place. Details of the cell transformation are given in _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process. See also _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[][].

_diffrn_refln.index_k.name: _diffrn_refln.index_k
_diffrn_refln.index_k.description: Miller index k of a reflection. The values of the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category need not match the values of the Miller indices in the REFLN category if a transformation of the original measured cell has taken place. Details of the cell transformation are given in _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process. See also _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[][].

_diffrn_refln.index_l.name: _diffrn_refln.index_l
_diffrn_refln.index_l.description: Miller index l of a reflection. The values of the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category need not match the values of the Miller indices in the REFLN category if a transformation of the original measured cell has taken place. Details of the cell transformation are given in _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process. See also _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[][].

_diffrn_refln.intensity_net.name: _diffrn_refln.intensity_net
_diffrn_refln.intensity_net.description: Net intensity calculated from the diffraction counts after the attenuator and standard scales have been applied.

_diffrn_refln.intensity_sigma.name: _diffrn_refln.intensity_sigma
_diffrn_refln.intensity_sigma.description: Standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of the intensity calculated from the diffraction counts after the attenuator and standard scales have been applied.

_diffrn_refln.scale_group_code.name: _diffrn_refln.scale_group_code
_diffrn_refln.scale_group_code.description:
The code identifying the scale applying to this reflection.

This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_scale_group.code in the DIFFRN_SCALE_GROUP category.

_diffrn_refln.scan_mode.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_mode
_diffrn_refln.scan_mode.description: The code identifying the mode of scanning for measurements using a diffractometer. See _diffrn_refln.scan_width and _diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd.

_diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd
_diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd.description: The code identifying the mode of scanning a reflection to measure the background intensity.

_diffrn_refln.scan_rate.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_rate
_diffrn_refln.scan_rate.description: The rate of scanning a reflection in degrees per minute to measure the intensity.

_diffrn_refln.scan_time_backgd.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_time_backgd
_diffrn_refln.scan_time_backgd.description: The time spent measuring each background in seconds.

_diffrn_refln.scan_width.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_width
_diffrn_refln.scan_width.description: The scan width in degrees of the scan mode defined by the code _diffrn_refln.scan_mode.

_diffrn_refln.sint_over_lambda.name: _diffrn_refln.sint_over_lambda
_diffrn_refln.sint_over_lambda.description: The (sin theta)/lambda value in reciprocal angstroms for this reflection.

_diffrn_refln.standard_code.name: _diffrn_refln.standard_code
_diffrn_refln.standard_code.description:
The code identifying that this reflection was measured as a standard intensity.

This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_standard_refln.code in the DIFFRN_STANDARD_REFLN category.

_diffrn_refln.wavelength.name: _diffrn_refln.wavelength
_diffrn_refln.wavelength.description: The mean wavelength in angstroms of the radiation used to measure the intensity of this reflection. This is an important parameter for data collected using energy-dispersive detectors or the Laue method.

_diffrn_refln.wavelength_id.name: _diffrn_refln.wavelength_id
_diffrn_refln.wavelength_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id in the DIFFRN_RADIATION category.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_image_id.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_image_id
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_image_id.description: The identifier of the diffraction image this reflection is associated with. This is often the image where the calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_scan_angle.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_scan_angle
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_scan_angle.description: The value of the angle around the scan axis for this reflection. This is often the scan angle at which the calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

_diffrn_refln.class_code.name: _diffrn_refln.class_code
_diffrn_refln.class_code.description: The code identifying the class to which this reflection has been assigned. This code must match a value of _diffrn_reflns_class.code. Reflections may be grouped into classes for a variety of purposes. For example, for modulated structures each reflection class may be defined by the number m=sum|m~i~|, where the m~i~ are the integer coefficients that, in addition to h,k,l, index the corresponding diffraction vector in the basis defined for the reciprocal lattice.

_diffrn_refln.intensity_u.name: _diffrn_refln.intensity_u
_diffrn_refln.intensity_u.description: Standard uncertainty of the net intensity calculated from the diffraction counts after the attenuator and standard scales have been applied.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_x.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_x
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_x.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the X-direction for this reflection. This is often the position where the calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

The detector X-direction is most often along the fast changing array index of the 2D diffraction array, while the Y-coordinate is along the slow changing array index.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_y.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_y
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_y.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the Y-direction for this reflection. This is often the position where the calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

The detector X-direction is most often along the fast changing array index of the 2D diffraction array, while the Y-coordinate is along the slow changing array index.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_rotation_angle.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_rotation_angle
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_rotation_angle.description: The value of the angle around the scan axis for this reflection. This is often the scan angle at which the calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_scale_value.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_scale_value
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_scale_value.description: The scale factor applied to an individual reflection intensity at the last scaling step before merging all measurements belonging to symmetry-unique reflections into a merged intensity.

_diffrn_refln.frame_id.name: _diffrn_refln.frame_id
_diffrn_refln.frame_id.description: Pointer to _diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id in the DIFFRN_SCAN_FRAME category.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_batch_id.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_batch_id
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_batch_id.description: Pointer to _pdbx_diffrn_batch.id in the PDBX_DIFFRN_BATCH category.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_fast.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_fast
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_fast.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of the fast changing array index (of the 2D diffraction data) as observed for this reflection. This is often the position where the reflection centroid is observed.

The fast changing array index of the 2D diffraction array is often also defined as the detector X-coordinate, while the slow changing array index is defined as the Y-coordinate. This can vary depending on the convention of the detector and the facility, especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_slow.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_slow
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_slow.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of the slow changing array index (of the 2D diffraction data) as observed for this reflection. This is often the position where the reflection centroid is observed.

The slow changing array index of the 2D diffraction array is often also defined as the detector Y-coordinate, while the fast changing array index is defined as the X-coordinate. This can vary depending on the convention of the detector and the facility, especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_fast.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_fast
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_fast.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of the fast changing array index (of the 2D diffraction data) for this reflection. This is often the position where the calculated reflection is predicted to occur.

The fast changing array index of the 2D diffraction array is often also defined as the detector X-coordinate, while the slow changing array index is defined as the Y-coordinate. This can vary depending on the convention of the detector and the facility, especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_slow.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_slow
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_slow.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of the slow changing array index (of the 2D diffraction data) for this reflection. This is often the position where the calculated reflection is predicted to occur.

The slow changing array index of the 2D diffraction array is often also defined as the detector Y-coordinate, while the fast changing array index is defined as the X-coordinate. This can vary depending on the convention of the detector and the facility, especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_panel_mapping_id.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_panel_mapping_id
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_panel_mapping_id.description: Pointer to _pdbx_diffrn_detector_panel_mapping.id in the PDBX_DIFFRN_DETECTOR_PANEL_MAPPING category

_diffrn_reflns.av_R_equivalents.name: _diffrn_reflns.av_R_equivalents
_diffrn_reflns.av_R_equivalents.description: The residual [sum|avdel(I)| / sum|av(I)|] for symmetry-equivalent reflections used to calculate the average intensity av(I). The avdel(I) term is the average absolute difference between av(I) and the individual symmetry-equivalent intensities.

_diffrn_reflns.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_reflns.diffrn_id
_diffrn_reflns.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_h_max
_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_max.description: The maximum value of the Miller index h for the reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_h.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_h_min
_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_min.description: The minimum value of the Miller index h for the reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_h.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_k_max
_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_max.description: The maximum value of the Miller index k for the reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_k.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_k_min
_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_min.description: The minimum value of the Miller index k for the reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_k.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_l_max
_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_max.description: The maximum value of the Miller index l for the reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_l.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_l_min
_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_min.description: The minimum value of the Miller index l for the reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_l.

_diffrn_reflns.number.name: _diffrn_reflns.number
_diffrn_reflns.number.description: The total number of measured intensities, excluding reflections that are classified as systematically absent.

_diffrn_reflns.reduction_process.name: _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process
_diffrn_reflns.reduction_process.description: A description of the process used to reduce the intensity data into structure-factor magnitudes.

_diffrn_reflns.theta_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.theta_max
_diffrn_reflns.theta_max.description: Maximum theta angle in degrees for the measured diffraction intensities.

_diffrn_reflns.theta_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.theta_min
_diffrn_reflns.theta_min.description: Minimum theta angle in degrees for the measured diffraction intensities.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][1].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][1]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][1].description: The [1][1] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][2].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][2]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][2].description: The [1][2] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][3].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][3]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][3].description: The [1][3] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][1].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][1]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][1].description: The [2][1] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][2].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][2]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][2].description: The [2][2] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][3].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][3]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][3].description: The [2][3] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][1].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][1]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][1].description: The [3][1] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][2].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][2]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][2].description: The [3][2] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][3].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][3]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][3].description: The [3][3] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.av_unetI/netI.name: _diffrn_reflns.av_unetI/netI
_diffrn_reflns.av_unetI/netI.description: Measure [sum u(net I)|/sum|net I|] for all measured reflections.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_d_res_low.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_d_res_low
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_d_res_low.description: The lowest resolution for the interplanar spacings in the reflection data set. This is the largest d value.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_d_res_high.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_d_res_high
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_d_res_high.description: The highest resolution for the interplanar spacings in the reflection data set. This is the smallest d value.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_obs.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_obs
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_obs.description: The percentage of geometrically possible reflections represented by reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _diffrn_reflns.d_resolution_high and _diffrn_reflns.d_resolution_low and the observation limit established by   _diffrn_reflns.observed_criterion.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_Rmerge_I_obs.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_Rmerge_I_obs
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_Rmerge_I_obs.description:
The R factor for merging the reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _diffrn_reflns.d_resolution_high and _diffrn_reflns.d_resolution_low and the observation limit established by _diffrn_reflns.observed_criterion.

Rmerge(I) = [sum~i~(sum~j~|I~j~ - |)] / [sum~i~(sum~j~)]

I~j~ = the intensity of the jth observation of reflection i = the mean of the amplitudes of all observations of reflection i

sum~i~ is taken over all reflections sum~j~ is taken over all observations of each reflection

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_Rsym_value.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_Rsym_value
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_Rsym_value.description: The R factor for averaging the symmetry related reflections to a unique data set.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_chi_squared.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_chi_squared
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_chi_squared.description: Overall  Chi-squared statistic for the data set.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_redundancy.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_redundancy
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_redundancy.description: The overall redundancy for the data set.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_rejects.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_rejects
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_rejects.description: The number of rejected reflections in the data set. The reflections may be rejected by setting the observation criterion, _diffrn_reflns.observed_criterion.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_observed_criterion.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_observed_criterion
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_observed_criterion.description: The criterion used to classify a reflection as 'observed'. This criterion is usually expressed in terms of a sigma(I) or sigma(F) threshold.

_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_number_obs.name: _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_number_obs
_diffrn_reflns.pdbx_number_obs.description: The  number of reflections satisfying the observation criterion as in _diffrn_reflns.pdbx_observed_criterion

_diffrn_reflns_class.av_R_eq.name: _diffrn_reflns_class.av_R_eq
_diffrn_reflns_class.av_R_eq.description: For each reflection class, the residual [sum av|del(I)|/sum|av(I)|] for symmetry-equivalent reflections used to calculate the average intensity av(I). The av|del(I)| term is the average absolute difference between av(I) and the individual intensities.

_diffrn_reflns_class.av_sgI/I.name: _diffrn_reflns_class.av_sgI/I
_diffrn_reflns_class.av_sgI/I.description: Measure [sum|sigma(net I)|/sum|net I|] for all measured intensities in a reflection class.

_diffrn_reflns_class.av_uI/I.name: _diffrn_reflns_class.av_uI/I
_diffrn_reflns_class.av_uI/I.description: Measure [sum|u(net I)|/sum|net I|] for all measured intensities in a reflection class.

_diffrn_reflns_class.code.name: _diffrn_reflns_class.code
_diffrn_reflns_class.code.description: The code identifying a certain reflection class.

_diffrn_reflns_class.d_res_high.name: _diffrn_reflns_class.d_res_high
_diffrn_reflns_class.d_res_high.description: The smallest value in angstroms for the interplanar spacings for the reflections in each measured reflection class. This is called the highest resolution for this reflection class.

_diffrn_reflns_class.d_res_low.name: _diffrn_reflns_class.d_res_low
_diffrn_reflns_class.d_res_low.description: The largest value in angstroms of the interplanar spacings for the reflections for each measured reflection class. This is called the lowest resolution for this reflection class.

_diffrn_reflns_class.number.name: _diffrn_reflns_class.number
_diffrn_reflns_class.number.description: The total number of measured intensities for each reflection class, excluding the systematic absences arising from centring translations.

_diffrn_scale_group.code.name: _diffrn_scale_group.code
_diffrn_scale_group.code.description:
The value of _diffrn_scale_group.code must uniquely identify a record in the DIFFRN_SCALE_GROUP list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_diffrn_scale_group.I_net.name: _diffrn_scale_group.I_net
_diffrn_scale_group.I_net.description: The scale for a specific measurement group which is to be multiplied with the net intensity to place all intensities in the DIFFRN_REFLN or REFLN list on a common scale.

_diffrn_scan.id.name: _diffrn_scan.id
_diffrn_scan.id.description: The value of _diffrn_scan.id  uniquely identifies each scan.  The identifier is used to tie together all the information about the scan.

_diffrn_scan.date_end.name: _diffrn_scan.date_end
_diffrn_scan.date_end.description: The date and time of the end of the scan.  Note that this may be an estimate generated during the scan, before the precise time of the end of the scan is known, in which case _diffrn_scan.date_end_estimated  should be used instead.

_diffrn_scan.date_end_estimated.name: _diffrn_scan.date_end_estimated
_diffrn_scan.date_end_estimated.description: The estimated date and time of the end of the scan.  Note that this may be generated at the start or during the scan, before the precise time of the end of the scan is known.

_diffrn_scan.date_start.name: _diffrn_scan.date_start
_diffrn_scan.date_start.description: The date and time of the start of the scan.

_diffrn_scan.integration_time.name: _diffrn_scan.integration_time
_diffrn_scan.integration_time.description: Approximate average time in seconds to integrate each step of the scan.  The precise time for integration of each particular step must be provided in _diffrn_scan_frame.integration_time, even if all steps have the same integration time.

_diffrn_scan.frame_id_start.name: _diffrn_scan.frame_id_start
_diffrn_scan.frame_id_start.description:
The value of this data item is the identifier of the first frame in the scan.

This item is a pointer to _diffrn_data_frame.id  in the DIFFRN_DATA_FRAME  category.

_diffrn_scan.frame_id_end.name: _diffrn_scan.frame_id_end
_diffrn_scan.frame_id_end.description:
The value of this data item is the identifier of the last frame in the scan.

This item is a pointer to _diffrn_data_frame.id  in the DIFFRN_DATA_FRAME  category.

_diffrn_scan.frames.name: _diffrn_scan.frames
_diffrn_scan.frames.description: The value of this data item is the number of frames in the scan.

_diffrn_scan.time_period.name: _diffrn_scan.time_period
_diffrn_scan.time_period.description: Approximate average time in seconds between the start of each step of the scan.  The precise start-to-start time increment of each particular step may be provided in _diffrn_scan_frame.time_period.

_diffrn_scan.time_rstrt_incr.name: _diffrn_scan.time_rstrt_incr
_diffrn_scan.time_rstrt_incr.description:
Approximate average time in seconds between the end of integration of each step of the scan and the start of integration of the next step.

In general, this will agree with _diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr .  The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame.integration_time and  _diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr  is the time from the start of integration of one frame and the start of integration for the next frame and should equal the value of _diffrn_scan_frame.time_period  for this frame.   If the individual frame values vary, then the value of _diffrn_scan.time_rstrt_incr  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr  (e.g. the mean).

_diffrn_scan_axis.scan_id.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.scan_id
_diffrn_scan_axis.scan_id.description:
The value of this data item is the identifier of the scan for which axis settings are being specified.

Multiple axes may be specified for the same value of _diffrn_scan.id.

This item is a pointer to _diffrn_scan.id  in the DIFFRN_SCAN  category.

_diffrn_scan_axis.axis_id.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.axis_id
_diffrn_scan_axis.axis_id.description:
The value of this data item is the identifier of one of the axes for the scan for which settings are being specified.

Multiple axes may be specified for the same value of _diffrn_scan.id.

This item is a pointer to _axis.id  in the AXIS category.

_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_start.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.angle_start
_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_start.description: The starting position for the specified axis in degrees.

_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_range.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.angle_range
_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_range.description: The range from the starting position for the specified axis in degrees.

_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_increment.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.angle_increment
_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_increment.description: The increment for each step for the specified axis in degrees.  In general, this will agree with _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment . The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle  and _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment  is the angular setting of the axis at the end of the integration time for a given frame.  If the individual frame values vary, then the value of _diffrn_scan_axis.angle_increment  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment  (e.g. the mean).

_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_rstrt_incr.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.angle_rstrt_incr
_diffrn_scan_axis.angle_rstrt_incr.description: The increment after each step for the specified axis in degrees.  In general, this will agree with _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_rstrt_incr .  The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle, _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment and  _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_rstrt_incr  is the angular setting of the axis at the start of the integration time for the next frame relative to a given frame and should equal _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle  for this next frame.   If the individual frame values vary, then the value of _diffrn_scan_axis.angle_rstrt_incr  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_rstrt_incr  (e.g. the mean).

_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_start.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_start
_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_start.description: The starting position for the specified axis in millimetres.

_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_range.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_range
_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_range.description: The range from the starting position for the specified axis in millimetres.

_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_increment.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_increment
_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_increment.description: The increment for each step for the specified axis in millimetres.  In general, this will agree with _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_increment. The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement  and _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_increment  is the angular setting of the axis at the end of the integration time for a given frame.  If the individual frame values vary, then the value of _diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_increment  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_increment  (e.g. the mean).

_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_rstrt_inc.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_rstrt_incr
_diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_rstrt_inc.description: The increment for each step for the specified axis in millimetres.  In general, this will agree with _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_rstrt_incr. The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement, _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_increment  and _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_rstrt_incr  is the angular setting of the axis at the start of the integration time for the next frame relative to a given frame and should equal _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement for this next frame.  If the individual frame values vary, then the value of _diffrn_scan_axis.displacement_rstrt_incr  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_rstrt_incr  (e.g. the mean).

_diffrn_scan_axis.reference_angle.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.reference_angle
_diffrn_scan_axis.reference_angle.description:
The setting of the specified axis in degrees against which measurements of the reference beam centre and reference detector distance should be made.

In general, this will agree with _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_angle.

If the individual frame values vary, then the value of _diffrn_scan_axis.reference_angle  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_angle  (e.g. the mean).

If not specified, the value defaults to zero.

_diffrn_scan_axis.reference_displacement.name: _diffrn_scan_axis.reference_displacement
_diffrn_scan_axis.reference_displacement.description:
The setting of the specified axis in millimetres against which measurements of the reference beam centre and reference detector distance should be made.

In general, this will agree with _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_displacement.

If the individual frame values vary, then the value of _diffrn_scan_axis.reference_displacement  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_displacement  (e.g. the mean).

_diffrn_scan_collection.details.name: _diffrn_scan_collection.details
_diffrn_scan_collection.details.description: The value of _diffrn_scan_collection.details  should give a description of special aspects of each collection strategy.

_diffrn_scan_collection.scan_id.name: _diffrn_scan_collection.scan_id
_diffrn_scan_collection.scan_id.description:
The value of _diffrn_scan_collection.scan_id  identifies the scan containing this frame.

This item is a pointer to _diffrn_scan.id  in the DIFFRN_SCAN  category.

In the case of a single-scan dataset, the value is implicit.

_diffrn_scan_collection.type.name: _diffrn_scan_collection.type
_diffrn_scan_collection.type.description:
The value of _diffrn_scan_collection.type  identifies the strategy used in this scan, e.g. `rotation', 'raster', 'vector', 'still', etc.

The default is 'rotation'.

_diffrn_scan_collection.translation_widt.name: _diffrn_scan_collection.translation_width
_diffrn_scan_collection.translation_widt.description: The value of _diffrn_scan_collection.translation_width gives the average single step translation in micrometres in collection strategies for which this information is appropriate, e.g. 'vector'.

_diffrn_scan_frame.date.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.date
_diffrn_scan_frame.date.description: The date and time of the start of the frame being scanned.

_diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id
_diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id.description:
The value of this data item is the identifier of the frame being examined.

This item is a pointer to _diffrn_data_frame.id  in the DIFFRN_DATA_FRAME  category.

_diffrn_scan_frame.frame_number.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.frame_number
_diffrn_scan_frame.frame_number.description: The value of this data item is the number of the frame within the scan, starting with 1.  It is not necessarily the same as the value of _diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id, but it may be.

_diffrn_scan_frame.integration_time.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.integration_time
_diffrn_scan_frame.integration_time.description: The time in seconds to integrate this step of the scan. This should be the precise time of integration of each particular frame.  The value of this data item should be given explicitly for each frame and not inferred from the value of _diffrn_scan.integration_time.

_diffrn_scan_frame.polarizn_Stokes_I.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.polarizn_Stokes_I
_diffrn_scan_frame.polarizn_Stokes_I.description:
The quantity Ip+In+Inp, where Ip is the intensity (amplitude squared) of the electric vector in the plane of polarization, In is the intensity (amplitude squared) of the electric vector in the plane of the normal to the plane of polarization, and Inp is the intensity (amplitude squared) of the non-polarized (incoherent) electric vector.

This is an average or other representative sample of the frame.

This is the first of the Stokes polarization parameters, I, Q, U, V [also known as I, M, C, S; see Berry et al. (1977)].

If the absolute intensity is not known, the value 1.0 is assumed for I, and all four Stokes parameters are dimensionless.  When the absolute intensity is known, all four Stokes parameters are in units of watts per square metre.

Note that, if the polarized intensity Ip+In is required, (Ip+In)^2^ is the sum of Q^2^+U^2^+V^2^.

Reference: Berry, H. H., Gabrielse, G. & Livingston, A. E. (1977). 'Measurement of the Stokes parameters of light', Appl. Optics, 16:12, 3200--3205.

_diffrn_scan_frame.scan_id.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.scan_id
_diffrn_scan_frame.scan_id.description:
The value of _diffrn_scan_frame.scan_id  identifies the scan containing this frame.

This item is a pointer to _diffrn_scan.id  in the DIFFRN_SCAN  category.

_diffrn_scan_frame.time_period.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.time_period
_diffrn_scan_frame.time_period.description: The time in seconds between the start of this frame and the start of the next frame, if any.  If there is no next frame, a null value should be given.

_diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr.name: _diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr
_diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr.description:
The time in seconds between the end of integration of this step of the scan and the start of integration of the next step.

The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame.integration_time and  _diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr  is the time from the start of integration of one frame and the start of integration for the next frame and should equal the value of _diffrn_scan_frame.time_period  for this frame.   The value of _diffrn_scan.time_rstrt_incr  will be representative of the ensemble of values of _diffrn_scan_frame.time_rstrt_incr  (e.g. the mean).

If there is no next frame, a null value should be given.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.axis_id.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.axis_id
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.axis_id.description:
The value of this data item is the identifier of one of the axes for the frame for which settings are being specified.

Multiple axes may be specified for the same value of _diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id.

This item is a pointer to _axis.id  in the AXIS  category.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle.description: The setting of the specified axis in degrees for this frame. This is the setting at the start of the integration time.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment.description: The increment for this frame for the angular setting of the specified axis in degrees.  The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle  and _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment  is the angular setting of the axis at the end of the integration time for this frame.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_rstrt_incr.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_rstrt_incr
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_rstrt_incr.description: The increment after this frame for the angular setting of the specified axis in degrees.  The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle, _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_increment  and _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle_rstrt_incr  is the angular setting of the axis at the start of the integration time for the next frame and should equal _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.angle  for this next frame.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement.description: The setting of the specified axis in millimetres for this frame.  This is the setting at the start of the integration time.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_inc.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_increment
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_inc.description: The increment for this frame for the displacement setting of the specified axis in millimetres.  The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement  and _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_increment  is the angular setting of the axis at the end of the integration time for this frame.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_rst.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_rstrt_incr
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_rst.description: The increment for this frame for the displacement setting of the specified axis in millimetres.  The sum of the values of _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement, _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_increment  and _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement_rstrt_incr  is the angular setting of the axis at the start of the integration time for the next frame and should equal _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement  for this next frame.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.frame_id.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.frame_id
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.frame_id.description:
The value of this data item is the identifier of the frame for which axis settings are being specified.

Multiple axes may be specified for the same value of _diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id.

This item is a pointer to _diffrn_data_frame.id  in the DIFFRN_DATA_FRAME  category.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_angle.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_angle
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_angle.description:
The setting of the specified axis in degrees against which measurements of the reference beam centre and reference detector distance should be made.

This is normally the same for all frames, but the option is provided here of making changes when needed.

If not provided, it is assumed to be zero.

_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_displa.name: _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_displacement
_diffrn_scan_frame_axis.reference_displa.description:
The setting of the specified axis in millimetres for this frame against which measurements of the reference beam centre and reference detector distance should be made.

This is normally the same for all frames, but the option is provided here of making changes when needed.

If not provided, it is assumed to be equal to _diffrn_scan_frame_axis.displacement.

_diffrn_source.current.name: _diffrn_source.current
_diffrn_source.current.description: The current in milliamperes at which the radiation source was operated.

_diffrn_source.details.name: _diffrn_source.details
_diffrn_source.details.description: A description of special aspects of the radiation source used.

_diffrn_source.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_source.diffrn_id
_diffrn_source.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_source.power.name: _diffrn_source.power
_diffrn_source.power.description: The power in kilowatts at which the radiation source was operated.

_diffrn_source.size.name: _diffrn_source.size
_diffrn_source.size.description: The dimensions of the source as viewed from the sample.

_diffrn_source.source.name: _diffrn_source.source
_diffrn_source.source.description: The general class of the radiation source.

_diffrn_source.target.name: _diffrn_source.target
_diffrn_source.target.description: The chemical element symbol for the X-ray target (usually the anode) used to generate X-rays. This can also be used for spallation sources.

_diffrn_source.type.name: _diffrn_source.type
_diffrn_source.type.description: The make, model or name of the source of radiation.

_diffrn_source.voltage.name: _diffrn_source.voltage
_diffrn_source.voltage.description: The voltage in kilovolts at which the radiation source was operated.

_diffrn_source.take-off_angle.name: _diffrn_source.take-off_angle
_diffrn_source.take-off_angle.description: The complement of the angle in degrees between the normal to the surface of the X-ray tube target and the primary X-ray beam for beams generated by traditional X-ray tubes.

_diffrn_source.pdbx_wavelength_list.name: _diffrn_source.pdbx_wavelength_list
_diffrn_source.pdbx_wavelength_list.description: Comma separated list of wavelengths or wavelength range.

_diffrn_source.pdbx_synchrotron_y_n.name: _diffrn_source.pdbx_synchrotron_y_n
_diffrn_source.pdbx_synchrotron_y_n.description: Yes/No if synchrotron source was used or not.

_diffrn_standard_refln.code.name: _diffrn_standard_refln.code
_diffrn_standard_refln.code.description: The code identifying a reflection measured as a standard reflection with the indices _diffrn_standard_refln.index_h, _diffrn_standard_refln.index_k and _diffrn_standard_refln.index_l. This is the same code as the _diffrn_refln.standard_code in the DIFFRN_REFLN list.

_diffrn_standard_refln.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_standard_refln.diffrn_id
_diffrn_standard_refln.diffrn_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN category.

_diffrn_standard_refln.index_h.name: _diffrn_standard_refln.index_h
_diffrn_standard_refln.index_h.description: Miller index h of a standard reflection used in the diffraction measurement process.

_diffrn_standards.decay_%.description: The percentage decrease in the mean of the intensities for the set of standard reflections from the start of the measurement process to the end. This value usually affords a measure of the overall decay in crystal quality during the diffraction measurement process. Negative values are used in exceptional instances where the final intensities are greater than the initial ones.

_em_2d_crystal_entity.space_group_name_H.name: _em_2d_crystal_entity.space_group_name_H-M
_em_2d_crystal_entity.space_group_name_H.description:
There are 17 plane groups classified as oblique, rectangular, square, and hexagonal. To describe the symmetry of 2D crystals of biological molecules, plane groups are expanded to equivalent noncentrosymmetric space groups. The 2D crystal plane corresponds to the 'ab' plane of the space group.

Enumerated space group descriptions include the plane group number in parentheses, the H-M plane group symbol, and the plane group class.

_em_3d_reconstruction.resolution_method.name: _em_3d_reconstruction.resolution_method
_em_3d_reconstruction.resolution_method.description: The  method used to determine the final resolution of the 3d reconstruction. The Fourier Shell Correlation criterion as a measure of resolution is based on the concept of splitting the (2D) data set into two halves; averaging each and comparing them using the Fourier Ring Correlation (FRC) technique.

_em_3d_reconstruction.magnification_cali.name: _em_3d_reconstruction.magnification_calibration
_em_3d_reconstruction.magnification_cali.description: The magnification calibration method for the 3d reconstruction.

_em_3d_reconstruction.ctf_correction_met.name: _em_3d_reconstruction.ctf_correction_method
_em_3d_reconstruction.ctf_correction_met.description: The  CTF-correction method. The Contrast Transfer Function CTF compensation for low contrast specimens (e.g. frozen-hydrated), for which phase contrast is the only significant mechanism, then higher defocus levels must be used to achieve any significant transfer, and several images at different focus levels must be combined to complete the information lost from the transfer gaps of any one image.  The CTF correction can be applied to each extracted particle separately or to the whole micrograph after digitisation. The simplest level of compensation is to reverse phases at the negative lobes of the CTF.
examples: The volumes were CTF-corrected in defocus groups, with an average of approximately 999 individual images per group

_em_3d_reconstruction.nominal_pixel_size.name: _em_3d_reconstruction.nominal_pixel_size
_em_3d_reconstruction.nominal_pixel_size.description: The nominal pixel size of the projection set of images in Angstroms.

_em_depui.depositor_hold_instructions.description: Choose the manner in which you would like the map and associated files (half maps, additional maps, masks, FSC curves, structure factors, layer lines, and images) to be released.  Release of these files can be delayed either until publication of the associated primary citation or until one year after completion of the deposition. It is the responsibility of the depositor to notify the EMDB when the primary citation has been published. Please note that map-associated experimental information and metadata (header data) are made available to the public when an entry is placed on hold.

Example of valid request: * Author wants to replace a map that has already been released, following discovery of a processing error Examples of invalid requests: * Someone other than the author wants to obsolete a map. * Legal conflict of interest Obsoletion is not required for the following actions: * to change released metadata info. * to update the map of an HPUB entry. * to change the hand of a released map. * to deposit an improved version of a released map.

_em_detector.detective_quantum_efficienc.name: _em_detector.detective_quantum_efficiency
_em_detector.detective_quantum_efficienc.description: The detective_quantum_efficiency (DQE)is defined as the square of the signal-to-noise ratio in the recording device divided by the square of the signal-to-ratio in the electron beam: (SIGNAL/NOISE)2 recording device DQE =    ------------------------------- (SIGNAL/NOISE)2 electron beam A DQE value of 1 indicates a perfect recorder. "DQE = 0.25" menas that the signal-to-noise ratio is reduced by half in the recording step. (0.5)**2 DQE = --------- = 0.25. (1.0)**2

_em_map.data_type.name: _em_map.data_type
_em_map.data_type.description: The map data_type describes the data structure of the map voxels. (CCP4 map header word 4 MODE) EMDB currently holds MODE=0,1,and 2 maps; the majority are MODE=2. MAPS with MODES other than 2 and 0 may not work in CCP4 programs. MODE = 0: 8 bits, density stored as a signed byte (-128 to 127, ISO/IEC 10967) MODE = 1: 16 bits, density stored as a signed integer (-32768 to 32767, ISO/IEC 10967) MODE = 2: 32 bits, density stored as a floating point number (IEEE 754)
enums: Image stored as floating point number (4 bytes)
Image stored as signed integer (2 bytes)
