_em_sample_preparation.details.name: _em_sample_preparation.details
examples:
Detergent-solubilized particles eluted from the cation-exchange
 column were directly adsorbed for 1 min to parlodion carbon-coated
 copper grids rendered hydrophilic by glow discharge at low pressure
 in air. Grids were washed with 4 drops of double-distilled water
 and stained with 2 drops of 0.75% uranyl formate.

Selectively stained by injection of horseradish peroxidase,
 embedded in Spurr's resin and cut into 2-3 um thick sections.

S. cerevisiae PDC was purified to near homogeneity
 from baker's yeast by modification of a published procedure. Highly
 purified E1 was obtained by resolution of PDC with 2 M NaCl at pH 7.3
 followed by FPLC on a Superdex 200 column. The weight-average molecular
 weight of the PDC was determined by light scattering measurement to be
 ~8 x 106. On the basis of the known molecular weight of the complex and
 its component enzymes and the experimentally determined polypeptide chain
 ratios of E2/BP/E3, we estimated that the subunit composition of the
 S. cerevisiae PDC is ~24 E1 tetramers, 60 E2 monomers, 12 BP monomers,
 and 8 E3 dimers. Sufficient E1 was added to a sample of the PDC
 preparation to increase the molar ratio of E1/E2 core to 60:1.

_entity.formula_weight.description: Formula mass in daltons of the entity.

_entity.id.name: _entity.id
_entity.id.description:
The value of _entity.id must uniquely identify a record in the ENTITY list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_entity.src_method.name: _entity.src_method
_entity.src_method.description: The method by which the sample for the entity was produced. Entities isolated directly from natural sources (tissues, soil samples etc.) are expected to have further information in the ENTITY_SRC_NAT category. Entities isolated from genetically manipulated sources are expected to have further information in the ENTITY_SRC_GEN category.
enums: entity isolated from a genetically manipulated source
entity isolated from a natural source

_entity.type.name: _entity.type
_entity.type.description:
Defines the type of the entity.

Polymer entities are expected to have corresponding ENTITY_POLY and associated entries.

Non-polymer entities are expected to have corresponding CHEM_COMP and associated entries.

Water entities are not expected to have corresponding entries in the ENTITY category.

_entity.pdbx_description.name: _entity.pdbx_description
_entity.pdbx_description.description:
A description of the entity.

Corresponds to the compound name in the PDB format.

_entity.pdbx_parent_entity_id.name: _entity.pdbx_parent_entity_id
_entity.pdbx_parent_entity_id.description: An identifier for the parent entity if this entity is part of a complex entity.  For instance a chimeric entity may be decomposed into several independent chemical entities where each component entity was obtained from a different source.

_entity.pdbx_entities_per_biological_uni.name: _entity.pdbx_entities_per_biological_unit
_entity.pdbx_entities_per_biological_uni.description: Number of entity molecules in the biological assembly.

_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code.name: _entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code
_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code.description:
Sequence of protein or nucleic acid polymer in standard one-letter codes of amino acids or nucleotides. Non-standard amino acids/nucleotides are represented by their Chemical Component Dictionary (CCD) codes in parenthesis. Deoxynucleotides are represented by the specially-assigned 2-letter CCD codes in parenthesis, with 'D' prefix added to their ribonucleotide counterparts. For hybrid polymer, each residue is represented by the code of its individual type. A cyclic polymer is represented in linear sequence from the chosen start to end.

A for Alanine or Adenosine-5'-monophosphate C for Cysteine or Cytidine-5'-monophosphate D for Aspartic acid E for Glutamic acid F for Phenylalanine G for Glycine or Guanosine-5'-monophosphate H for Histidine I for Isoleucine or Inosinic Acid L for Leucine K for Lysine M for Methionine N for Asparagine  or Unknown ribonucleotide O for Pyrrolysine P for Proline Q for Glutamine R for Arginine S for Serine T for Threonine U for Selenocysteine or Uridine-5'-monophosphate V for Valine W for Tryptophan Y for Tyrosine (DA) for 2'-deoxyadenosine-5'-monophosphate (DC) for 2'-deoxycytidine-5'-monophosphate (DG) for 2'-deoxyguanosine-5'-monophosphate (DT) for Thymidine-5'-monophosphate (MSE) for Selenomethionine (SEP) for Phosphoserine (TPO) for Phosphothreonine (PTR) for Phosphotyrosine (PCA) for Pyroglutamic acid (UNK) for Unknown amino acid (ACE) for Acetylation cap (NH2) for Amidation cap

_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_ca.name: _entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_can
_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_ca.description:
Canonical sequence of protein or nucleic acid polymer in standard one-letter codes of amino acids or nucleotides, corresponding to the sequence in _entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code. Non-standard amino acids/nucleotides are represented by the codes of their parents if parent is specified in _chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent_comp_id, or by letter 'X' if parent is not specified. Deoxynucleotides are represented by their canonical one-letter codes of A, C, G, or T.

For modifications with several parent amino acids, all corresponding parent amino acid codes will be listed (ex. chromophores).

_entity_poly.pdbx_target_identifier.name: _entity_poly.pdbx_target_identifier
_entity_poly.pdbx_target_identifier.description: For Structural Genomics entries, the sequence's target identifier registered at the TargetTrack database.

_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_sa.name: _entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_sample
_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_sa.description:
For cases in which the sample and model sequence differ this item contains the sample chemical sequence expressed as string of one-letter amino acid codes.

Modified may be include as 'X' or with their 3-letter codes in parentheses.
examples:
A  for alanine or adenine
B  for ambiguous asparagine/aspartic-acid
R  for arginine
N  for asparagine
D  for aspartic-acid
C  for cysteine or cystine or cytosine
Q  for glutamine
E  for glutamic-acid
Z  for ambiguous glutamine/glutamic acid
G  for glycine or guanine
H  for histidine
I  for isoleucine
L  for leucine
K  for lysine
M  for methionine
F  for phenylalanine
P  for proline
S  for serine
T  for threonine or thymine
W  for tryptophan
Y  for tyrosine
V  for valine
U  for uracil
O  for water
X  for other

_exptl.absorpt_correction_type.description: The absorption correction type and method. The value 'empirical' should NOT be used unless more detailed information is not available.

_exptl.absorpt_process_details.description: Description of the absorption process applied to the intensities. A literature reference should be supplied for psi-scan techniques.

_exptl.method_details.description: A description of special aspects of the experimental method.

_exptl_crystal.density_diffrn.name: _exptl_crystal.density_diffrn
_exptl_crystal.density_diffrn.description: Density values calculated from the crystal cell and contents. The units are megagrams per cubic metre (grams per cubic centimetre).

_exptl_crystal.density_Matthews.name: _exptl_crystal.density_Matthews
_exptl_crystal.density_Matthews.description:
The density of the crystal, expressed as the ratio of the volume of the asymmetric unit to the molecular mass of a monomer of the structure, in units of angstroms^3^ per dalton.

Ref: Matthews, B. W. (1968). J. Mol. Biol. 33, 491-497.

_exptl_crystal.density_percent_sol.name: _exptl_crystal.density_percent_sol
_exptl_crystal.density_percent_sol.description:
Density value P calculated from the crystal cell and contents, expressed as per cent solvent.

P = 1 - (1.23 N MMass) / V

N     = the number of molecules in the unit cell MMass = the molecular mass of each molecule (gm/mole) V     = the volume of the unit cell (A^3^) 1.23  = a conversion factor evaluated as:

(0.74 cm^3^/g) (10^24^ A^3^/cm^3^) -------------------------------------- (6.02*10^23^) molecules/mole

where 0.74 is an assumed value for the partial specific volume of the molecule

_exptl_crystal.description.name: _exptl_crystal.description
_exptl_crystal.description.description: A description of the quality and habit of the crystal. The crystal dimensions should not normally be reported here; use instead the specific items in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL category relating to size for the gross dimensions of the crystal and data items in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL_FACE category to describe the relationship between individual faces.

_exptl_crystal.F_000.name: _exptl_crystal.F_000
_exptl_crystal.F_000.description:
The effective number of electrons in the crystal unit cell contributing to F(000). This may contain dispersion contributions and is calculated as

F(000) = [ sum (f~r~^2^ + f~i~^2^) ]^1/2^

f~r~ = real part of the scattering factors at theta = 0 degree f~i~ = imaginary part of the scattering factors at theta = 0 degree

the sum is taken over each atom in the unit cell

_exptl_crystal.id.name: _exptl_crystal.id
_exptl_crystal.id.description:
The value of _exptl_crystal.id must uniquely identify a record in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_exptl_crystal.preparation.name: _exptl_crystal.preparation
_exptl_crystal.preparation.description: Details of crystal growth and preparation of the crystal (e.g. mounting) prior to the intensity measurements.
examples: mounted in an argon-filled quartz capillary

_exptl_crystal.size_max.name: _exptl_crystal.size_max
_exptl_crystal.size_max.description: The maximum dimension of the crystal. This item may appear in a list with _exptl_crystal.id if multiple crystals are used in the experiment.

_exptl_crystal.size_mid.name: _exptl_crystal.size_mid
_exptl_crystal.size_mid.description: The medial dimension of the crystal. This item may appear in a list with _exptl_crystal.id if multiple crystals are used in the experiment.

_exptl_crystal.size_min.name: _exptl_crystal.size_min
_exptl_crystal.size_min.description: The minimum dimension of the crystal. This item may appear in a list with _exptl_crystal.id if multiple crystals are used in the experiment.

_exptl_crystal.size_rad.name: _exptl_crystal.size_rad
_exptl_crystal.size_rad.description: The radius of the crystal, if the crystal is a sphere or a cylinder. This item may appear in a list with _exptl_crystal.id if multiple crystals are used in the experiment.

_exptl_crystal.colour_lustre.name: _exptl_crystal.colour_lustre
_exptl_crystal.colour_lustre.description: The enumeration list of standardized names developed for the International Centre for Diffraction Data. The colour of a crystal is given by the combination of _exptl_crystal.colour_modifier with _exptl_crystal.colour_primary, as in 'dark-green' or 'bluish-violet', if necessary combined with _exptl_crystal.colour_lustre, as in 'metallic-green'.

_exptl_crystal.colour_modifier.name: _exptl_crystal.colour_modifier
_exptl_crystal.colour_modifier.description: The enumeration list of standardized names developed for the International Centre for Diffraction Data. The colour of a crystal is given by the combination of _exptl_crystal.colour_modifier with _exptl_crystal.colour_primary, as in 'dark-green' or 'bluish-violet', if necessary combined with _exptl_crystal.colour_lustre, as in 'metallic-green'.

_exptl_crystal.colour_primary.name: _exptl_crystal.colour_primary
_exptl_crystal.colour_primary.description: The enumeration list of standardized names developed for the International Centre for Diffraction Data. The colour of a crystal is given by the combination of _exptl_crystal.colour_modifier with _exptl_crystal.colour_primary, as in 'dark-green' or 'bluish-violet', if necessary combined with _exptl_crystal.colour_lustre, as in 'metallic-green'.

_exptl_crystal.density_meas.name: _exptl_crystal.density_meas
_exptl_crystal.density_meas.description: Density values measured using standard chemical and physical methods. The units are megagrams per cubic metre (grams per cubic centimetre).

_exptl_crystal.density_meas_gt.name: _exptl_crystal.density_meas_gt
_exptl_crystal.density_meas_gt.description: The value above which the density measured using standard chemical and physical methods lies. The units are megagrams per cubic metre (grams per cubic centimetre). _exptl_crystal.density_meas_gt and _exptl_crystal.density_meas_lt should not be used to report new experimental work, for which _exptl_crystal.density_meas should be used.  These items are intended for use in reporting information in existing databases and archives which would be misleading if reported under _exptl_crystal.density_meas.

_exptl_crystal.density_meas_lt.name: _exptl_crystal.density_meas_lt
_exptl_crystal.density_meas_lt.description: The value below which the density measured using standard chemical and physical methods lies. The units are megagrams per cubic metre (grams per cubic centimetre). _exptl_crystal.density_meas_gt and _exptl_crystal.density_meas_lt should not be used to report new experimental work, for which _exptl_crystal.density_meas should be used.  These items are intended for use in reporting information in existing databases and archives which would be misleading if reported under _exptl_crystal.density_meas.

_exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp.name: _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp
_exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp.description: Temperature in kelvins at which _exptl_crystal.density_meas was determined.

_exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_gt.name: _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_gt
_exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_gt.description: Temperature in kelvins above which _exptl_crystal.density_meas was determined. _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_gt and _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_lt should not be used for reporting new work,  for which the correct temperature of measurement should be given. These items are intended for use in reporting information stored in databases or archives which would be misleading if reported under _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp.

_exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_lt.name: _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_lt
_exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_lt.description: Temperature in kelvins below which _exptl_crystal.density_meas was determined. _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_gt and _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp_lt should not be used for reporting new work,  for which the correct temperature of measurement should be given. These items are intended for use in reporting information stored in databases or archives which would be misleading if reported under _exptl_crystal.density_meas_temp.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image_url.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image_url
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image_url.description: The URL for an a file containing the image of crystal.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image_format.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image_format
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image_format.description: The image format for the file containing the image of crystal specified as an RFC2045/RFC2046 mime type.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaicity.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaicity
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaicity.description:
Isotropic approximation of the distribution of mis-orientation angles specified in degrees of all the mosaic domain blocks in the crystal, represented as a standard deviation. Here, a mosaic block is a set of contiguous unit cells assumed to be perfectly aligned. Lower mosaicity indicates better ordered crystals. See for example:

Nave, C. (1998). Acta Cryst. D54, 848-853.

Note that many software packages estimate the mosaic rotation distribution differently and may combine several physical properties of the experiment into a single mosaic term. This term will help fit the modeled spots to the observed spots without necessarily being directly related to the physics of the crystal itself.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaicity_esd.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaicity_esd
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaicity_esd.description: The uncertainty in the mosaicity estimate for the crystal.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_image.description: A code to indicate that a crystal image is available for this crystal.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_x-ray_image.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_x-ray_image
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_x-ray_image.description: A code to indicate that an x-ray image is available for this crystal.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_x-ray_image_type.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_x-ray_image_type
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_x-ray_image_type.description: A description of the type of x-ray image for this crystal.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_diffrn_limit.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_diffrn_limit
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_diffrn_limit.description: The measured diffraction limit for this crystal.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_diffrn_lifet.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_diffrn_lifetime
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_diffrn_lifet.description: The measured diffraction limit for this crystal.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_1.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_1
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_1.description: The crystal size along the first measured direction in millimeters.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_2.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_2
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_2.description: The crystal size along the second measured direction in millimeters.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_3.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_3
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_3.description: The crystal size along the third measured direction in millimeters.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_method.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_method
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_method.description:
How parameters derived from the spot shape (such as mosaic block size and rotation, beam divergence, and crossfire) and their errors were estimated. See the related items section.

This can be a written description or a citation to a specific software package that determined these parameters.

Note, these parameters are considered derived terms from the data, as opposed to measured terms from the instrument (such as diffrn_radiation.div_x_source, a term similar to pdbx_crystal_alignment.crossfire_x).

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size.description: Isotropic and resolution-independent term representing the average size of mosaic domains in the crystal specified in Angstroms.  Larger size indicates better ordered crystals.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size_es.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size_esd
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size_es.description: The uncertainty in the mosaic block size estimate for the crystal.

_exptl_crystal_face.crystal_id.name: _exptl_crystal_face.crystal_id
_exptl_crystal_face.crystal_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_chi.name: _exptl_crystal_face.diffr_chi
_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_chi.description: The chi diffractometer setting angle in degrees for a specific crystal face associated with _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_kappa.name: _exptl_crystal_face.diffr_kappa
_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_kappa.description: The kappa diffractometer setting angle in degrees for a specific crystal face associated with _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_phi.name: _exptl_crystal_face.diffr_phi
_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_phi.description: The phi diffractometer setting angle in degrees for a specific crystal face associated with _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_psi.name: _exptl_crystal_face.diffr_psi
_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_psi.description: The psi diffractometer setting angle in degrees for a specific crystal face associated with _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.

_exptl_crystal_face.index_h.name: _exptl_crystal_face.index_h
_exptl_crystal_face.index_h.description: Miller index h of the crystal face associated with the value _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.

_exptl_crystal_face.index_k.name: _exptl_crystal_face.index_k
_exptl_crystal_face.index_k.description: Miller index k of the crystal face associated with the value _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.

_exptl_crystal_face.index_l.name: _exptl_crystal_face.index_l
_exptl_crystal_face.index_l.description: Miller index l of the crystal face associated with the value _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.

_exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.name: _exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist
_exptl_crystal_face.perp_dist.description: The perpendicular distance in millimetres from the face to the centre of rotation of the crystal.

_exptl_crystal_grow.apparatus.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.apparatus
_exptl_crystal_grow.apparatus.description: The physical apparatus in which the crystal was grown.

_exptl_crystal_grow.atmosphere.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.atmosphere
_exptl_crystal_grow.atmosphere.description: The nature of the gas or gas mixture in which the crystal was grown.

_exptl_crystal_grow.crystal_id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.crystal_id
_exptl_crystal_grow.crystal_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_exptl_crystal_grow.details.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.details
_exptl_crystal_grow.details.description: A description of special aspects of the crystal growth.
examples:
Solution 2 was prepared as a well solution and
 mixed. A droplet containing 2 \ml of solution
 1 was delivered onto a cover slip; 2 \ml of
 solution 2 was added to the droplet without
 mixing.

Crystal plates were originally stored at room
 temperature for 1 week but no nucleation
 occurred. They were then transferred to 4
 degrees C, at which temperature well formed
 single crystals grew in 2 days.

The dependence on pH for successful crystal
 growth is very sharp. At pH 7.4 only showers
 of tiny crystals grew, at pH 7.5 well formed
 single crystals grew, at pH 7.6 no
 crystallization occurred at all.

_exptl_crystal_grow.method.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.method
_exptl_crystal_grow.method.description: The method used to grow the crystals.

_exptl_crystal_grow.method_ref.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.method_ref
_exptl_crystal_grow.method_ref.description: A literature reference that describes the method used to grow the crystals.

_exptl_crystal_grow.pH.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pH
_exptl_crystal_grow.pH.description: The pH at which the crystal was grown. If more than one pH was employed during the crystallization process, the final pH should be noted here and the protocol involving multiple pH values should be described in _exptl_crystal_grow.details.

_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pressure
_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure.description: The ambient pressure in kilopascals at which the crystal was grown.

_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure_esd.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pressure_esd
_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _exptl_crystal_grow.pressure.

_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.seeding
_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding.description: A description of the protocol used for seeding the crystal growth.
examples:
Microcrystals were introduced from a previous
 crystal growth experiment by transfer with a
 human hair.

_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding_ref.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.seeding_ref
_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding_ref.description: A literature reference that describes the protocol used to seed the crystal.

_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_details.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.temp_details
_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_details.description: A description of special aspects of temperature control during crystal growth.

_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_esd.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.temp_esd
_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _exptl_crystal_grow.temp.

_exptl_crystal_grow.time.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.time
_exptl_crystal_grow.time.description: The approximate time that the crystal took to grow to the size used for data collection.

_exptl_crystal_grow.pdbx_details.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pdbx_details
_exptl_crystal_grow.pdbx_details.description: Text description of crystal growth procedure.
examples: PEG 4000, potassium phosphate, magnesium chloride, cacodylate

_exptl_crystal_grow.pdbx_pH_range.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pdbx_pH_range
_exptl_crystal_grow.pdbx_pH_range.description: The range of pH values at which the crystal was grown.   Used when a point estimate of pH is not appropriate.

_exptl_crystal_grow.temp.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.temp
_exptl_crystal_grow.temp.description: The temperature in kelvins at which the crystal was grown. If more than one temperature was employed during the crystallization process, the final temperature should be noted here and the protocol  involving multiple temperatures should be described in _exptl_crystal_grow.details.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.conc.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.conc
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.conc.description: The concentration of the solution component.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.details.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.details
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.details.description: A description of any special aspects of the solution component. When the solution component is the one that contains the macromolecule, this could be the specification of the buffer in which the macromolecule was stored. When the solution component is a buffer component, this could be the methods (or formula) used to achieve a desired pH.
examples:
The protein solution was in a buffer
 containing 25 mM NaCl, 100 mM NaMES/MES
 buffer, pH 7.5, 3 mM NaAzide

in 3 mM NaAzide. Buffer components were mixed
 to produce a pH of 4.7 according to a ratio
 calculated from the pKa. The actual pH of
 solution 2 was not measured.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.crystal_id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.crystal_id
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.crystal_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id.description:
The value of _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id must uniquely identify each item in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL_GROW_COMP list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.name.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.name
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.name.description: A common name for the component of the solution.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.sol_id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.sol_id
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.sol_id.description: An identifier for the solution to which the given solution component belongs.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.volume.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.volume
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.volume.description: The volume of the solution component.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_conc_final.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_conc_final
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_conc_final.description: The concentration of this component in the final crystallization solution. This data item should include units of concentration.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_bath.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_bath
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_bath.description: The identification of the cystallization bath.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_salt.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_salt
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_salt.description: The identification of the crystallization salt.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_soak_salt.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_soak_salt
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_soak_salt.description: The identification of the crystallization soaking salt.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_soak_solv.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_soak_solv
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_soak_solv.description: The identification of the crystallization soaking solvent

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_solv.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_solv
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.pdbx_solv.description: The identification of the crystallization solvent.

_geom.entry_id.name: _geom.entry_id
_geom.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_geom.details.name: _geom.details
_geom.details.description: A description of geometry not covered by the existing data names in the GEOM categories, such as least-squares planes.

_geom_angle.atom_site_id_1.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_id_1
_geom_angle.atom_site_id_1.description:
The identifier of the first of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_alt_id_1.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_alt_id_1
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_alt_id_1.description:
An optional identifier of the first of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_alt_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_atom_id_1.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_atom_id_1
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_atom_id_1.description:
An optional identifier of the first of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_atom_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_comp_id_1.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_comp_id_1
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_comp_id_1.description:
An optional identifier of the first of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_comp_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_seq_id_1.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_seq_id_1
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_seq_id_1.description:
An optional identifier of the first of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_seq_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_asym_id_1.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_asym_id_1
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_asym_id_1.description:
An optional identifier of the first of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_asym_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_id_2.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_id_2
_geom_angle.atom_site_id_2.description:
The identifier of the second of the three atom sites that define the angle. The second atom is taken to be the apex of the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_alt_id_2.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_alt_id_2
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_alt_id_2.description:
An optional identifier of the second of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_alt_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_atom_id_2.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_atom_id_2
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_atom_id_2.description:
An optional identifier of the second of the three atom sites that define the angle.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_atom_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_geom_angle.atom_site_label_comp_id_2.name: _geom_angle.atom_site_label_comp_id_2
_geom_angle.atom_site_label_comp_id_2.description:
An optional identifier of the second of the three atom sites that define the angle.

_ndb_struct_na_base_pair.hbond_type_12.name: _ndb_struct_na_base_pair.hbond_type_12

_ndb_struct_na_base_pair.hbond_type_leon.name: _ndb_struct_na_base_pair.hbond_type_leontis_westhof
_ndb_struct_na_base_pair.hbond_type_leon.description:
Base pair classification of Leontis and Westhof.

The Leontis-Westhof classification system annotates basepairs according to the interacting edge used by each base (Watson-Crick, Hoogsteen, or Sugar), and glycosidic bond orientation (cis, trans).  For six of twelve classes, the correct Leontis-Westhof notation depends upon the order (i,j) of the identified nucleotides.

Leontis NB and Westhof E (2001) Geometric nomenclature and classification of RNA base pairs.  RNA 7:499-512. https://doi.org/10.1017/s1355838201002515

Replacement for category item _ndb_struct_na_base_pair.hbond_type_12. The older item encodes the 12 classes by a numerical index, without identifying the interacting edges used by each base in nucleotides i,j.

_pdbx_alt_groups.id.name: _pdbx_alt_groups.id
_pdbx_alt_groups.id.description: Unique identifier of this membership record (one row of a network).

_pdbx_alt_groups.alt_group_id.name: _pdbx_alt_groups.alt_group_id
_pdbx_alt_groups.alt_group_id.description: The name of the network (alternate state) this membership row belongs to. A network that spans several residues or atoms is written as several rows that share this id. This is the state identity that the altloc letter alone cannot carry across residues.

_pdbx_alt_groups.auth_asym_id.name: _pdbx_alt_groups.auth_asym_id
_pdbx_alt_groups.auth_asym_id.description: The chain of the selected atoms. This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_asym_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_alt_groups.auth_seq_id_start.name: _pdbx_alt_groups.auth_seq_id_start
_pdbx_alt_groups.auth_seq_id_start.description: First author residue number (auth_seq_id) of this membership row's range, inclusive. For a single residue, start and end are equal.

_pdbx_alt_groups.auth_seq_id_end.name: _pdbx_alt_groups.auth_seq_id_end
_pdbx_alt_groups.auth_seq_id_end.description: Last author residue number (auth_seq_id) of this membership row's range, inclusive.

_pdbx_alt_groups.label_alt_id.name: _pdbx_alt_groups.label_alt_id
_pdbx_alt_groups.label_alt_id.description: The alternate-location letter the selected atoms carry. This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_alt_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_alt_groups.label_atom_id.name: _pdbx_alt_groups.label_atom_id
_pdbx_alt_groups.label_atom_id.description: Optional atom-level selector. A value of '.' selects every atom in the residue range; otherwise it names a single atom (e.g. OD1), and one row is written per atom. This is the escape hatch that gives the network table atom resolution -- exactly as expressive as a per-atom state label -- without changing ATOM_SITE. This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_atom_id.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geometry_generic
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.description: This data item translates the geometry output from a software program (for example, FindGeo or MetalCoord) into a unified naming scheme. As software programs sometimes have different names for the same geometry (for instance, 'tetrahedral' and 'tetrahedron' correspond to the same geometry), this data item provides a name for the geometry that is consistent regardless of the provenance to enhance findability. The geometry derived from observed geometries in atomic coordinate files.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.description: This data item lists the abbreviated name of the geometry, as specified in the _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.descriptor. For instance, square planar is abbreviated to SPL. The geometry derived from observed geometries in atomic coordinate files.

_pdbx_chem_comp_depositor_info.formula.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_depositor_info.formula
_pdbx_chem_comp_depositor_info.formula.description:
The formula for the chemical component. Formulae are written according to the following rules:

(1) Only recognized element symbols may be used.

(2) Each element symbol is followed by a 'count' number. A count of '1' may be omitted.

(3) A space or parenthesis must separate each cluster of (element symbol + count), but in general parentheses are not used.

(4) The order of elements depends on whether carbon is present or not. If carbon is present, the order should be: C, then H, then the other elements in alphabetical order of their symbol. If carbon is not present, the elements are listed purely in alphabetic order of their symbol. This is the 'Hill' system used by Chemical Abstracts.

_pdbx_chem_comp_instance_depositor_info..description:
The observed formula for the chemical component in the deposited coordinates. Formulae are written according to the following rules:

(1) Only recognized element symbols may be used.

(2) Each element symbol is followed by a 'count' number. A count of '1' may be omitted.

(3) A space or parenthesis must separate each cluster of (element symbol + count), but in general parentheses are not used.

(4) The order of elements depends on whether carbon is present or not. If carbon is present, the order should be: C, then H, then the other elements in alphabetical order of their symbol. If carbon is not present, the elements are listed purely in alphabetic order of their symbol. This is the 'Hill' system used by Chemical Abstracts.

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id.description: Chemical component identifier for the CCD that contains the modification group.

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id.description: Chemical component identifier for the amino acid residue that is being modified.

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.category.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.category
enums: Canonical amino acid linked to a peptide residue
A diverse range of chemical compounds that form covalent bonds with amino acids. Not covered by any of the other modification categories
Chemical modifications which covalently link two amino-acid residues. Excludes reaction intermediates and modifications covered by other modification categories

All protein modifications that are well defined into known types, have limited diversity, and are not explicitly covered by any other category. It includes PTMs, PCMs, rare amino acids and unnatural amino acids

A diverse range of non-standard polypeptide residues that are not considered to be 'Named protein modifications'. These cannot be practically split into two CCDs, which describe the standard polypeptide residue and the modification group, and are not covered by any of the other modification categories

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.position.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.position
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.position.description: The position of the modification on the amino acid.
enums: Protein modification occurs on the amino acid backbone
Protein modification occurs on the amino acid side chain
Protein modification occurs on both the amino acid side chain and backbone

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.polypeptide_position.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.polypeptide_position
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.polypeptide_position.description: The position of the modification on the polypeptide.
enums: Protein modification can occur in any position of the polypeptide sequence
Protein modification can only occur on C-terminus
Protein modification can only occur on N-terminus

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id_linking_atom.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id_linking_atom
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id_linking_atom.description: The atom on the modification group that covalently links the modification to the residue that is being modified. This is only added when the protein modification is linked and so the amino acid group and the modification group are described by separate CCDs.

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id_.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id_linking_atom
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id_.description: The atom on the polypeptide residue group that covalently links the modification to the residue that is being modified. This is only added when the protein modification is linked and so the amino acid group and the modification group are described by separate CCDs.

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.uniprot_specific_ptm.description: The UniProt PTM accession code that is an exact match for the protein modification.

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.uniprot_generic_ptm_.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.uniprot_generic_ptm_accession
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.uniprot_generic_ptm_.description: The UniProt PTM accession code that describes the group of PTMs of which this protein modification is a member.

_pdbx_construct.entry_id.name: _pdbx_construct.entry_id
_pdbx_construct.entry_id.description: The value of _pdbx_construct.entry_id uniquely identifies a sample consisting of one or more proteins whose structure is to be determined. This is a pointer to _entry.id. This item may be a site dependent bar code.

_pdbx_construct.id.name: _pdbx_construct.id
_pdbx_construct.id.description: The value of _pdbx_construct.id must uniquely identify a record in the PDBX_CONSTRUCT list and should be arranged so that it is composed of a site-speicific prefix combined with a value that is unique within a given site.Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_pdbx_construct.name.name: _pdbx_construct.name
_pdbx_construct.name.description: _pdbx_construct.name  provides a placeholder for the local name of the construct, for example the plasmid name if this category is used to list plasmids.

_pdbx_construct.organisation.name: _pdbx_construct.organisation
_pdbx_construct.organisation.description: _pdbx_construct.organisation describes the organisation in which the _pdbx_construct.id is unique. This will normally be the lab in which the constrcut originated. It is envisaged that this item will permit a globally unique identifier to be constructed in cases where this is not possible from the _pdbx_construct.id alone.

_pdbx_construct.entity_id.name: _pdbx_construct.entity_id
_pdbx_construct.entity_id.description: In cases where the construct IS found in the co-ordinates then this item provides a  pointer to _entity.id in the ENTITY category for the corresponding molecule.

_pdbx_construct.robot_id.name: _pdbx_construct.robot_id
_pdbx_construct.robot_id.description: In cases where the sequence has been determined by a robot this data item provides a pointer to pdbx_robot_system.id in the PDBX_ROBOT_SYSTEM category for the robot responsible

_pdbx_construct.date.name: _pdbx_construct.date
_pdbx_construct.date.description: The date that the sequence was determined.

_pdbx_construct.details.name: _pdbx_construct.details
_pdbx_construct.details.description: Additional details about the construct that cannot be represented in the category _pdbx_construct_feature.

_pdbx_construct.class.name: _pdbx_construct.class
_pdbx_construct.class.description: The primary function of the construct. This should be considered as a guideline only.

_pdbx_construct.type.name: _pdbx_construct.type
_pdbx_construct.type.description: The type of nucleic acid sequence in the construct. Note that to find all the DNA molecules it is necessary to search for DNA + cDNA and for RNA, RNA + mRNA + tRNA.

_pdbx_construct.seq.name: _pdbx_construct.seq
_pdbx_construct.seq.description: sequence expressed as string of one-letter base codes or one letter amino acid codes. Unusual residues may be represented either using the appropriate one letter code wild cards or by the three letter code in parentheses.
examples:
gatgctgtag gcataggctt ggttatgccg gtactgccgg gcctcttgcg ggatatcgtc
 gctcaaggcg cactcccgtt ctggataatg ttttttgcgc cgacatcata acggttctgg
 caaatattct gaaatgagct gttgacaatt aatcatcgat aagcttcttg

_pdbx_construct_feature.id.name: _pdbx_construct_feature.id
_pdbx_construct_feature.id.description: The value of _pdbx_construct_feature.id must uniquely identify a record in the PDBX_CONSTRUCT_FEATURE list. Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_pdbx_construct_feature.construct_id.name: _pdbx_construct_feature.construct_id
_pdbx_construct_feature.construct_id.description: The value of _pdbx_construct_feature.construct_id uniquely identifies the construct with which the feature is associated. This is a pointer to _pdbx_construct.id This item may be a site dependent bar code.

_pdbx_construct_feature.entry_id.name: _pdbx_construct_feature.entry_id
_pdbx_construct_feature.entry_id.description: The value of _pdbx_construct_feature.entry_id uniquely identifies a sample consisting of one or more proteins whose structure is to be determined. This is a pointer to _entry.id. This item may be a site dependent bar code.

_pdbx_construct_feature.start_seq.name: _pdbx_construct_feature.start_seq
_pdbx_construct_feature.start_seq.description: The sequence position at which the feature begins

_pdbx_construct_feature.end_seq.name: _pdbx_construct_feature.end_seq
_pdbx_construct_feature.end_seq.description: The sequence position at which the feature ends

_pdbx_contact_author.address_3.name: _pdbx_contact_author.address_3

The recommended style includes the international dialing prefix, the area code in parentheses, followed by the local number with no spaces.

_pdbx_contact_author.name_first.name: _pdbx_contact_author.name_first

_pdbx_contact_author.name_last.name: _pdbx_contact_author.name_last

_pdbx_contact_author.name_salutation.name: _pdbx_contact_author.name_salutation

_pdbx_contact_author.country.name: _pdbx_contact_author.country

_pdbx_contact_author.continent.name: _pdbx_contact_author.continent
_pdbx_contact_author.continent.description: The continent of the author of the data block to whom correspondence should be addressed.

_pdbx_contact_author.phone.name: _pdbx_contact_author.phone

The telephone number of the author of the data block to whom correspondence should be addressed.

The recommended style includes the international dialing prefix, the area code in parentheses, followed by the local number and any extension number prefixed by 'x', with no spaces. The earlier convention of including the international dialing prefixes in parentheses is no longer recommended.

_pdbx_database_status.dep_release_code_c.name: _pdbx_database_status.dep_release_code_coordinates
_pdbx_database_status.dep_release_code_c.description: The deposited coordinates for this deposition will be released according the value of this item.
enums: Hold until primary citation is published
examples:
RELEASE NOW          =    Release immediately
HOLD FOR PUBLICATION =    Hold until the primary citation is published
HOLD FOR 4 WEEKS     =    Hold for 4 weeks
HOLD FOR 6 WEEKS     =    Hold for 6 weeks
HOLD FOR 8 WEEKS     =    Hold for 8 weeks
HOLD FOR 6 MONTHS    =    Hold for 6 months
HOLD FOR 1 YEAR      =    Hold for 1 year

_pdbx_database_status.dep_release_code_s.name: _pdbx_database_status.dep_release_code_sequence
_pdbx_database_status.dep_release_code_s.description: The sequence information for this deposition will be released according the value of this item.  Setting this status code to "RELEASE NOW" indicates that the macromolecular sequence(s) for this entry may be displayed in PDB status reports prior to the release of the entry.   Setting this status code to "HOLD FOR RELEASE" conceals the sequence information in PDB status reports until the coordinate data for this entry are released.
enums: Conceal sequence information in status reports until coordinate data is released
Release sequence information in status reports immediately
examples:
RELEASE NOW =      Release sequence information in status reports immediately
HOLD FOR RELEASE = Conceal sequence information in status reports until
                   coordinate data is release

_pdbx_database_status.dep_release_code_s.description: The deposited structure factors for this deposition will be released according the value of this item.
enums: Hold until primary citation is published
examples:

HOLD FOR PUBLICATION =    Hold until the primary citation is published
HOLD FOR 4 WEEKS     =    Hold for 4 weeks
HOLD FOR 6 WEEKS     =    Hold for 6 weeks
HOLD FOR 8 WEEKS     =    Hold for 8 weeks
HOLD FOR 6 MONTHS    =    Hold for 6 months
HOLD FOR 1 YEAR      =    Hold for 1 year

_pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_diff.description: The difference of the <I/SigmaI> in the last two points of the plot of <I/SigmaI> vs resolution (expected to be negative).

_pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_mean.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_mean

_pdbx_dcc_density.B_solvent.description: One of the scale factors used in the bulk solvent correction (from REFMAC).

_pdbx_dcc_density.K_solvent.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.K_solvent
_pdbx_dcc_density.K_solvent.description: One of the scale factors used in the bulk solvent correction (from REFMAC).

_pdbx_dcc_density.partial_B_value_correc.description: Whether attempt to convert the partial to full B factors before validation.

_pdbx_dcc_density.partial_B_value_correc.description: Whether the conversion of partial B to full B factor is successful (Y) or not (N).

_pdbx_dcc_density.reflection_status_arch.description: Whether the status is of reflection is archived (Y) or not (N).

_pdbx_dcc_density.reflection_status_used.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.reflection_status_used
_pdbx_dcc_density.reflection_status_used.description: Whether the status of the reflection is used (Y) or not (N).

_pdbx_dcc_density.iso_B_value_type.description: The type of B factors (partial or full) for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_density.reflns_twin.description: Boolean (Y/N) whether the twin is reported.

_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_by_xtriage.description: Boolean (Y/N) whether twin is detected by Xtriage.

_pdbx_dcc_density.Cruickshank_dpi_xyz.description: The DPI on the model determined by REFMAC.

_pdbx_dcc_density.correlation_overall.description: The overall electron density correlation coefficient.

_pdbx_dcc_density.real_space_R_overall.description: The overall real space R factor.

_pdbx_dcc_density.error.description: Details of any error or warning messages.

_pdbx_dcc_density_corr.ordinal.description: The ordered number in the output list.

_pdbx_dcc_density_corr.ls_R_factor_R_fre.description: The calculated Rfree using the test set data.

_pdbx_dcc_density_corr.correlation_coeff.description: The correlations between the observed and calculated structure factors.

_pdbx_dcc_density_corr.real_space_R.description: The overall real space R factor.

_pdbx_dcc_density_corr.correlation.description: The overall real space density correlation coefficient.

_pdbx_dcc_density_corr.details.description: Any additional details of the calculations.

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.Ramachandran_outlier_.description: Percentage of outliers in Ramachandran plot.

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.Ramachandran_favored_.description: Number of favored residues in Ramachandran plot.

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.all_atom_clashscore.description: All atom score is determined by steric overlaps >0.4A per 1000 atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.overall_score.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.overall_score
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.overall_score.description: The overall score for the geometry (the smaller the better).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_overall_rms.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_overall_rms
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_overall_rms.description: The overall root mean square deviation for bond lengths (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_overall_max.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_overall_max
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_overall_max.description: The maximum root mean square deviation for bond lengths (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_ligand_rms.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_ligand_rms
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_ligand_rms.description: The overall root mean square deviation for bond lengths (ligand only).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_ligand_max.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_ligand_max
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.bond_ligand_max.description: The maximum root mean square deviation for bond lengths (ligand only).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_overall_rms.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_overall_rms
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_overall_rms.description: The overall root mean square deviation for bond angles (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_overall_max.description: The maximum root mean square deviation for bond angles (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_ligand_rms.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_ligand_rms
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_ligand_rms.description: The overall root mean square deviation for bond angles (ligand only).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_ligand_max.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_ligand_max
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.angle_ligand_max.description: The maximum root mean square deviation for bond angles (ligand only).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.dihedral_overall_rms.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.dihedral_overall_rms
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.dihedral_overall_rms.description: The overall root mean square deviation for dihedrals (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.dihedral_overall_max.description: The maximum root mean square deviation for dihedrals (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.chirality_overall_rms.description: The overall root mean square deviation for chirality (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.chirality_overall_max.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.chirality_overall_max
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.chirality_overall_max.description: The maximum root mean square deviation for chirality (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.planarity_overall_rms.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.planarity_overall_rms
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.planarity_overall_rms.description: The overall root mean square deviation for planarity (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_geometry.planarity_overall_max.name: _pdbx_dcc_geometry.planarity_overall_max
_pdbx_dcc_geometry.planarity_overall_max.description: The maximum root mean square deviation for planarity (all atoms).

_pdbx_dcc_map.id.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.id
_pdbx_dcc_map.id.description: The ordered number of residue in the output list.

_pdbx_dcc_map.model_id.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.model_id
_pdbx_dcc_map.model_id.description: The component model identifier for this analysis.

_pdbx_dcc_map.auth_comp_id.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.auth_comp_id
_pdbx_dcc_map.auth_comp_id.description: The author provided compound ID (residue name).

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC.description: The Real Space electron density Correlation Coefficient for the residue. It is defined as RSCC=(<xy>-<x><y>)/[sqrt(<x**2>-<x>**2)*sqrt(<y**2>-<y>**2)] where x is the observed density from the (2mFo-DFc) map and y is the calculated density from the Fc map

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSR
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR.description: The Real space Rfactor (RSR) for the residue. It is defined as RSR=sum(|x-y|/x+y|) which is the summation over all grid points around the residue, where x is the observed density from the (2mFo-DFc) map and y is the calculated density from the Fc map.

_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSR.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSR
_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSR.description: The weighted Real Space Rfactor (defined as RSR/RSCC) for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ.description: Zscore of the Real Space Rfactor (RSRZ) for the residue. It is defined as (RSR-<RSR>)/sigma_RSR, where <RSR> and sigma_RSR are for the whole map, RSR is for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSRZ.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSRZ
_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSRZ.description: Zscore of weighted Real Space Rfactor for the residue. It is defined as (wRSR-<wRSR>)/sigma_wRSR,  where <wRSR> and sigma_wRSR are for the whole map, wRSR is for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean
_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean.description: The occupancy weighted average isotropic B factors for the residue. It is defined as (SUM B*Q)/(SUM Q), where B is the isotropic B factor for each atom and Q is the occupancy.

_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean
_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean.description: The averaged occupancy for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_main_chain.description: The Real Space electron density Correlation Coefficient for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_main_chain.description: The Real space Rfactor (RSR) for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_main_chain.description: The weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_main_chain.description: Zscore of the Real Space Rfactor (RSRZ) for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_main_chain.description: Zscore of weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_main_chain.description: The occupancy weighted average isotropic B factors for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_main_chain.description: The averaged occupancy forthe main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_side_chain.description: The Real Space electron density Correlation Coefficient for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_side_chain.description: The Real space Rfactor (RSR) for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_side_chain.description: The weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_side_chain.description: Zscore of the Real Space Rfactor (RSRZ) for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_side_chain.description: Zscore of weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_side_chain.description: The occupancy weighted average isotropic B factors for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_side_chain.description: The averaged occupancy forthe side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_phosphate_group.description: The Real Space electron density Correlation Coefficient for the phosphate atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_phosphate_group.description: The Real space Rfactor (RSR) for the phosphate atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_phosphate_group.description: The weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the phosphate atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_phosphate_group.description: Zscore of the Real Space Rfactor (RSRZ) for the phosphate atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_phosphate_group.description: Zscore of weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the phosphate atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_phosphate_group.description: The occupancy weighted average isotropic B factors for the phosphate atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_phosphate_g.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_phosphate_g.description: The averaged occupancy forthe phosphate atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.shift.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.shift
_pdbx_dcc_map.shift.description: Defined in sfcheck (displacement/sigma_of_displacement). Displacement of atoms from electron density is estimated from the difference (Fobs - Fcal) map. The displacement vector is the ratio of the gradient of difference density to the curvature. The amplitude of the displacement vector is an indicator of the positional error.

_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.shift_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_main_chain.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.density_shift, but it is for main chain.

_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.shift_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_side_chain.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.density_shift, but it is for side chain.

_pdbx_dcc_map.density_connectivity.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.density_connectivity
_pdbx_dcc_map.density_connectivity.description: Defined in sfcheck. It is the product of the (2Fobs-Fcal) electron density values for the backbone atoms N, CA and C, and in the case of RNA/DNA, for P, O5', C5', C3', O3'. Low values of this index indicate breaks in the backbone electron density which may be due to flexibility of the chain or incorrect tracing.

_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_main_chain.description: Defined in sfcheck. It is the mean density of the main chain atoms divided by the mean density of the map.

_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_side_chain.description: Defined in sfcheck. It is the mean density of the side chain atoms divided by the mean density of the map.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD.description: The real space difference density Z score (defined as Delta_rho/sigma(Delta_rho)) from Tickle (2012). It is related to the model accuracy. RSZD>3, significant extra positive density around the residue; RSZD<-3, significant extra negative density around the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO.description: The real space observed density Z score (defined as <rho_obs>/sigma(Delta_rho)) from Tickle (2012). It is related to the model precision (B factors). Small or large value means weak or strong density for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_Zscore.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_Zscore
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_Zscore.description: The Zscore of RSZO (or the Zscore of <rho_obs>/sigma(Delta_rho)).

_pdbx_dcc_map.LLDF.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.LLDF
_pdbx_dcc_map.LLDF.description: It is the Ligand Local Density Function defined as the (<RsR>_local - RsR_Ligand)/sigma_RsR, where <RsR>_local is the mean RSR of polymer residues in 5 angstroms (including the crystallographic symmetry) surrounding the ligand, and sigma_RsR is sigma value calculated from the surrounding residues.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_main_chain.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD, use for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_main_chain.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO, use for the main chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_side_chain.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD, use for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_side_chain.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO, use for the side chain atoms.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_phosphate_group.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD, use for the phosphate atoms of nucleic acids.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_phosphate_group.description: The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO, use for the phosphate atoms of nucleic acids.

_pdbx_dcc_map.quality_indicator.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.quality_indicator
_pdbx_dcc_map.quality_indicator.description: The value indicates any problems with this group of atoms. If given as 'd', it means there is problem of local model accuracy. If given as 'w', it means weak density (there is problem of local model precision). If given as 'dw', both then problems exist.

It is envisaged that this should be the same as and product code associated with the sample and would provide the key by which information about the production process may be extracted from the protein production facility. For files describing the protein production process (i.e. where _entity.type is 'P' or 'E') this should have the same value as _entry.id

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_device_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_device_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_device_id.description: This data item is a pointer to pdbx_robot_system.id in the PDBX_ROBOT_SYSTEM category.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_details.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_details
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_details.description: Details of the protein concentration procedure

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_assay_met.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_assay_method
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.conc_assay_met.description: The method used to measure the protein concentration

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_concen.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_concentration
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_concen.description: The final concentration of the protein.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_yield.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_yield
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_yield.description: The yield of protein in milligrams.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_oligom.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_oligomeric_state
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.protein_oligom.description: The oligomeric state of the protein. Monomeric is 1, dimeric 2, etc.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.storage_buffer.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.storage_buffer_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.storage_buffer.description: This item is a pointer to pdbx_buffer.id in the PDBX_BUFFER category. The referenced buffer is that in which the protein was stored.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.storage_temper.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.storage_temperature
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.storage_temper.description: The temperature in degrees celsius at which the protein was stored.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.summary.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.summary
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_pure.summary.description: Summary of the details of protein purification method used to obtain the final protein product.  This description should include any lysis, fractionation, proteolysis, refolding, chromatography used as well as the method used the characterize the final product.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entry_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entry_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entry_id.description: The value of _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entry_id uniquely identifies a sample consisting of one or more proteins whose structure is to be determined. This is a pointer to _entry.id. This item may be a site dependent bar code.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entity_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entity_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entity_id.description: The value of _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.entity_id uniquely identifies each protein contained in the project target protein complex whose structure is to be determined. This data item is a pointer to _entity.id in the ENTITY category. This item may be a site dependent bar code.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.step_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.step_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.step_id.description: This item is the unique identifier for this refolding step.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.next_step_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.next_step_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.next_step_id.description: This item unique identifier for the next production step. This allows a workflow to have multiple entry points leading to a single product.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.end_construc.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.end_construct_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.end_construc.description: This item is a pointer to pdbx_construct.id in the PDBX_CONSTRUCT category. The referenced sequence is expected to be the amino acid sequence of the expressed product after the refolding step.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.robot_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.robot_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.robot_id.description: This data item is a pointer to pdbx_robot_system.id in the PDBX_ROBOT_SYSTEM category.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.date.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.date
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.date.description: The date of this production step.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.denature_buf.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.denature_buffer_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.denature_buf.description: This item is a pointer to pdbx_buffer.id in the PDBX_BUFFER category. The referenced buffer is that in which the protein was denatured.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.refold_buffe.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.refold_buffer_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.refold_buffe.description: This item is a pointer to pdbx_buffer.id in the PDBX_BUFFER category. The referenced buffer is that in which the protein was refolded.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.temperature.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.temperature
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.temperature.description: The temperature in degrees celsius at which the protein was refolded.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.time.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.time
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.time.description: The time in hours over which the protein was refolded.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.storage_buff.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.storage_buffer_id
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.storage_buff.description: This item is a pointer to pdbx_buffer.id in the PDBX_BUFFER category. The referenced buffer is that in which the refolded protein was stored.

_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.details.name: _pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.details
_pdbx_entity_src_gen_refold.details.description: String value containing details of the refolding.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.details.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.details
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.details.description: A description of special aspects of the source for the synthetic entity.
examples: This sequence occurs naturally in humans.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.organism_scientific.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.organism_scientific
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.organism_scientific.description: The scientific name of the organism from which the sequence of the synthetic entity was derived.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.organism_common_nam.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.organism_common_name
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.organism_common_nam.description: The common name of the organism from which the sequence of the synthetic entity was derived.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.strain.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.strain
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.strain.description: The strain of the organism from which the sequence of the synthetic entity was derived.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.ncbi_taxonomy_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.ncbi_taxonomy_id
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.ncbi_taxonomy_id.description:
NCBI Taxonomy identifier of the organism from which the sequence of the synthetic entity was derived.

Reference:

Wheeler DL, Chappey C, Lash AE, Leipe DD, Madden TL, Schuler GD, Tatusova TA, Rapp BA (2000). Database resources of the National Center for Biotechnology Information. Nucleic Acids Res 2000 Jan 1;28(1):10-4

Benson DA, Karsch-Mizrachi I, Lipman DJ, Ostell J, Rapp BA, Wheeler DL (2000). GenBank. Nucleic Acids Res 2000 Jan 1;28(1):15-18.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.entity_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.entity_id
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.entity_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entity.id in the ENTITY category.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_src_id.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_src_id
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_src_id.description: This data item is an ordinal identifier for pdbx_entity_src_syn data records.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_alt_source_fla.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_alt_source_flag
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_alt_source_fla.description: This data item identifies cases in which an alternative source modeled.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_beg_seq_num.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_beg_seq_num
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_beg_seq_num.description:
The beginning polymer sequence position for the polymer section corresponding to this source.

A reference to the sequence position in the entity_poly category.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_end_seq_num.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_end_seq_num
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_end_seq_num.description:
The ending polymer sequence position for the polymer section corresponding to this source.

A reference to the sequence position in the entity_poly category.

_pdbx_entry_details.entry_id.description: This item identifies the entry.  This is a reference to _entry.id.

_pdbx_entry_details.nonpolymer_details.description: Additional details about the non-polymer components in this entry.

_pdbx_entry_details.sequence_details.description: Additional details about the sequence or sequence database correspondences for this entry.

_pdbx_entry_details.compound_details.description: Additional details about the macromolecular compounds in this entry.

_pdbx_entry_details.source_details.description: Additional details about the source and taxonomy of the macromolecular components in this entry.

_pdbx_entry_details.has_ligand_of_intere.description: A flag to indicate if author has indicated that there are any or no ligands that are the focus of research.

_pdbx_entry_details.has_protein_modifica.description: A flag to indicate if the model contains any protein modifications.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_cryo_treatment.cryst.description: This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_cryo_treatment.final.description: Details of the final solution used in the treatment of this crystal

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_cryo_treatment.soaki.description: Details of the soaking treatment applied to this crystal.
examples:
A series of 1 min soaks beginning at 5% (v/v) glycerol in precipitant,
 transiting to 20% (v/v) glycerol, and finally to 25% glycerol

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_cryo_treatment.cooli.description: Details of the cooling treatment applied to this crystal.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_cryo_treatment.annea.description: Details of the annealing treatment applied to this crystal.
examples:
10 sec interruption of cold stream with plastic ruler.
 Performed twice.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_cryo_treatment.cryog.description: The name of cryogen used for vitrification

The value of _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.comp_id must uniquely identify each item in the PDBX_EXPTL_CRYSTAL_GROW_COMP list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.comp_name.description: A common name for the component of the solution.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.sol_id.description: An identifier for the solution to which the given solution component belongs.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.conc.description: The concentration value of the solution component.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_grow_sol.volume_unit.description: The volume units of the solution.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_process.microcrystal.description: The instrument used to produce microcrystals for the experiments, if applicable.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_process.microcrystal.description: The minimal dimension of the final crystal ready for diffraction experiments. For example, for microcrystals processed by FIB milling, this item refers to the thickness of the thin lamella.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_process.microcrystal.description: The maximal dimension of the final crystal ready for diffraction experiments. For example, for microcrystals processed by FIB milling, this item refers to the length of the thin lamella.

_pdbx_exptl_crystal_process.microcrystal.name: _pdbx_exptl_crystal_process.microcrystal_description
_pdbx_exptl_crystal_process.microcrystal.description: Characteristics of the final crystal ready for diffraction experiments, e.g. shape.

spray dried in water with 1% clay

_pdbx_modification_feature.category.name: _pdbx_modification_feature.category
enums: Canonical amino acid linked to a peptide residue
A diverse range of chemical compounds that form covalent bonds with amino acids. Not covered by any of the other modification categories
Chemical modifications which covalently link two amino-acid residues. Excludes reaction intermediates and modifications covered by other modification categories

All protein modifications that are well defined into known types, have limited diversity, and are not explicitly covered by any other category. It includes PTMs, PCMs, rare amino acids and unnatural amino acids
Non-standard covalent chemical linkages directly between two polypeptide residues

A diverse range of non-standard polypeptide residues that are not considered to be 'Named protein modifications'. These cannot be practically split into two CCDs, which describe the standard polypeptide residue and the modification group, and are not covered by any of the other modification categories

insert at center of experimental sample tube
insert at outer edge of a separate sample tube
