_pdbx_nmr_refine.entry_id.name: _pdbx_nmr_refine.entry_id
_pdbx_nmr_refine.entry_id.description: You can leave this blank as an ID will be assigned by the RCSB to the constraint file.

_pdbx_nmr_refine.method.name: _pdbx_nmr_refine.method
_pdbx_nmr_refine.method.description: The method used to determine the structure.
examples:
distance geometry
simulated annealing
molecular dynamics
matrix relaxation
torsion angle dynamics

_pdbx_nmr_refine.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_refine.details
_pdbx_nmr_refine.details.description: Additional details about the NMR refinement.
examples:
Additional comments about the NMR refinement can be placed here, e.g.
the structures are based on a total of 3344 restraints, 3167 are NOE-derived
distance constraints, 68 dihedral angle restraints,109 distance restraints
from hydrogen bonds.

_pdbx_nmr_representative.conformer_id.name: _pdbx_nmr_representative.conformer_id
_pdbx_nmr_representative.conformer_id.description: If a member of the ensemble has been selected as a representative structure, identify it by its model number.

_pdbx_nmr_representative.selection_crite.name: _pdbx_nmr_representative.selection_criteria
_pdbx_nmr_representative.selection_crite.description: By highlighting the appropriate choice(s), describe the criteria used to select this structure as a representative structure, or if an average structure has been calculated describe how this was done.
examples:
The structure closest to the average.
The structure with the lowest energy was selected.
The structure with the fewest number of violations was selected.
A minimized average structure was calculated.

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.contents.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.contents
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.contents.description:
A complete description of each NMR sample. Include the concentration and concentration units for each component (include buffers, etc.). For each component describe the isotopic composition, including the % labeling level, if known.

For example: 1. Uniform (random) labeling with 15N: U-15N 2. Uniform (random) labeling with 13C, 15N at known labeling levels: U-95% 13C;U-98% 15N 3. Residue selective labeling: U-95% 15N-Thymine 4. Site specific labeling: 95% 13C-Ala18, 5. Natural abundance labeling in an otherwise uniformly labeled biomolecule is designated by NA: U-13C; NA-K,H
examples: 2mM Ribonuclease  U-15N,13C; 50mM phosphate buffer NA; 90% H2O, 10% D2O

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.solvent_system.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.solvent_system
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.solvent_system.description: The solvent system used for this sample.

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.label.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.label
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.label.description: A value that uniquely identifies this sample from the other samples listed in the entry.

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.type.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.type
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.type.description: A descriptive term for the sample that defines the general physical properties of the sample.
enums: gel samples used for solid-state NMR
gel samples used for solution NMR

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.details
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.details.description: Brief description of the sample providing additional information not captured by other items in the category.
examples: The added glycerol was used to raise the viscosity of the solution to 1.05 poisson.

_pdbx_nmr_software.ordinal.name: _pdbx_nmr_software.ordinal
_pdbx_nmr_software.ordinal.description: An ordinal index for this category

_pdbx_nmr_software.name.name: _pdbx_nmr_software.name
_pdbx_nmr_software.name.description: The name of the software used for the task.

_pdbx_nmr_software.authors.name: _pdbx_nmr_software.authors
_pdbx_nmr_software.authors.description: The name of the authors of the software used in this procedure.

_pdbx_nmr_software_task.task.name: _pdbx_nmr_software_task.task
_pdbx_nmr_software_task.task.description: A word or brief phrase that describes the task that a software application was used to carry out.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id.description: An integer value that specifies the dimension of a multidimensional NMR spectrum.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_type.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_type
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_type.description: The value for this tag is a standard IUPAC abbreviation for an element (i.e., H, C, N, P, etc).

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_isotope_numb.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_isotope_number
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_isotope_numb.description: The mass number for the specified atom.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.spectral_region.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.spectral_region
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.spectral_region.description: A code defining the type of nuclei that would be expected to be observed in the spectral region observed in the dimension of the spectrum.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.magnetization_lin.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.magnetization_linkage_id
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.magnetization_lin.description: The magnetization linkage ID is used to designate dimensions of a multidimensional NMR experiment where the nuclei observed in the dimensions are directly linked by a one bond scalar coupling. For example, the amide proton and amide nitrogen in an 1H-15N HSQC experiment.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width.description: The width of the spectral window observed in Hz.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoding_code.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoding_code
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoding_code.description: Code describing how information from one spectral dimension has been encoded in another dimension in for example a reduced dimensionality experiment.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoded_source_di.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoded_source_dimension_id
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoded_source_di.description: Pointer to '_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id'. The spectral dimension in a reduced dimensionality experiment that is the source of the magnetization that has been encoded.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width_units.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width_units
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width_units.description: The units for the sweep width value (Hz or ppm)

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.center_frequency_.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.center_frequency_offset
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.center_frequency_.description: The center frequency offset for the spectral dimension specified.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.under_sampling_ty.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.under_sampling_type
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.under_sampling_ty.description: The type of spectral folding/aliasing that was used or occurred when the spectrum was collected.
enums: Under sampling that results in observed peaks that have circular chemical shift offsets
Under sampling that results in observed peaks that have chemical shift offsets that are folded relative to the left or right edge of the spectral window
Neither aliased nor folded peaks were observed

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.id.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.id
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.id.description: Unique identifier for a spectral peak list in an entry.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.data_file_n.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.data_file_name
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.data_file_n.description: The name of the file submitted with a deposition that contains the quantitative data for a spectral peak list.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.number_of_s.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.number_of_spectral_dimensions
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.number_of_s.description: Number of dimension in the spectrum from which the peak list was extracted.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.details
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.details.description: Text describing the reported list of spectral peaks.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.text_data_f.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.text_data_format
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.text_data_f.description: The data format used to represent the spectral peak data as ASCII text in the text block that is the value to the '_Spectral_peak_list.Text_data' tag.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.label.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.label
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.label.description: A descriptive label that uniquely identifies a list of reported spectral peaks.

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.spectrometer_id.description: Assign a numerical ID to each instrument.

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.model.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.model
_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.model.description: The model of the NMR spectrometer.

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.type.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.type
_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.type.description: Select the instrument manufacturer(s) and the model(s) of the NMR(s) used for this work.

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.manufacturer.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.manufacturer
_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.manufacturer.description: The name of the manufacturer of the spectrometer.

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.field_strength.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.field_strength
_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.field_strength.description: The field strength in MHz of the spectrometer

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.details
_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.details.description: A text description of the NMR spectrometer.

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.name.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.name
_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.name.description: A label that uniquely identifies the NMR spectrometer from other spectrometers listed in the entry.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.t.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.type
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.t.description: The kind of chemical shift offset that should be applied to all chemical shifts observed for a specific type of atom.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.a.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.atom_type
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.a.description: The kind of atom to which the chemical shift offset value applies.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.a.description: Mass number for the atom defined by the '.Atom_type' tag with a systematic chemical shift offset.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.val
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.description: Chemical shift value that has been applied to all of the reported chemical shifts to bring their values in line with values expected to be observed for the chemical shift referencing that was used.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.val_err
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.description: Error in the reported chemical shift offset value used.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.o.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.ordinal
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.o.description: An ordinal identifier uniquely identifying records in the pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset category.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_id.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_id
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_id.description: Unique code assigned to the file being uploaded by the depositor and that contains data that will be incorporated into this entry.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_name.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_name
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_name.description: The directory path and file name for the data file that is to be uploaded.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_category.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_category
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_category.description: This item defines the kind of data in the file uploaded for deposition.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_syntax.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_syntax
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_syntax.description: The syntax or format of the file that is uploaded.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description: This data item is used to group together equivalent geometries coming from different software programs. For instance, if one program has an output of 'tetrahedral' and another has an output of 'tetrahedron' for the same atom, these two records will have the same geometry_id because these two outputs represent the same coordination geometry.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_as.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_asym_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_as.description: The identifier of the instance of the STRUCT_ASYM_ID to which this feature applies.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_se.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_seq_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_se.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_seq_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_co.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_comp_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_co.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_comp_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_at.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_atom_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_at.description: The identifier for the target atom to which the feature is assigned.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_al.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_alt_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.label_al.description: A place holder to indicate alternate conformation.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_asy.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_asym_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_asy.description:
A component of the identifier for the residue at which the conformation segment begins.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_asym_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_seq.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_seq_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_seq.description:
A component of the identifier for the residue at which the conformation segment begins.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_seq_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_com.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_comp_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_com.description:
A component of the identifier for the residue at which the conformation segment begins.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_comp_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_ato.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_atom_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_ato.description:
A component of the identifier for partner 1 of the structure connection.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_atom_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.number.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.number
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.number.description: The coordination number of the target atom.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_calculated
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description: This data item contains the geometry output from a software program (for example, FindGeo or MetalCoord). The geometry is based on what is observed in the coordinate file.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.software.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.software_remark
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.software.description: This data item describes the agreement of the software-generated geometry with the idealized reference geometries. This information is generated by the software that generates geometry annotation.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_generic
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description: This data item translates the geometry output from a software program (for example, FindGeo or MetalCoord) into a unified naming scheme. As software programs sometimes have different names for the same geometry (for instance, 'tetrahedral' and 'tetrahedron' correspond to the same geometry), this data item provides a name for the geometry that is consistent regardless of the provenance to enhance findability. The geometry is based on what is observed in the coordinate file.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_abbr
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description: This data item lists the abbreviated name of the geometry, as specified in the _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.descriptor. For instance, square planar is abbreviated to SPL. The geometry derived from observed geometries in atomic coordinate files.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.provenan.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.provenance
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.provenan.description: The provenance of the feature assigned to this atom.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.assessme.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.assessment
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.assessme.description: This data item provides an assessment of the coordination. The assessment is based on comparison with the CCD. If coordination is annotated in the CCD, then the coordination in the structure will be compared with the reference(s) in the CCD. If there is a match with the reference CCD, assessment will be annotated as Expected, whereas if there is not a match, assessment will be annotated as Unexpected. If there is no reference in the CCD, assessment will be annotated as 'No reference'.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.i.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.i.description: This data item is the parent for _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere_order.sphere_id.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.g.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.geometry_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.g.description: This data item is a foreign key to _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_id. This item maps the coordination descriptor to a specific geometry described in the pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.label_asym_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.description: The identifier of the instance of the STRUCT_ASYM_ID to which this feature applies.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.label_seq_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.label_seq_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.label_comp_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.description: This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.comp_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.label_atom_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.description: The identifier for the target atom to which the feature is assigned.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.label_alt_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.l.description: A place holder to indicate alternate conformation.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.a.description:
A component of the identifier for the residue at which the conformation segment begins.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_asym_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.a.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.auth_seq_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.a.description:
A component of the identifier for the residue at which the conformation segment begins.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_seq_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.a.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.auth_comp_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.a.description:
A component of the identifier for the residue at which the conformation segment begins.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_comp_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.a.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.auth_atom_id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.a.description:
A component of the identifier for partner 1 of the structure connection.

This data item is a pointer to _atom_site.auth_atom_id in the ATOM_SITE category.

_pdbx_nonstandard_list.id.name: _pdbx_nonstandard_list.id
_pdbx_nonstandard_list.id.description:
The value of _pdbx_nonstandard_list.id must uniquely identify each item in the PDBX_NONSTANDARD_LIST list.

For protein polymer entities, this is the three-letter code for amino acids.

For nucleic acid polymer entities, this is the one-letter code for the bases.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.method_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.method_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.method_rotation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.method_rotation identifies the method used for rotation search. For example, the rotation method may be realspace,  fastdirect, or direct. .

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_rotation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_rotation identifies the highest resolution used for rotation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_rotation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_rotation identifies the lowest resolution used for rotation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_rotation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_rotation identifies the sigma cut off of structure factor used for rotation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_rotation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_rotation identifies the sigma cut off of intensity used for rotation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_rotation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_rotation identifies the completness of data used for rotation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.method_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.method_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.method_translation.description:
The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.method_translation identifies the method used for translation search. For example in CNS, the translation method may be "general" or "phased" with PC refinement target using "fastf2f2" "e2e2" "e1e1" "f2f2" "f1f1" "residual" "vector".

.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_translation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_translation identifies the highest resolution used for translation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_translation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_translation identifies the lowest resolution used for translation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_translation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_F_translation identifies the sigma cut off of structure factor used for translation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_translation.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.sigma_I_translation identifies the sigma cut off of intensity used for translation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_translat.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_translat.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.reflns_percent_translation identifies the completness of data used for translation search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Io_to.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Io_to_Ic
_pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Io_to.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Io_to_Ic identifies the correlation between the observed and the calculated intensity (~|F|^2) after rotation and translation.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Fo_to.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc
_pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Fo_to.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc identifies the correlation between the observed and the calculated structure factor after rotation and translation.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.R_factor.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.R_factor
_pdbx_phasing_MR.R_factor.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.R_factor identifies the R factor (defined as uasual) after rotation and translation.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.R_rigid_body.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.R_rigid_body
_pdbx_phasing_MR.R_rigid_body.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.R_rigid_body identifies the R factor for rigid body refinement after rotation and translation.(In general, rigid body refinement has to be carried out after molecular replacement.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.packing.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.packing
_pdbx_phasing_MR.packing.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.packing identifies the packing of search model in the unit cell. Too many crystallographic contacts may indicate a bad search.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.model_details.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.model_details
_pdbx_phasing_MR.model_details.description: The value of _pdbx_phasing_MR.model_details records the details of model used. For example, the original model can be truncated by deleting side chains,  doubtful parts, using the monomer if the original model was an oligomer. The search model may be one domain of a large molecule. What is the pdb IDs.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.native_set_id.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.native_set_id
_pdbx_phasing_MR.native_set_id.description:
The data set that was treated as the native in this experiment.

This data item is a pointer to _phasing_set.id in the PHASING_SET category.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_fit.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_fit
_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_fit.description: The highest resolution limit used for rigid body refinement after molecular replacement (MR) solution.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_fit.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_fit
_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_fit.description: The lowest resolution limit used for rigid body refinement after molecular replacement (MR) solution.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_rotation.description: It is computed by comparing the LLG values from the rotation search with LLG values for a set of random rotations. The mean and the RMS deviation from the mean are computed from the random set, then the Z-score for a search peak is defined as its LLG minus the mean, all divided by the RMS deviation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood." Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_rotation.description: The log-likelihood gain after rotation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood." Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_translation.description: It is computed by comparing the LLG values from the translation search with LLG values for a set of random translations. The mean and the RMS deviation from the mean are computed from the random set, then the Z-score for a search peak is defined as its LLG minus the mean, all divided by the RMS deviation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood." Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_translation.description: The log-likelihood gain after translation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood." Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

_pdbx_reference_molecule.prd_id.name: _pdbx_reference_molecule.prd_id
_pdbx_reference_molecule.prd_id.description:
The value of _pdbx_reference_molecule.prd_id is the unique identifier for the reference molecule in this family.

By convention this ID uniquely identifies the reference molecule in in the PDB reference dictionary.

The ID has the template form PRD_dddddd (e.g. PRD_000001)

_pdbx_reference_molecule.formula_weight.name: _pdbx_reference_molecule.formula_weight
_pdbx_reference_molecule.formula_weight.description: Formula mass in daltons of the entity.

_pdbx_reference_molecule.formula.name: _pdbx_reference_molecule.formula
_pdbx_reference_molecule.formula.description:
The formula for the reference entity. Formulae are written according to the rules:

1. Only recognised element symbols may be used.

2. Each element symbol is followed by a 'count' number. A count of '1' may be omitted.

3. A space or parenthesis must separate each element symbol and its count, but in general parentheses are not used.

4. The order of elements depends on whether or not carbon is present. If carbon is present, the order should be: C, then H, then the other elements in alphabetical order of their symbol. If carbon is not present, the elements are listed purely in alphabetic order of their symbol. This is the 'Hill' system used by Chemical Abstracts.

_pdbx_reference_molecule.type.description: Defines the structural classification of the entity.

_pdbx_refine_component.shift.name: _pdbx_refine_component.shift
_pdbx_refine_component.shift.description:
The tendency of the group of atoms (e.g. residue or ligand, side chain, main chain)  to move away from its current position.

Displacement of atoms from electron density is estimated from the difference (Fobs - Fcal) map. The displacement vector is the ratio of the gradient of difference density to the curvature. The amplitude of the displacement vector is an indicator of the positional error.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.shift_side_chain.description:

Displacement of atoms from electron density is estimated from the difference (Fobs - Fcal) map. The displacement vector is the ratio of the gradient of difference density to the curvature. The amplitude of the displacement vector is an indicator of the positional error.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.shift_main_chain.name: _pdbx_refine_component.shift_main_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.shift_main_chain.description:

_pdbx_refine_component.correlation.name: _pdbx_refine_component.correlation
_pdbx_refine_component.correlation.description:
Correlation coefficient of electron density for each residue or ligand, side chain, main chain

The density correlation coefficient is calculated for each component from atomic densities of (2Fobs-Fcalc) map - "Robs" and the model map (Fcalc) - "Rcalc" :

D_corr =  <Robs><Rcalc>/sqrt(<Robs**2><Rcalc**2>)

where <Robs> is the mean of "observed" densities of atoms of the component (backbone or side chain).

<Rcalc> is the mean of "calculated" densities of component atoms.

The value of density for some atom from map R(x) is:

sum_i ( R(xi) * Ratom(xi - xa) ) Dens =  ---------------------------------- sum_i ( Ratom(xi - xa) )

where  Ratom(x) is atomic electron density for the x-th grid point. xa - vector of the centre of atom. xi - vector of the i-th point of grid. Sum is taken over all grid points which have distance from the center of the atom less than the Radius_limit. For all atoms Radius_limit = 2.5 A.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.correlation_side_.name: _pdbx_refine_component.correlation_side_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.correlation_side_.description:
Correlation coefficient of electron density for each residue or ligand, side chain, main chain

The density correlation coefficient is calculated for each component from atomic densities of (2Fobs-Fcalc) map - "Robs" and the model map (Fcalc) - "Rcalc" :

D_corr =  <Robs><Rcalc>/sqrt(<Robs**2><Rcalc**2>)

where <Robs> is the mean of "observed" densities of atoms of the component (backbone or side chain).

<Rcalc> is the mean of "calculated" densities of component atoms.

The value of density for some atom from map R(x) is:

sum_i ( R(xi) * Ratom(xi - xa) ) Dens =  ---------------------------------- sum_i ( Ratom(xi - xa) )

where  Ratom(x) is atomic electron density for the x-th grid point. xa - vector of the centre of atom. xi - vector of the i-th point of grid. Sum is taken over all grid points which have distance from the center of the atom less than the Radius_limit. For all atoms Radius_limit = 2.5 A.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.correlation_main_.name: _pdbx_refine_component.correlation_main_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.correlation_main_.description:
Correlation coefficient of electron density for each residue or ligand, side chain, main chain

The density correlation coefficient is calculated for each component from atomic densities of (2Fobs-Fcalc) map - "Robs" and the model map (Fcalc) - "Rcalc" :

D_corr =  <Robs><Rcalc>/sqrt(<Robs**2><Rcalc**2>)

where <Robs> is the mean of "observed" densities of atoms of the component (backbone or side chain).

<Rcalc> is the mean of "calculated" densities of component atoms.

The value of density for some atom from map R(x) is:

sum_i ( R(xi) * Ratom(xi - xa) ) Dens =  ---------------------------------- sum_i ( Ratom(xi - xa) )

where  Ratom(x) is atomic electron density for the x-th grid point. xa - vector of the centre of atom. xi - vector of the i-th point of grid. Sum is taken over all grid points which have distance from the center of the atom less than the Radius_limit. For all atoms Radius_limit = 2.5 A.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R.name: _pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R
_pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R.description:
Real space R factor of electron density for each component, residue side chain, or main chain.

The real space R factor is calculated by the equation

R_real = [Sum~i (|Dobs - Dcal|)]/[Sum~i (|Dobs + Dcal|)]

Where: Dobs is the observed  electron density, Dcal is the calculated  electron density, summation is for all the grid points

Ref: Branden, C.I. & Jones, T.A. (1990).  Nature, 343, 687-689

_pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R_side.name: _pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R_side_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R_side.description:
Real space R factor of electron density for each component, residue side chain, or main chain.

The real space R factor is calculated by the equation

R_real = [Sum~i (|Dobs - Dcal|)]/[Sum~i (|Dobs + Dcal|)]

Where: Dobs is the observed  electron density, Dcal is the calculated  electron density, summation is for all the grid points

Ref: Branden, C.I. & Jones, T.A. (1990).  Nature, 343, 687-689

_pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R_main.name: _pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R_main_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.real_space_R_main.description:
Real space R factor of electron density for each component, residue side chain, or main chain.

The real space R factor is calculated by the equation

R_real = [Sum~i (|Dobs - Dcal|)]/[Sum~i (|Dobs + Dcal|)]

Where: Dobs is the observed  electron density, Dcal is the calculated  electron density, summation is for all the grid points

Ref: Branden, C.I. & Jones, T.A. (1990).  Nature, 343, 687-689

_pdbx_refine_component.connect.name: _pdbx_refine_component.connect
_pdbx_refine_component.connect.description:
The index of connectivity is the product of the (2Fobs-Fcal) electron density values for the backbone atoms (N, CA and C) divided by the average value for the structure. Low values (less than 1.0) of this index indicate breaks in the backbone electron density which may be due to flexibility of the chain or incorrect tracing.

connect = [(D(xi)...D(xi))^(1/N)] /<D>_all

Where: D(xi) = (2*Fobs - Fcal) <D>_all is the averaged value of density for the structure. The product is for N atoms of group.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.density_index.name: _pdbx_refine_component.density_index
_pdbx_refine_component.density_index.description:
The index of density is the product of the (2Fobs-Fcal) electron density values for the group of atoms divided by the average value for the structure. Low values (less than 1.0) may be problematic for model fitting.

index = [(D(xi)...D(xi))^(1/N)] /<D>_all

Where : D(xi) = (2*Fobs - Fcal) <D>_all is the averaged value of density for the structure. The product is for N atoms of group.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.density_index_mai.name: _pdbx_refine_component.density_index_main_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.density_index_mai.description:
The index of density is the product of the (2Fobs-Fcal) electron density values for the group of atoms divided by the average value for the structure. Low values (less than 1.0) may be problematic for model fitting.

index = [(D(xi)...D(xi))^(1/N)] /<D>_all

Where : D(xi) = (2*Fobs - Fcal) <D>_all is the averaged value of density for the structure. The product is for N atoms of group.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.density_index_sid.name: _pdbx_refine_component.density_index_side_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.density_index_sid.description:
The index of density is the product of the (2Fobs-Fcal) electron density values for the group of atoms divided by the average value for the structure. Low values (less than 1.0) may be problematic for model fitting.

index = [(D(xi)...D(xi))^(1/N)] /<D>_all

Where : D(xi) = (2*Fobs - Fcal) <D>_all is the averaged value of density for the structure. The product is for N atoms of group.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio.name: _pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio
_pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio.description:
The density ratio is similar to the density index, but summation of the density for the group is used for calculation.

The ratio of density is the summation of the (2Fobs-Fcal) electron density values for the group of atoms divided by the average value for the structure. Low values (less than 0.4) of this ratio may be problematic for the group.

index = [Sum~i D(xi)]/<D>_all

Where:

D(xi) = (2*Fobs - Fcal) <D>_all is the average value of density for the structure. The summation is for all the atoms of group.

_pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio_mai.name: _pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio_main_chain
_pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio_mai.description:

index = [Sum~i D(xi)]/<D>_all

D(xi) = (2*Fobs - Fcal) <D>_all is the average value of density for the structure. The summation is for all the atoms of group.

_pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio_sid.name: _pdbx_refine_component.density_ratio_side_chain

_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.dom_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _struct_ncs_dom.id in the STRUCT_NCS_DOM category.

_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.type.name: _pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.type
_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.type.description: The type of restraint applied to the domain specified by _pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.dom_id and in the domains against which it was restrained.

_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.number.name: _pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.number
_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.number.description: The number of this type of restraint applied to the domain specified by _pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.dom_id and in the domains against which it was restrained.

_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.rms_dev.name: _pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.rms_dev
_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.rms_dev.description: The root-mean-square deviation in restraints for the domain specified by _pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.dom_id and in the domains against which it was restrained.

_pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.weight.description: The value of the weighting coefficient used in non-crystalographic restaint in the domain specified by _pdbx_refine_ls_restr_ncs.dom_id to equivalent atomic positions in the domains against which it was restrained.

_pdbx_refine_tls.id.name: _pdbx_refine_tls.id
_pdbx_refine_tls.id.description: The value of _pdbx_refine_tls.id must uniquely identify a record in the PDBX_REFINE_TLS list. Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_pdbx_refine_tls.pdbx_refine_id.name: _pdbx_refine_tls.pdbx_refine_id
_pdbx_refine_tls.pdbx_refine_id.description: This data item uniquely identifies a refinement within an entry. _pdbx_refine_tls.pdbx_refine_id can be used to distinguish the results of joint refinements.

_pdbx_refine_tls.details.name: _pdbx_refine_tls.details
_pdbx_refine_tls.details.description: A description of the TLS group, such as a domain name or a chemical group name.

_pdbx_refine_tls.method.name: _pdbx_refine_tls.method
_pdbx_refine_tls.method.description: The method by which the TLS parameters were obtained.
enums: TLS parameters fitted to previously refined anisotropic displacement parameters
TLS parameters refined directly against crystallographic residual

_pdbx_refine_tls.origin_x.name: _pdbx_refine_tls.origin_x
_pdbx_refine_tls.origin_x.description:
The x coordinate in angstroms of the origin to which the TLS parameters are referred, specified according to a set of orthogonal Cartesian axes related to the cell axes as given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

If the origin is omitted, it is assumed to be the centre of reaction of the group, in which case S must be symmetric

_pdbx_reflns_twin.diffrn_id.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.diffrn_id

_pdbx_reflns_twin.crystal_id.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.crystal_id
_pdbx_reflns_twin.crystal_id.description: The crystal identifier.  A reference to _exptl_crystal.id in category EXPTL_CRYSTAL.

_pdbx_reflns_twin.domain_id.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.domain_id
_pdbx_reflns_twin.domain_id.description: An identifier for the twin domain.

_pdbx_reflns_twin.type.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.type
_pdbx_reflns_twin.type.description:
There are two types of twinning: merohedral or hemihedral non-merohedral or epitaxial

For merohedral twinning the diffraction patterns from the different domains are completely superimposable.   Hemihedral twinning is a special case of merohedral twinning. It only involves two distinct domains.  Pseudo-merohedral twinning is a subclass merohedral twinning in which lattice is coincidentally superimposable.

In the case of non-merohedral or epitaxial twinning  the reciprocal lattices do not superimpose exactly. In this case the  diffraction pattern consists of two (or more) interpenetrating lattices, which can in principle be separated.

_pdbx_reflns_twin.operator.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.operator
_pdbx_reflns_twin.operator.description:
The possible merohedral or hemihedral twinning operators for different point groups are:

True point group   Twin operation   hkl related to 3                       2 along a,b             h,-h-k,-l 2 along a*,b*           h+k,-k,-l 2 along c               -h,-k,l 4                       2 along a,b,a*,b*       h,-k,-l 6                       2 along a,b,a*,b*       h,-h-k,-l 321                     2 along a*,b*,c         -h,-k,l 312                     2 along a,b,c           -h,-k,l 23                      4 along a,b,c            k,-h,l

References: Yeates, T.O. (1997) Methods in Enzymology 276, 344-358. Detecting and Overcoming Crystal Twinning.

and information from the following on-line sites:

CNS site http://cns.csb.yale.edu/v1.1/ CCP4 site http://www.ccp4.ac.uk/dist/html/detwin.html SHELX site http://shelx.uni-ac.gwdg.de/~rherbst/twin.html

_pdbx_reflns_twin.fraction.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.fraction
_pdbx_reflns_twin.fraction.description: The twin fraction or twin factor represents a quantitative parameter for the crystal twinning.  The value 0 represents no twinning, < 0.5 partial twinning, = 0.5 for perfect twinning.

_pdbx_reflns_twin.mean_I2_over_mean_I_sq.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.mean_I2_over_mean_I_square
_pdbx_reflns_twin.mean_I2_over_mean_I_sq.description:
The ideal statistics for twinned crystals. The values calculated with the acentric data are given below.

Statistic Untwinned data Perfect twinned data <I^2>/<I>^2 2.0             1.5 <F>^2/<F^2> 0.785           0.865

References: Yeates, T.O. (1997) Methods in Enzymology 276, 344-358. Detecting and Overcoming Crystal Twinning.

and information from the following on-line sites: CNS site http://cns.csb.yale.edu/v1.1/ CCP4 site http://www.ccp4.ac.uk/dist/html/detwin.html SHELX site http://shelx.uni-ac.gwdg.de/~rherbst/twin.html

_pdbx_reflns_twin.mean_F_square_over_mea.name: _pdbx_reflns_twin.mean_F_square_over_mean_F2
_pdbx_reflns_twin.mean_F_square_over_mea.description:
The ideal statistics for twinned crystals. The values calculated with the acentric data are given below.

Statistic Untwinned data Perfect twinned data <I^2>/<I>^2 2.0             1.5 <F>^2/<F^2> 0.785           0.865

References: Yeates, T.O. (1997) Methods in Enzymology 276, 344-358. Detecting and Overcoming Crystal Twinning.

and information from the following on-line sites: CNS site http://cns.csb.yale.edu/v1.1/ CCP4 site http://www.ccp4.ac.uk/dist/html/detwin.html SHELX site http://shelx.uni-ac.gwdg.de/~rherbst/twin.html

_pdbx_soln_scatter.buffer_name.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter.buffer_name

_pdbx_soln_scatter.mean_guiner_radius.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter.mean_guiner_radius
_pdbx_soln_scatter.mean_guiner_radius.description:
The mean radius of structural elongation of the sample. In a given solute-solvent contrast, the radius of gyration R_G is a measure of structural elongation if the internal inhomogeneity of scattering densities has no effect. Guiner analysis at low Q gives the R_G and the forward scattering at zero angle I(0).

lnl(Q) = lnl(0) - R_G^2Q^2/3

where Q = 4(pi)sin(theta/lamda) 2theta = scattering angle lamda = wavelength

The above expression is valid in a QR_G range for extended rod-like particles. The relative I(0)/c values ( where c = sample concentration) for sample measurements in a constant buffer for a single sample data session, gives the relative masses of the protein(s) studied when referenced against a standard.

see: O.Glatter & O.Kratky, (1982). Editors of "Small angle X-ray Scattering, Academic Press, New York. O.Kratky. (1963). X-ray small angle scattering with substances of biological interest in diluted solutions. Prog. Biophys. Chem., 13, 105-173.

G.D.Wignall & F.S.Bates, (1987). The small-angle approximation of X-ray and neutron scatter from rigid rods of non-uniform cross section and finite length. J.Appl. Crystallog., 18, 452-460.

If the structure is elongated, the mean radius of gyration of the cross-sectional structure R_XS  and the mean cross sectional intensity at zero angle [I(Q).Q]_Q->0 is obtained from

ln[I(Q).Q] = ln[l(Q).(Q)]_Q->0 - ((R_XS)^2Q^2)/2

_pdbx_soln_scatter.mean_guiner_radius_es.description:
The estimated standard deviation for the mean radius of structural elongation of the sample. X-ray small angle scattering with substances of biological interest in diluted solutions. Prog. Biophys. Chem., 13, 105-173. G.D.Wignall & F.S.Bates, (1987). The small-angle approximation of X-ray and neutron scatter from rigid rods of non-uniform cross section and finite length. J.Appl. Crystallog., 18, 452-460.

If the structure is elongated, the mean radius of gyration of the cross-sectional structure R_XS  and the mean cross sectional intensity at zero angle [I(Q).Q]_Q->0 is obtained from ln[I(Q).Q] = ln[l(Q).(Q)]_Q->0 - ((R_XS)^2Q^2)/2

_pdbx_soln_scatter.max_mean_cross_sectio.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter.max_mean_cross_sectional_radii_gyration_esd
_pdbx_soln_scatter.max_mean_cross_sectio.description:
The estimated standard deviation for the minimum mean radius of structural elongation of the sample. In a given solute-solvent contrast, the radius of gyration R_G is a measure of structural elongation if the internal inhomogeneity of scattering densities has no effect. Guiner analysis at low Q give the R_G and the forward scattering at zero angle I(0).

lnl(Q) = lnl(0) - R_G^2Q^2/3

where Q = 4(pi)sin(theta/lamda) 2theta = scattering angle lamda = wavelength

The above expression is valid in a QR_G range for extended rod-like particles. The relative I(0)/c values ( where c = sample concentration) for sample measurements in a constant buffer for a single sample data session, gives the relative masses of the protein(s) studied when referenced against a standard.

see: O.Glatter & O.Kratky, (1982). Editors of "Small angle X-ray Scattering, Academic Press, New York. O.Kratky. (1963). X-ray small angle scattering with substances of biological interest in diluted solutions. Prog. Biophys. Chem., 13, 105-173. G.D.Wignall & F.S.Bates, (1987). The small-angle approximation of X-ray and neutron scatter from rigid rods of non-uniform cross section and finite length. J.Appl. Crystallog., 18, 452-460.

If the structure is elongated, the mean radius of gyration of the cross-sectional structure R_XS  and the mean cross sectional intensity at zero angle [I(Q).Q]_Q->0 is obtained from ln[I(Q).Q] = ln[l(Q).(Q)]_Q->0 - ((R_XS)^2Q^2)/2

_pdbx_soln_scatter.protein_length.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter.protein_length
_pdbx_soln_scatter.protein_length.description:
The length (or range) of the protein sample under study. If the solution structure is approximated as an elongated elliptical cyclinder the length L is determined from,

L = sqrt [12( (R_G)^2  -  (R_XS)^2 ) ]

The length should also be given by

L = pi I(0) / [ I(Q).Q]_Q->0

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.id.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.id
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.id.description: The value of _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.id must uniquely identify the sample in the category PDBX_SOLN_SCATTER_MODEL

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.details.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.details
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.details.description: A description of any additional details concerning the experiment.
examples:
Homology models were built for
 the 17 SCR domains and energy minimisations were
 performed to improve the connectivity in the fh model.
 triantennary complex-type carbohydrate structures
 (MAN3GLCNAC6GAL3FUC3NEUNAC1) were added to each of the
 N-linked glycosylation sites. a library of linker peptide
 conformations was used in domain modelling constrained
 by the solution scattering fits. modelling with the
 scattering data was also carried out by rotational
 search methods. the x-ray and neutron scattering curve
 I(Q) was calculated assuming a uniform scattering density
 for the spheres using the debye equation as adapted to
 spheres. x-ray curves were calculated from the hydrated
 sphere models without corrections for wavelength spread or
 beam divergence, while these corrections were applied for
 the neutron curves but now using unhydrated models.

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.method.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.method
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.method.description: A description of the methods used in the modelling
examples: Constrained scattering fitting of homology models

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.software_list.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.software_list
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.software_list.description: A list of the software used in the modeeling
examples: INSIGHT II, HOMOLOGY, DISCOVERY, BIOPOLYMER, DELPHI

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.software_author.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.software_author_list
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.software_author.description: A list of the software authors

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.entry_fitting_l.description: A list of the entries used to fit the model to the scattering data

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.num_conformers_.description: The number of model conformers calculated.

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.num_conformers_.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.num_conformers_submitted
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.num_conformers_.description: The number of model conformers submitted in the entry

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.representative_.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.representative_conformer
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.representative_.description: The index of the representative conformer among the submitted conformers for the entry

_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.conformer_selec.name: _pdbx_soln_scatter_model.conformer_selection_criteria
_pdbx_soln_scatter_model.conformer_selec.description: A description of the conformer selection criteria used.
examples:
The modelled scattering curves were assessed by calculation of the
 RG, RSX-1 and RXS-2 values in the same Q ranges
 used in the experimental Guinier fits. models were
 then ranked using a goodness-of-fit R-factor
 defined by analogy with protein crystallography
 and based on the experimental curves in the Q range
 extending to 1.4 nm-1.

_pdbx_struct_assembly_gen.oper_expressio.name: _pdbx_struct_assembly_gen.oper_expression
_pdbx_struct_assembly_gen.oper_expressio.description:
Identifies the operation of collection of operations from category PDBX_STRUCT_OPER_LIST.

Operation expressions may have the forms:

(1)        the single operation 1 (1,2,5)    the operations 1, 2, 5 (1-4)      the operations 1,2,3 and 4 (1,2)(3,4) the combinations of operations 3 and 4 followed by 1 and 2 (i.e. the cartesian product of parenthetical groups applied from right to left)

_pdbx_struct_entity_inst.details.description: A description of special aspects of this portion of the contents of the deposited unit.
examples:
The drug binds to this enzyme in two roughly
 twofold symmetric modes. Hence this
 biological unit (3) is roughly twofold
 symmetric to biological unit (2). Disorder in
 the protein chain indicated with alternative
 ID 2 should be used with this biological unit.

Component and/or atom selection defining an NCS group
Component and/or atom selection defining a TLS group

_pdbx_struct_group_list.group_enumeratio.name: _pdbx_struct_group_list.group_enumeration_type
_pdbx_struct_group_list.group_enumeratio.description:
The manner in which the group is defined.  Groups consist of collections within the set of deposited coordinates which can be defined in terms of a list of chemical components in category PDBX_STRUCT_GROUP_COMPONENTS, ranges of chemical components in PDBX_STRUCT_GROUP_COMPONENT_RANGE, or as individual atoms using _atom_site.pdbx_group_id.

Groups can be composed of selections from multiple categories in which case the enumeration types are separated by commas.

examples:
A  for alanine or adenine
B  for ambiguous asparagine/aspartic-acid
R  for arginine
N  for asparagine
D  for aspartic-acid
C  for cysteine or cystine or cytosine
Q  for glutamine
E  for glutamic-acid
Z  for ambiguous glutamine/glutamic acid
G  for glycine or guanine
H  for histidine
I  for isoleucine
L  for leucine
K  for lysine
M  for methionine
F  for phenylalanine
P  for proline
S  for serine
T  for threonine or thymine
W  for tryptophan
Y  for tyrosine
V  for valine
U  for uracil
O  for water
X  for other

A description of the MAD phasing method used to phase this structure.

Note that this is not the computer program used, which is described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method itself.

This data item should be used to describe significant methodological options used within the MAD phasing program.

_phasing_MAD_clust.id.name: _phasing_MAD_clust.id
_phasing_MAD_clust.id.description:
The value of _phasing_MAD_clust.id must, together with _phasing_MAD_clust.expt_id, uniquely identify a record in the PHASING_MAD_CLUST list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_phasing_MAD_clust.number_set.name: _phasing_MAD_clust.number_set
_phasing_MAD_clust.number_set.description: The number of data sets in this cluster of data sets.

_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_delta_phi.description: The difference between two independent determinations of _phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi.

_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi.description: The phase difference between F~t~(h), the structure factor due to normal scattering from all atoms, and F~a~(h), the structure factor due to normal scattering from only the anomalous scatterers.

_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi_sigma.name: _phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi_sigma
_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi_sigma.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi.

_phasing_MAD_expt.id.name: _phasing_MAD_expt.id
_phasing_MAD_expt.id.description: The value of _phasing_MAD_expt.id must uniquely identify each record in the PHASING_MAD_EXPT list.

_phasing_MAD_expt.number_clust.description: The number of clusters of data sets in this phasing experiment.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_high.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_high
_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_high.description: The lowest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data used for the comparison of Bijvoet differences. This is called the highest resolution.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_low.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_low
_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_low.description: The highest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data used for the comparison of Bijvoet differences. This is called the lowest resolution.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.expt_id.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.expt_id
_phasing_MAD_ratio.expt_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _phasing_MAD_expt.id in the PHASING_MAD_EXPT category.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl.description: The root-mean-square Bijvoet difference at one wavelength for all reflections.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl_centric.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl_centric
_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl_centric.description: The root-mean-square Bijvoet difference at one wavelength for centric reflections. This would be equal to zero for perfect data and thus serves as an estimate of the noise in the anomalous signals.

_phasing_MIR.FOM.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for all reflections phased in the native data set.

int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha m = -------------------------------- int P~alpha~ dalpha

P~a~ = the probability that the phase angle a is correct

the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR.FOM_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR.FOM_acentric
_phasing_MIR.FOM_acentric.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for the acentric reflections phased in the native data set.

int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha m = -------------------------------- int P~alpha~ dalpha

P~a~ = the probability that the phase angle a is correct

the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR.FOM_centric.name: _phasing_MIR.FOM_centric
_phasing_MIR.FOM_centric.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for the centric reflections phased in the native data set.

int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha m = -------------------------------- int P~alpha~ dalpha

P~a~ = the probability that the phase angle a is correct

the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR.method.name: _phasing_MIR.method
_phasing_MIR.method.description:
A description of the MIR phasing method applied to phase this structure.

Note that this is not the computer program used, which is described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method itself.

This data item should be used to describe significant methodological options used within the MIR phasing program.

_phasing_MIR.reflns.name: _phasing_MIR.reflns
_phasing_MIR.reflns.description: The total number of reflections phased in the native data set.

_phasing_MIR.reflns_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR.reflns_acentric
_phasing_MIR.reflns_acentric.description: The number of acentric reflections phased in the native data set.

_phasing_MIR.reflns_centric.name: _phasing_MIR.reflns_centric
_phasing_MIR.reflns_centric.description: The number of centric reflections phased in the native data set.

_phasing_MIR.reflns_criterion.name: _phasing_MIR.reflns_criterion
_phasing_MIR.reflns_criterion.description: Criterion used to limit the reflections used in the phasing calculations.

_phasing_MIR.pdbx_number_derivatives.name: _phasing_MIR.pdbx_number_derivatives
_phasing_MIR.pdbx_number_derivatives.description: The number of derivatives used in this phasing experiment.

_phasing_MIR_der.d_res_high.name: _phasing_MIR_der.d_res_high
_phasing_MIR_der.d_res_high.description: The lowest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data used for this derivative. This is called the highest resolution.

_phasing_MIR_der.d_res_low.name: _phasing_MIR_der.d_res_low
_phasing_MIR_der.d_res_low.description: The highest value for  the interplanar spacings for the reflection data used for this derivative. This is called the lowest resolution.

_phasing_MIR_der.der_set_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der.der_set_id
_phasing_MIR_der.der_set_id.description:
The data set that was treated as the derivative in this experiment.

This data item is a pointer to _phasing_set.id in the PHASING_SET category.

_phasing_MIR_der.details.name: _phasing_MIR_der.details
_phasing_MIR_der.details.description: A description of special aspects of this derivative, its data, its solution or its use in phasing.

_phasing_MIR_der.id.name: _phasing_MIR_der.id
_phasing_MIR_der.id.description:
The value of _phasing_MIR_der.id must uniquely identify a record in the PHASING_MIR_DER list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_phasing_MIR_der.native_set_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der.native_set_id
_phasing_MIR_der.native_set_id.description:
The data set that was treated as the native in this experiment.

This data item is a pointer to _phasing_set.id in the PHASING_SET category.

_phasing_MIR_der.number_of_sites.name: _phasing_MIR_der.number_of_sites
_phasing_MIR_der.number_of_sites.description: The number of heavy-atom sites in this derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.power_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.power_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.power_acentric.description:
The mean phasing power P for acentric reflections for this derivative.

sum|Fh~calc~^2^| P = (----------------------------)^1/2^ sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|^2^

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of this derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of this derivative Fh~calc~  = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der.power_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.power_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.power_centric.description:
The mean phasing power P for centric reflections for this derivative.

sum|Fh~calc~^2^| P = (----------------------------)^1/2^ sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|^2^

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~  = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_acentric.description:
Residual factor R~cullis,acen~ for acentric reflections for this derivative.

The Cullis R factor was originally defined only for centric reflections.  It is, however, also a useful statistical measure for acentric reflections, which is how it is used in this data item.

sum| |Fph~obs~ +/- Fp~obs~| - Fh~calc~ | R~cullis,acen~ = ---------------------------------------- sum|Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~|

Fp~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~ = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G. & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc. London Ser. A, 265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_anomalous.name: _phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_anomalous
_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_anomalous.description:
Residual factor R~cullis,ano~ for anomalous reflections for this derivative.

The Cullis R factor was originally defined only for centric reflections.  It is, however, also a useful statistical measure for anomalous  reflections, which is how it is used in this data item.

This is tabulated for acentric terms.  A value less than 1.0 means there is some contribution to the phasing from the anomalous data.

sum |Fph+~obs~Fph-~obs~ - Fh+~calc~ - Fh-~calc~| R~cullis,ano~ = ------------------------------------------------ sum|Fph+~obs~ - Fph-~obs~|

Fph+~obs~  = the observed positive Friedel structure-factor amplitude for the derivative Fph-~obs~  = the observed negative Friedel structure-factor amplitude for the derivative

Fh+~calc~  = the calculated positive Friedel structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model Fh-~calc~  = the calculated negative Friedel structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G. & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc.  London Ser. A, 265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_centric.description:
Residual factor R~cullis~ for centric reflections for this derivative.

sum| |Fph~obs~ +/- Fp~obs~| - Fh~calc~ | R~cullis~ = ---------------------------------------- sum|Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~|

Fp~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~ = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G. & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc. London Ser. A, 265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_acentric.description: The number of acentric reflections used in phasing for this derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_anomalous.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_anomalous
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_anomalous.description: The number of anomalous reflections used in phasing for this derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_centric.description: The number of centric reflections used in phasing for this derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_criteria.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_criteria
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_criteria.description: Criteria used to limit the reflections used in the phasing calculations.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut_centric.description: record R_kraut obtained from centric data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut_acentric.description: record R_kraut obtained from acentric data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_R_kraut.description: record R_kraut obtained from all data data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc_centric.description: record lack of closure obtained from centric data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc_acentric.description: record lack of closure obtained from acentric data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_loc.description: record lack of closure obtained from all data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom_centric.description: record figure of merit obtained from centric data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom_acentric.description: record figure of merit obtained from acentric data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_fom.description: record figure of merit obtained from all data for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_power.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_power
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_power.description: record phasing power for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_reflns.name: _phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_reflns
_phasing_MIR_der.pdbx_reflns.description: record number of reflections used for each derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.der_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.der_id
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.der_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _phasing_MIR_der.id in the PHASING_MIR_DER category.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc.description: The calculated value of the structure factor for this derivative, in electrons.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc_au.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc_au
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc_au.description: The calculated value of the structure factor for this derivative, in arbitrary units.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas.description: The measured value of the structure factor for this derivative, in electrons.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au.description: The measured value of the structure factor for this derivative, in arbitrary units.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas, in electrons.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma_au.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma_au
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma_au.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au, in arbitrary units.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_A_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_A_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_A_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient A~iso~ for this reflection for this derivative.

-2.0 * (Fp~obs~^2^ + Fh~calc~^2^ - Fph~obs~^2^) *  Fp~obs~ * cos(alphah~calc~) A~iso~ =  ----------------------------------------------- E^2^

E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^ for centric reflections = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^ for acentric reflections

Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model alphah~calc~ = the calculated phase from the heavy-atom model

This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase probability of each isomorphous derivative:

P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha) + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_B_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_B_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_B_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient B~iso~ for this reflection for this derivative.

-2.0 * (Fp~obs~^2^ + Fh~calc~^2^ - Fph~obs~^2^) *  Fp~obs~ * sin(alphah~calc~) B~iso~ =  ----------------------------------------------- E^2^

E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^ for centric reflections = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^ for acentric reflections

Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model alphah~calc~ = the phase calculated from the heavy-atom model

This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase probability of each isomorphous derivative:

P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha) + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_C_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_C_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_C_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient C~iso~ for this reflection for this derivative.

-Fp~obs~^2^ * [sin(alphah~calc~)^2^ -  cos(alphah~calc~)^2^] C~iso~ =  ------------------------------------ E^2^

E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^ for centric reflections = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^ for acentric reflections

Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model alphah~calc~ = the phase calculated from the heavy-atom model

This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase probability of each isomorphous derivative:

P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha) + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_D_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_D_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_D_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient D~iso~ for this reflection for this derivative.

-2.0 * Fp~obs~^2^ * sin(alphah~calc~)^2^ * cos(alphah~calc~)^2^ D~iso~ =  ---------------------------------------- E^2^

E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^ for centric reflections = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^ for acentric reflections

Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model alphah~calc~ = the phase calculated from the heavy-atom model

This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase probability of each isomorphous derivative:

P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha) + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.index_k.description: Miller index k for this reflection for this derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.index_l.description: Miller index l for this reflection for this derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.phase_calc.description: The calculated value of the structure-factor phase based on the heavy-atom model for this derivative in degrees.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.set_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.set_id
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.set_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _phasing_set.id in the PHASING_SET category.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.d_res_high.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.d_res_high
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.d_res_high.description: The lowest value  for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data for this derivative in this shell. This is called the highest resolution.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.d_res_low.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.d_res_low
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.d_res_low.description: The highest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data for this derivative in this shell. This is called the lowest resolution.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.der_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.der_id
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.der_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _phasing_MIR_der.id in the PHASING_MIR_DER category.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.fom.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.fom
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.fom.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for reflections for this derivative in this shell.

int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha m = -------------------------------- int P~alpha~ dalpha

P~alpha~ = the probability that the phase angle alpha is correct

int is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.ha_ampl.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.ha_ampl
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.ha_ampl.description: The mean heavy-atom amplitude for reflections for this derivative in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.loc.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.loc
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.loc.description:
The mean lack-of-closure error loc for reflections for this derivative in this shell.

loc = sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the derivative

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.phase.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.phase
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.phase.description: The mean of the phase values for reflections for this derivative in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.power.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.power
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.power.description:
The mean phasing power P for reflections for this derivative in this shell.

sum|Fh~calc~^2^| P = (----------------------------)^1/2^ sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|^2^

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~  = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_cullis.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_cullis
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_cullis.description:
Residual factor R~cullis~ for centric reflections for this derivative in this shell.

sum| |Fph~obs~ +/- Fp~obs~| - Fh~calc~ | R~cullis~ = ---------------------------------------- sum|Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~|

Fp~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~ = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G. & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc. London Ser. A, 265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_kraut.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_kraut
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_kraut.description:
Residual factor R~kraut~ for general reflections for this derivative in this shell.

sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~| R~kraut~ = ------------------------- sum|Fph~obs~|

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the derivative

sum is taken over the specified reflections

Ref: Kraut, J., Sieker, L. C., High, D. F. & Freer, S. T. (1962). Proc. Natl Acad. Sci. USA, 48, 1417-1424.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.reflns.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.reflns
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.reflns.description: The number of reflections in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_R_cullis_cen.description: record R Cullis obtained from centric data for each derivative, but broken into resolution shells

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_power_centri.description: record phasing power obtained from centric data for each derivative, but broken into resolution shells

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_power_acentr.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_power_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_power_acentr.description: record phasing power obtained from acentric data for each derivative, but broken into resolution shells

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_fom_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_fom_centric
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_fom_centric.description: record figure of merit obtained from centric data for each derivative, but broken into resolution shells

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_fom_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_fom_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_fom_acentric.description: record figure of merit obtained from acentric data for each derivative, but broken into resolution shells

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_reflns_centr.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_reflns_centric
