_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_reflns_centr.description: record  number of centric reflections used for phasing for each derivative, but broken into resolution shells

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_reflns_acent.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_reflns_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.pdbx_reflns_acent.description: record  number of acentric reflections used for phasing for each derivative, but broken into resolution shells

_phasing_MIR_der_site.atom_type_symbol.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.atom_type_symbol
_phasing_MIR_der_site.atom_type_symbol.description:
This data item is a pointer to _atom_type.symbol in the ATOM_TYPE category.

The scattering factors referenced via this data item should be those used in the refinement of the heavy-atom data; in some cases this is the scattering factor for the single heavy atom, in other cases these are the scattering factors for an atomic cluster.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso.description: Isotropic displacement parameter for this heavy-atom site in this derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x.description: The x coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative specified as orthogonal angstroms. The orthogonal Cartesian axes are related to the cell axes as specified by the description given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y.description: The y coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative specified as orthogonal angstroms. The orthogonal Cartesian axes are related to the cell axes as specified by the description given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z.description: The z coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative specified as orthogonal angstroms. The orthogonal Cartesian axes are related to the cell axes as specified by the description given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.der_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.der_id
_phasing_MIR_der_site.der_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _phasing_MIR_der.id in the PHASING_MIR_DER category.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.details.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.details
_phasing_MIR_der_site.details.description: A description of special aspects of the derivative site.
examples: minor site obtained from difference Fourier
same as site 2 in the K2HgI4 derivative

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x.description: The x coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative specified as a fraction of _cell.length_a.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y.description: The y coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative specified as a fraction of _cell.length_b.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z.description: The z coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative specified as a fraction of _cell.length_c.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.id
_phasing_MIR_der_site.id.description:
The value of _phasing_MIR_der_site.id must uniquely identify each site in each derivative in the PHASING_MIR_DER_SITE list.

The atom identifiers need not be unique over all sites in all derivatives; they need only be unique for each site in each derivative.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy.description: The fraction of the atom type present at this heavy-atom site in a given derivative. The sum of the occupancies of all the atom types at this site may not significantly exceed 1.0 unless it is a dummy site.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom.description: The relative anomalous occupancy of the atom type present at this heavy-atom site in a given derivative. This atom occupancy will probably be on an arbitrary scale.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom_su.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom_su
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom_su.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso.description: The relative real isotropic occupancy of the atom type present at this heavy-atom site in a given derivative. This atom occupancy will probably be on an arbitrary scale.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso_su.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso_su
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso_su.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso.

_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high
_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high.description: The lowest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data in this shell. This is called the highest resolution. Note that the resolution limits of shells in the items _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high and _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low are independent of the resolution limits of shells in the items _reflns_shell.d_res_high and _reflns_shell.d_res_low.

_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low
_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low.description: The highest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data in this shell. This is called the lowest resolution. Note that the resolution limits of shells in the items _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high and _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low are independent of the resolution limits of shells in the items _reflns_shell.d_res_high and _reflns_shell.d_res_low.

_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.FOM
_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for reflections in this shell.

int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha m = -------------------------------- int P~alpha~ dalpha

P~alpha~ = the probability that the phase angle alpha is correct

the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_acentric
_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_acentric.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for acentric reflections in this shell.

int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha m = -------------------------------- int P~alpha~ dalpha

P~a~ = the probability that the phase angle a is correct

the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_centric
_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_centric.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for centric reflections in this shell.

int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha m = -------------------------------- int P~alpha~ dalpha

P~a~ = the probability that the phase angle a is correct

the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR_shell.loc.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.loc
_phasing_MIR_shell.loc.description:
The mean lack-of-closure error loc for reflections in this shell.

loc = sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the derivative

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_shell.mean_phase.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.mean_phase
_phasing_MIR_shell.mean_phase.description: The mean of the phase values for all reflections in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.power.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.power
_phasing_MIR_shell.power.description:
The mean phasing power P for reflections in this shell.

sum|Fh~calc~^2^| P = (----------------------------)^1/2^ sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|^2^

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~  = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_shell.R_cullis.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.R_cullis
_phasing_MIR_shell.R_cullis.description:
Residual factor R~cullis~ for centric reflections in this shell.

sum| |Fph~obs~ +/- Fp~obs~| - Fh~calc~ | R~cullis~ = ---------------------------------------- sum|Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~|

Fp~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native Fph~obs~ = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fh~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the heavy-atom model

sum is taken over the specified reflections

Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G. & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc. London Ser. A, 265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_shell.R_kraut.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.R_kraut
_phasing_MIR_shell.R_kraut.description:
Residual factor R~kraut~ for general reflections in this shell.

sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~| R~kraut~ = ------------------------- sum|Fph~obs~|

Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the derivative Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the derivative

sum is taken over the specified reflections

Ref: Kraut, J., Sieker, L. C., High, D. F. & Freer, S. T. (1962). Proc. Natl Acad. Sci. USA, 48, 1417-1424.

_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.reflns
_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns.description: The number of reflections in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_acentric
_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_acentric.description: The number of acentric reflections in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_anomalous.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_anomalous
_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_anomalous.description: The number of anomalous reflections in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_centric
_phasing_MIR_shell.reflns_centric.description: The number of centric reflections in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_loc_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_loc_centric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_loc_centric.description: record lack of closure from centric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_loc_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_loc_acentric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_loc_acentric.description: record lack of closure from acentric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_power_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_power_centric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_power_centric.description: record phasing power from centric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_power_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_power_acentric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_power_acentric.description: record phasing power from acentric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_kraut_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_kraut_centric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_kraut_centric.description: record R_Kraut from from centric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_kraut_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_kraut_acentric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_kraut_acentric.description: record R_kraut from from acentric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_cullis_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_cullis_centric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_cullis_centric.description: record R_Cullis from from centric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_cullis_acentri.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_cullis_acentric
_phasing_MIR_shell.pdbx_R_cullis_acentri.description: record R_Cullis from from acentric reflection for each shell.

_phasing_averaging.details.name: _phasing_averaging.details
_phasing_averaging.details.description: A description of special aspects of the averaging process.

_phasing_averaging.entry_id.name: _phasing_averaging.entry_id
_phasing_averaging.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_phasing_averaging.method.name: _phasing_averaging.method
_phasing_averaging.method.description:
A description of the phase-averaging phasing method used to phase this structure.

Note that this is not the computer program used, which is described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method itself.

This data item should be used to describe significant methodological options used within the phase-averaging program.

_phasing_isomorphous.details.name: _phasing_isomorphous.details
_phasing_isomorphous.details.description: A description of special aspects of the isomorphous phasing.
examples:
Residues 13-18 were eliminated from the
 starting model as it was anticipated that
 binding of the inhibitor would cause a
 structural rearrangement in this part of the
 structure.

_phasing_isomorphous.entry_id.name: _phasing_isomorphous.entry_id
_phasing_isomorphous.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_phasing_isomorphous.method.name: _phasing_isomorphous.method
_phasing_isomorphous.method.description:
A description of the isomorphous-phasing method used to phase this structure.

Note that this is not the computer program used, which is described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method itself.

This data item should be used to describe significant methodological options used within the isomorphous phasing program.
examples:
Iterative threefold averaging alternating with
 phase extension by 0.5 reciprocal lattice
 units per cycle.

_phasing_isomorphous.parent.name: _phasing_isomorphous.parent
_phasing_isomorphous.parent.description: Reference to the structure used to generate starting phases if the structure referenced in this data block was phased by virtue of being isomorphous to a known structure (e.g. a mutant that crystallizes in the same space group as the wild-type protein.)

_phasing_set.cell_angle_alpha.name: _phasing_set.cell_angle_alpha
_phasing_set.cell_angle_alpha.description: Unit-cell angle alpha for this data set in degrees.

_phasing_set.cell_angle_beta.name: _phasing_set.cell_angle_beta
_phasing_set.cell_angle_beta.description: Unit-cell angle beta for this data set in degrees.

_phasing_set.cell_angle_gamma.name: _phasing_set.cell_angle_gamma
_phasing_set.cell_angle_gamma.description: Unit-cell angle gamma for this data set in degrees.

_phasing_set.cell_length_a.name: _phasing_set.cell_length_a
_phasing_set.cell_length_a.description: Unit-cell length a for this data set in angstroms.

_phasing_set.cell_length_b.name: _phasing_set.cell_length_b
_phasing_set.cell_length_b.description: Unit-cell length b for this data set in angstroms.

_phasing_set.cell_length_c.name: _phasing_set.cell_length_c
_phasing_set.cell_length_c.description: Unit-cell length c for this data set in angstroms.

_phasing_set.detector_specific.name: _phasing_set.detector_specific
_phasing_set.detector_specific.description: The particular radiation detector. In general, this will be a manufacturer, description, model number or some combination of these.

_phasing_set.detector_type.name: _phasing_set.detector_type
_phasing_set.detector_type.description: The general class of the radiation detector.

_phasing_set.id.name: _phasing_set.id
_phasing_set.id.description:
The value of _phasing_set.id must uniquely identify a record in the PHASING_SET list.

Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique identifier.

_phasing_set.radiation_source_specific.name: _phasing_set.radiation_source_specific
_phasing_set.radiation_source_specific.description: The particular source of radiation. In general, this will be a manufacturer, description, or model number (or some combination of these) for laboratory sources and an institution name and beamline name for synchrotron sources.

_phasing_set.radiation_wavelength.name: _phasing_set.radiation_wavelength
_phasing_set.radiation_wavelength.description: The mean wavelength of the radiation used to measure this data set.

_phasing_set.temp.name: _phasing_set.temp
_phasing_set.temp.description: The temperature in kelvins at which the data set was measured.

_phasing_set.pdbx_temp_details.name: _phasing_set.pdbx_temp_details
_phasing_set.pdbx_temp_details.description: The value of _phasing_set.pdbx_temp_details describes any special details about the data collection temperature for this phasing data set.

_phasing_set.pdbx_d_res_high.name: _phasing_set.pdbx_d_res_high
_phasing_set.pdbx_d_res_high.description: The smallest value in angstroms for the interplanar spacings for the reflections in this shell. This is called the highest resolution.

_phasing_set.pdbx_d_res_low.name: _phasing_set.pdbx_d_res_low
_phasing_set.pdbx_d_res_low.description: The highest value in angstroms for the interplanar spacings for the reflections in this shell. This is called the lowest resolution.

_phasing_set_refln.set_id.name: _phasing_set_refln.set_id
_phasing_set_refln.set_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _phasing_set.id in the PHASING_SET category.

_phasing_set_refln.F_meas.name: _phasing_set_refln.F_meas
_phasing_set_refln.F_meas.description: The measured value of the structure factor for this reflection in this data set in electrons.

_phasing_set_refln.F_meas_au.name: _phasing_set_refln.F_meas_au
_phasing_set_refln.F_meas_au.description: The measured value of the structure factor for this reflection in this data set in arbitrary units.

_phasing_set_refln.F_meas_sigma.name: _phasing_set_refln.F_meas_sigma
_phasing_set_refln.F_meas_sigma.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_set_refln.F_meas in electrons.

_phasing_set_refln.F_meas_sigma_au.name: _phasing_set_refln.F_meas_sigma_au
_phasing_set_refln.F_meas_sigma_au.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _phasing_set_refln.F_meas_au in arbitrary units.

_phasing_set_refln.index_h.name: _phasing_set_refln.index_h
_phasing_set_refln.index_h.description: Miller index h of this reflection in this data set.

_phasing_set_refln.index_k.name: _phasing_set_refln.index_k
_phasing_set_refln.index_k.description: Miller index k of this reflection in this data set.

_phasing_set_refln.index_l.name: _phasing_set_refln.index_l
_phasing_set_refln.index_l.description: Miller index l of this reflection in this data set.

_publ.entry_id.name: _publ.entry_id
_publ.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_publ.contact_author.name: _publ.contact_author
_publ.contact_author.description: The name and address of the author submitting the manuscript and data block. This is the person contacted by the journal editorial staff. It is preferable to use the separate data items _publ.contact_author_name and _publ.contact_author_address.
examples:
Professor George Ferguson
 Department of Chemistry and Biochemistry
 University of Guelph
 Ontario
 Canada
 N1G 2W1

_publ.contact_author_address.name: _publ.contact_author_address
_publ.contact_author_address.description: The address of the author submitting the manuscript and data block. This is the person contacted by the journal editorial staff.
examples:
Department of Chemistry and Biochemistry
 University of Guelph
 Ontario
 Canada
 N1G 2W1

_publ.contact_author_email.name: _publ.contact_author_email
_publ.contact_author_email.description: E-mail address in a form recognizable to international networks. The format of e-mail addresses is given in Section 3.4, Address Specification, of Internet Message Format, RFC 2822, P. Resnick (Editor), Network Standards Group, April 2001.

_publ.contact_author_fax.name: _publ.contact_author_fax
_publ.contact_author_fax.description:
Facsimile telephone number of the author submitting the manuscript and data block.

The recommended style starts with the international dialing prefix, followed by the area code in parentheses, followed by the local number with no spaces. The earlier convention of including the international dialing prefix in parentheses is no longer recommended.

_publ.contact_author_name.name: _publ.contact_author_name
_publ.contact_author_name.description: The name of the author submitting the manuscript and data block. This is the person contacted by the journal editorial staff.

_publ.contact_author_phone.name: _publ.contact_author_phone
_publ.contact_author_phone.description:
Telephone number of the author submitting the manuscript and data block.

The recommended style starts with the international dialing prefix, followed by the area code in parentheses, followed by the local number and any extension number prefixed by 'x', with no spaces. The earlier convention of including the international dialing prefix in parentheses is no longer recommended.

_publ.contact_letter.name: _publ.contact_letter
_publ.contact_letter.description: A letter submitted to the journal editor by the contact author.

_publ.manuscript_creation.name: _publ.manuscript_creation
_publ.manuscript_creation.description: A description of the word-processor package and computer used to create the word-processed manuscript stored as _publ.manuscript_processed.
examples: Tex file created by FrameMaker on a Sun 3/280

_publ.manuscript_processed.name: _publ.manuscript_processed
_publ.manuscript_processed.description: The full manuscript of a paper (excluding possibly the figures and the tables) output in ASCII characters from a word processor. Information about the generation of this data item must be specified in the data item _publ.manuscript_creation.

_publ.manuscript_text.name: _publ.manuscript_text
_publ.manuscript_text.description: The full manuscript of a paper (excluding figures and possibly the tables) output as standard ASCII text.

_publ.requested_category.name: _publ.requested_category
_publ.requested_category.description: The category of paper submitted. For submission to Acta Crystallographica Section C or Acta Crystallographica Section E, ONLY the codes indicated for use with these journals should be used.
enums: Addenda and Errata (Acta C, Acta E)
CIF-access paper - inorganic (Acta C) (no longer in use)
CIF-access paper - metal-organic (Acta C) (no longer in use)
CIF-access paper - organic (Acta C) (no longer in use)

_publ.requested_coeditor_name.name: _publ.requested_coeditor_name
_publ.requested_coeditor_name.description: The name of the co-editor whom the authors would like to handle the submitted manuscript.

_publ.requested_journal.name: _publ.requested_journal
_publ.requested_journal.description: The name of the journal to which the manuscript is being submitted.

_publ.section_abstract.name: _publ.section_abstract
_publ.section_abstract.description: The abstract section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_acknowledgements.name: _publ.section_acknowledgements
_publ.section_acknowledgements.description: The acknowledgements section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_comment.name: _publ.section_comment
_publ.section_comment.description: The comment section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_discussion.name: _publ.section_discussion
_publ.section_discussion.description: The discussion section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_experimental.name: _publ.section_experimental
_publ.section_experimental.description: The experimental section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed. The _publ.section_exptl_prep, _publ.section_exptl_solution and _publ.section_exptl_refinement items are preferred for separating the chemical preparation, structure solution and refinement aspects of the description of the experiment.

_publ.section_exptl_prep.name: _publ.section_exptl_prep
_publ.section_exptl_prep.description: The experimental preparation section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_exptl_refinement.name: _publ.section_exptl_refinement
_publ.section_exptl_refinement.description: The experimental refinement section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_exptl_solution.name: _publ.section_exptl_solution
_publ.section_exptl_solution.description: The experimental solution section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_figure_captions.name: _publ.section_figure_captions
_publ.section_figure_captions.description: The figure captions section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_introduction.name: _publ.section_introduction
_publ.section_introduction.description: The introduction section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_references.name: _publ.section_references
_publ.section_references.description: The references section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_synopsis.name: _publ.section_synopsis
_publ.section_synopsis.description: The synopsis section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_table_legends.name: _publ.section_table_legends
_publ.section_table_legends.description: The table legends section of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_title.name: _publ.section_title
_publ.section_title.description: The title of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ.section_title_footnote.name: _publ.section_title_footnote
_publ.section_title_footnote.description: The footnote to the title of a manuscript if the manuscript is submitted in parts. As an alternative see _publ.manuscript_text and _publ.manuscript_processed.

_publ_author.address.name: _publ_author.address
_publ_author.address.description: The address of a publication author. If there is more than one author this is looped with _publ_author.name.
examples:
Department
 Institute
 Street
 City and postcode
 COUNTRY

_publ_author.email.name: _publ_author.email
_publ_author.email.description: The e-mail address of a publication author. If there is more than one author, this will be looped with _publ_author.name. The format of e-mail addresses is given in Section 3.4, Address Specification, of  Internet Message Format, RFC 2822, P. Resnick (Editor), Network Standards Group, April 2001.

_publ_author.footnote.name: _publ_author.footnote
_publ_author.footnote.description: A footnote accompanying an author's name in the list of authors of a paper. Typically indicates sabbatical address, additional affiliations or date of decease.
examples: On leave from U. Western Australia

_publ_author.name.name: _publ_author.name
_publ_author.name.description: The name of a publication author. If there are multiple authors this will be looped with _publ_author.address. The family name(s), followed by a comma and including any dynastic components, precedes the first names or initials.

_publ_author.id_iucr.name: _publ_author.id_iucr
_publ_author.id_iucr.description: Identifier in the IUCr contact database of a publication author.  This identifier may be available from the World Directory of Crystallographers (http://wdc.iucr.org).

_publ_body.contents.name: _publ_body.contents
_publ_body.contents.description: A text section of a paper.

_publ_body.element.name: _publ_body.element
_publ_body.element.description: The functional role of the associated text section.

_publ_body.format.name: _publ_body.format
_publ_body.format.description: Code indicating the appropriate typesetting conventions for accented characters and special symbols in the text section.

_publ_body.label.name: _publ_body.label
_publ_body.label.description: Code identifying the section of text.

_publ_body.title.name: _publ_body.title
_publ_body.title.description: Title of the associated section of text.

_publ_manuscript_incl.entry_id.name: _publ_manuscript_incl.entry_id
_publ_manuscript_incl.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_publ_manuscript_incl.extra_defn.name: _publ_manuscript_incl.extra_defn
_publ_manuscript_incl.extra_defn.description: Flags whether the corresponding data item marked for inclusion in a journal request list is a standard CIF definition or not.
enums: not a standard CIF data name

_publ_manuscript_incl.extra_info.name: _publ_manuscript_incl.extra_info
_publ_manuscript_incl.extra_info.description: A short note indicating the reason why the author wishes the corresponding data item marked for inclusion in the journal request list to be published.
examples: the limited data is a problem here
a new data quantity needed here

_publ_manuscript_incl.extra_item.name: _publ_manuscript_incl.extra_item
_publ_manuscript_incl.extra_item.description: Specifies the inclusion of specific data into a manuscript which are not normally requested by the journal. The values of this item are the extra data names (which MUST be enclosed in single quotes) that will be added to the journal request list.

_refine.aniso_B[1][1].name: _refine.aniso_B[1][1]
_refine.aniso_B[1][1].description: The [1][1] element of the matrix that defines the overall anisotropic displacement model if one was refined for this structure.

_refine.aniso_B[1][2].name: _refine.aniso_B[1][2]
_refine.aniso_B[1][2].description: The [1][2] element of the matrix that defines the overall anisotropic displacement model if one was refined for this structure.

_refine.aniso_B[1][3].name: _refine.aniso_B[1][3]
_refine.aniso_B[1][3].description: The [1][3] element of the matrix that defines the overall anisotropic displacement model if one was refined for this structure.

_refine.aniso_B[2][2].name: _refine.aniso_B[2][2]
_refine.aniso_B[2][2].description: The [2][2] element of the matrix that defines the overall anisotropic displacement model if one was refined for this structure.

_refine.aniso_B[2][3].name: _refine.aniso_B[2][3]
_refine.aniso_B[2][3].description: The [2][3] element of the matrix that defines the overall anisotropic displacement model if one was refined for this structure.

_refine.aniso_B[3][3].name: _refine.aniso_B[3][3]
_refine.aniso_B[3][3].description: The [3][3] element of the matrix that defines the overall anisotropic displacement model if one was refined for this structure.

_refine.B_iso_max.name: _refine.B_iso_max
_refine.B_iso_max.description: The maximum isotropic displacement parameter (B value) found in the coordinate set.

_refine.B_iso_mean.name: _refine.B_iso_mean
_refine.B_iso_mean.description: The mean isotropic displacement parameter (B value) for the coordinate set.

_refine.B_iso_min.name: _refine.B_iso_min
_refine.B_iso_min.description: The minimum isotropic displacement parameter (B value) found in the coordinate set.

_refine.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc.name: _refine.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc
_refine.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc.description:
The correlation coefficient between the observed and calculated structure factors for reflections included in the refinement.

The correlation coefficient is scale-independent and gives an idea of the quality of the refined model.

sum~i~(Fo~i~ Fc~i~ - <Fo><Fc>) R~corr~ = ------------------------------------------------------------ SQRT{sum~i~(Fo~i~)^2^-<Fo>^2^} SQRT{sum~i~(Fc~i~)^2^-<Fc>^2^}

Fo = observed structure factors Fc = calculated structure factors <>   denotes average value

summation is over reflections included in the refinement

_refine.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc_free.name: _refine.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc_free
_refine.correlation_coeff_Fo_to_Fc_free.description:
The correlation coefficient between the observed and calculated structure factors for reflections not included in the refinement (free reflections).

The correlation coefficient is scale-independent and gives an idea of the quality of the refined model.

sum~i~(Fo~i~ Fc~i~ - <Fo><Fc>) R~corr~ = ------------------------------------------------------------ SQRT{sum~i~(Fo~i~)^2^-<Fo>^2^} SQRT{sum~i~(Fc~i~)^2^-<Fc>^2^}

Fo  = observed structure factors Fc  = calculated structure factors <>    denotes average value

summation is over reflections not included in the refinement (free reflections)

_refine.details.name: _refine.details
_refine.details.description: Description of special aspects of the refinement process.

_refine.diff_density_max.name: _refine.diff_density_max
_refine.diff_density_max.description: The maximum value of the electron density in the final difference Fourier map.

_refine.diff_density_max_esd.name: _refine.diff_density_max_esd
_refine.diff_density_max_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.diff_density_max.

_refine.diff_density_min.name: _refine.diff_density_min
_refine.diff_density_min.description: The minimum value of the electron density in the final difference Fourier map.

_refine.diff_density_min_esd.name: _refine.diff_density_min_esd
_refine.diff_density_min_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.diff_density_min.

_refine.diff_density_rms.name: _refine.diff_density_rms
_refine.diff_density_rms.description: The root-mean-square-deviation of the electron density in the final difference Fourier map. This value is measured with respect to the arithmetic mean density and is derived from summations over each grid point in the asymmetric unit of the cell. This quantity is useful for assessing the significance of the values of _refine.diff_density_min and _refine.diff_density_max, and also for defining suitable contour levels.

_refine.diff_density_rms_esd.name: _refine.diff_density_rms_esd
_refine.diff_density_rms_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.diff_density_rms.

_refine.entry_id.name: _refine.entry_id
_refine.entry_id.description: This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category.

_refine.pdbx_refine_id.name: _refine.pdbx_refine_id
_refine.pdbx_refine_id.description: This data item uniquely identifies a refinement within an entry. _refine.pdbx_refine_id can be used to distinguish the results of joint refinements.

_refine.ls_abs_structure_details.name: _refine.ls_abs_structure_details
_refine.ls_abs_structure_details.description: The nature of the absolute structure and how it was determined. For example, this may describe the Friedel pairs used.

_refine.ls_abs_structure_Flack.name: _refine.ls_abs_structure_Flack
_refine.ls_abs_structure_Flack.description:
The measure of absolute structure (enantiomorph or polarity) as defined by Flack (1983).

For centrosymmetric structures, the only permitted value, if the data name is present, is 'inapplicable', represented by '.' .

For noncentrosymmetric structures the value must lie in the 99.97% Gaussian confidence interval  -3u =< x =< 1 + 3u and a standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) u must be supplied. The item range of [0.0:1.0] is correctly interpreted as meaning (0.0 - 3u) =< x =< (1.0 + 3u).

Ref: Flack, H. D. (1983). Acta Cryst. A39, 876-881.

_refine.ls_abs_structure_Flack_esd.name: _refine.ls_abs_structure_Flack_esd
_refine.ls_abs_structure_Flack_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.ls_abs_structure_Flack.

_refine.ls_abs_structure_Rogers.name: _refine.ls_abs_structure_Rogers
_refine.ls_abs_structure_Rogers.description:
The measure of absolute structure (enantiomorph or polarity) as defined by Rogers.

The value must lie in the 99.97% Gaussian confidence interval -1 -3u =< \h =< 1 + 3u and a standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) u must be supplied. The item range of [-1.0, 1.0] is correctly interpreted as meaning (-1.0 - 3u) =< \h =< (1.0 + 3u).

Ref: Rogers, D. (1981). Acta Cryst. A37, 734-741.

_refine.ls_abs_structure_Rogers_esd.name: _refine.ls_abs_structure_Rogers_esd
_refine.ls_abs_structure_Rogers_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.ls_abs_structure_Rogers.

_refine.ls_d_res_high.name: _refine.ls_d_res_high
_refine.ls_d_res_high.description: The smallest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data used in the refinement in angstroms. This is called the highest resolution.

_refine.ls_d_res_low.name: _refine.ls_d_res_low
_refine.ls_d_res_low.description: The largest value for the interplanar spacings for the reflection data used in the refinement in angstroms. This is called the lowest resolution.

_refine.ls_extinction_coef.name: _refine.ls_extinction_coef
_refine.ls_extinction_coef.description:
The extinction coefficient used to calculate the correction factor applied to the structure-factor data. The nature of the extinction coefficient is given in the definitions of _refine.ls_extinction_expression and _refine.ls_extinction_method.

For the 'Zachariasen' method it is the r* value; for the 'Becker-Coppens type 1 isotropic' method it is the 'g' value, and for 'Becker-Coppens type 2 isotropic' corrections it is the 'rho' value. Note that the magnitude of these values is usually of the order of 10000.

Ref:  Becker, P. J. & Coppens, P. (1974). Acta Cryst. A30, 129-47, 148-153. Zachariasen, W. H. (1967). Acta Cryst. 23, 558-564. Larson, A. C. (1967). Acta Cryst. 23, 664-665.

_refine.ls_extinction_coef_esd.name: _refine.ls_extinction_coef_esd
_refine.ls_extinction_coef_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.ls_extinction_coef.

_refine.ls_extinction_expression.name: _refine.ls_extinction_expression
_refine.ls_extinction_expression.description: A description of or reference to the extinction-correction equation used to apply the data item _refine.ls_extinction_coef. This information must be sufficient to reproduce the extinction-correction factors applied to the structure factors.
examples:
Larson, A. C. (1970). "Crystallographic Computing", edited by
 F. R. Ahmed. Eq. (22), p.292. Copenhagen: Munksgaard.

_refine.ls_extinction_method.name: _refine.ls_extinction_method
_refine.ls_extinction_method.description:
A description of the extinction-correction method applied. This description should include information about the correction method, either 'Becker-Coppens' or 'Zachariasen'.  The latter is sometimes referred to as the 'Larson' method even though it employs Zachariasen's formula.

The Becker-Coppens procedure is referred to as 'type 1' when correcting secondary extinction dominated by the mosaic spread; as 'type 2' when secondary extinction is dominated by particle size and includes a primary extinction component; and as 'mixed' when there is a mixture of types 1 and 2.

For the Becker-Coppens method, it is also necessary to set the mosaic distribution as either 'Gaussian' or 'Lorentzian' and the nature of the extinction as 'isotropic' or 'anisotropic'. Note that if either the 'mixed' or 'anisotropic' corrections are applied, the multiple coefficients cannot be contained in *_extinction_coef and must be listed in _refine.details.

Ref: Becker, P. J. & Coppens, P. (1974). Acta Cryst. A30, 129-147, 148-153. Zachariasen, W. H. (1967). Acta Cryst. 23, 558- 564. Larson, A. C. (1967). Acta Cryst. 23, 664-665.

_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all.name: _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all
_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all.description:
The least-squares goodness-of-fit parameter S for all data after the final cycle of refinement. Ideally, account should be taken of parameters restrained in the least-squares refinement. See also the definition of _refine.ls_restrained_S_all.

( sum|w |Y~obs~ - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ S = ( ---------------------------- ) (       N~ref~ - N~param~      )

Y~obs~   = the observed coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) Y~calc~  = the calculated coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) w        = the least-squares reflection weight [1/(e.s.d. squared)]

N~ref~   = the number of reflections used in the refinement N~param~ = the number of refined parameters

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all_esd.name: _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all_esd
_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all.

_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs.name: _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs
_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs.description:
The least-squares goodness-of-fit parameter S for reflection data classified as 'observed' (see _reflns.observed_criterion) after the final cycle of refinement. Ideally, account should be taken of parameters restrained in the least-squares refinement. See also the definition of _refine.ls_restrained_S_obs.

( sum|w |Y~obs~ - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ S = ( ---------------------------- ) (       N~ref~ - N~param~      )

Y~obs~   = the observed coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) Y~calc~  = the calculated coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) w        = the least-squares reflection weight [1/(e.s.d. squared)]

N~ref~   = the number of reflections used in the refinement N~param~ = the number of refined parameters

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs_esd.name: _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs_esd
_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs_esd.description: The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs.

_refine.ls_hydrogen_treatment.name: _refine.ls_hydrogen_treatment
_refine.ls_hydrogen_treatment.description: Treatment of hydrogen atoms in the least-squares refinement.

_refine.ls_matrix_type.name: _refine.ls_matrix_type
_refine.ls_matrix_type.description: Type of matrix used to accumulate the least-squares derivatives.
enums: full with fixed elements per cycle

_refine.ls_number_constraints.name: _refine.ls_number_constraints
_refine.ls_number_constraints.description: The number of constrained (non-refined or dependent) parameters in the least-squares process. These may be due to symmetry or any other constraint process (e.g. rigid-body refinement). See also _atom_site.constraints and _atom_site.refinement_flags. A general description of constraints may appear in _refine.details.

_refine.ls_number_parameters.name: _refine.ls_number_parameters
_refine.ls_number_parameters.description: The number of parameters refined in the least-squares process. If possible, this number should include some contribution from the restrained parameters. The restrained parameters are distinct from the constrained parameters (where one or more parameters are linearly dependent on the refined value of another). Least-squares restraints often depend on geometry or energy considerations and this makes their direct contribution to this number, and to the goodness-of-fit calculation, difficult to assess.

_refine.ls_number_reflns_all.name: _refine.ls_number_reflns_all
_refine.ls_number_reflns_all.description: The number of reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low.

_refine.ls_number_reflns_obs.name: _refine.ls_number_reflns_obs
_refine.ls_number_reflns_obs.description: The number of reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion.

_refine.ls_number_reflns_R_free.name: _refine.ls_number_reflns_R_free
_refine.ls_number_reflns_R_free.description: The number of reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were used as the test reflections (i.e. were excluded from the refinement) when the refinement included the calculation of a 'free' R factor. Details of how reflections were assigned to the working and test sets are given in _reflns.R_free_details.

_refine.ls_number_reflns_R_work.name: _refine.ls_number_reflns_R_work
_refine.ls_number_reflns_R_work.description: The number of reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were used as the working reflections (i.e. were included in the refinement) when the refinement included the calculation of a 'free' R factor. Details of how reflections were assigned to the working and test sets are given in _reflns.R_free_details.

_refine.ls_number_restraints.name: _refine.ls_number_restraints
_refine.ls_number_restraints.description: The number of restrained parameters. These are parameters which are not directly dependent on another refined parameter. Restrained parameters often involve geometry or energy dependencies. See also _atom_site.constraints and _atom_site.refinement_flags. A general description of refinement constraints may appear in _refine.details.

_refine.ls_percent_reflns_obs.name: _refine.ls_percent_reflns_obs
_refine.ls_percent_reflns_obs.description: The number of reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, expressed as a percentage of the number of geometrically observable reflections that satisfy the resolution limits.

_refine.ls_percent_reflns_R_free.name: _refine.ls_percent_reflns_R_free
_refine.ls_percent_reflns_R_free.description: The number of reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were used as the test reflections (i.e. were excluded from the refinement) when the refinement included the calculation of a 'free' R factor, expressed as a percentage of the number of geometrically observable reflections that satisfy the resolution limits.

_refine.ls_R_factor_all.name: _refine.ls_R_factor_all
_refine.ls_R_factor_all.description:
Residual factor R for all reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low.

sum|F~obs~ - F~calc~| R = --------------------- sum|F~obs~|

F~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitudes F~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitudes

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_R_factor_obs.name: _refine.ls_R_factor_obs
_refine.ls_R_factor_obs.description:
Residual factor R for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion.

_refine.ls_R_factor_obs should not be confused with _refine.ls_R_factor_R_work; the former reports the results of a refinement in which all observed reflections were used, the latter a refinement in which a subset of the observed reflections were excluded from refinement for the calculation of a 'free' R factor. However, it would be meaningful to quote both values if a 'free' R factor were calculated for most of the refinement, but all of the observed reflections were used in the final rounds of refinement; such a protocol should be explained in _refine.details.

sum|F~obs~ - F~calc~| R = --------------------- sum|F~obs~|

F~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitudes F~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitudes

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_R_factor_R_free.name: _refine.ls_R_factor_R_free
_refine.ls_R_factor_R_free.description:
Residual factor R for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were used as the test reflections (i.e. were excluded from the refinement) when the refinement included the calculation of a 'free' R factor. Details of how reflections were assigned to the working and test sets are given in _reflns.R_free_details.

sum|F~obs~ - F~calc~| R = --------------------- sum|F~obs~|

F~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitudes F~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitudes

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_R_factor_R_free_error.name: _refine.ls_R_factor_R_free_error
_refine.ls_R_factor_R_free_error.description: The estimated error in _refine.ls_R_factor_R_free. The method used to estimate the error is described in the item _refine.ls_R_factor_R_free_error_details.

_refine.ls_R_factor_R_free_error_details.name: _refine.ls_R_factor_R_free_error_details
_refine.ls_R_factor_R_free_error_details.description: Special aspects of the method used to estimated the error in _refine.ls_R_factor_R_free.

_refine.ls_R_factor_R_work.name: _refine.ls_R_factor_R_work
_refine.ls_R_factor_R_work.description:
Residual factor R for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were used as the working reflections (i.e. were included in the refinement)  when the refinement included the calculation of a 'free' R factor. Details of how reflections were assigned to the working and test sets are given in _reflns.R_free_details.

_refine.ls_R_factor_obs should not be confused with _refine.ls_R_factor_R_work; the former reports the results of a refinement in which all observed reflections were used, the latter a refinement in which a subset of the observed reflections were excluded from refinement for the calculation of a 'free' R factor. However, it would be meaningful to quote both values if a 'free' R factor were calculated for most of the refinement, but all of the observed reflections were used in the final rounds of refinement; such a protocol should be explained in _refine.details.

sum|F~obs~ - F~calc~| R = --------------------- sum|F~obs~|

F~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitudes F~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitudes

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_R_Fsqd_factor_obs.name: _refine.ls_R_Fsqd_factor_obs
_refine.ls_R_Fsqd_factor_obs.description:
Residual factor R(Fsqd) for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, calculated on the squares of the observed and calculated structure-factor amplitudes.

sum|F~obs~^2^ - F~calc~^2^| R(Fsqd) = --------------------------- sum|F~obs~^2^|

F~obs~^2^  = squares of the observed structure-factor amplitudes F~calc~^2^ = squares of the calculated structure-factor amplitudes

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_R_I_factor_obs.name: _refine.ls_R_I_factor_obs
_refine.ls_R_I_factor_obs.description:
Residual factor R(I) for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, calculated on the estimated reflection intensities.

This is most often calculated in Rietveld refinements against powder data, where it is referred to as R~B~ or R~Bragg~.

sum|I~obs~ - I~calc~| R(I) = --------------------- sum|I~obs~|

I~obs~  = the net observed intensities I~calc~ = the net calculated intensities

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_redundancy_reflns_all.name: _refine.ls_redundancy_reflns_all
_refine.ls_redundancy_reflns_all.description: The ratio of the total number of observations of the reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low to the number of crystallographically unique reflections that satisfy the same limits.

_refine.ls_redundancy_reflns_obs.name: _refine.ls_redundancy_reflns_obs
_refine.ls_redundancy_reflns_obs.description: The ratio of the total number of observations of the reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion to the number of crystallographically unique reflections that satisfy the same limits.

_refine.ls_restrained_S_all.name: _refine.ls_restrained_S_all
_refine.ls_restrained_S_all.description:
The least-squares goodness-of-fit parameter S' for all reflections after the final cycle of least-squares refinement. This parameter explicitly includes the restraints applied in the least-squares process. See also the definition of _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_all.

(   sum   |w    |Y~obs~  - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ ( + sum~r~|w~r~ |P~calc~ - P~targ~|^2^| ) S' = ( ------------------------------------- ) (      N~ref~ + N~restr~ - N~param~     )

Y~obs~   = the observed coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) Y~calc~  = the calculated coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) w        = the least-squares reflection weight [1/(e.s.d. squared)]

P~calc~  = the calculated restraint values P~targ~  = the target restraint values w~r~     = the restraint weight

N~ref~   = the number of reflections used in the refinement (see _refine.ls_number_reflns_obs) N~restr~ = the number of restraints (see _refine.ls_number_restraints) N~param~ = the number of refined parameters (see _refine.ls_number_parameters)

sum  is taken over the specified reflections sumr is taken over the restraints

_refine.ls_restrained_S_obs.name: _refine.ls_restrained_S_obs
_refine.ls_restrained_S_obs.description:
The least-squares goodness-of-fit parameter S' for reflection data classified as observed (see _reflns.observed_criterion) after the final cycle of least-squares refinement. This parameter explicitly includes the restraints applied in the least-squares process. See also the definition of _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_obs.

(   sum   |w    |Y~obs~  - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ ( + sum~r~|w~r~ |P~calc~ - P~targ~|^2^| ) S' = ( ------------------------------------- ) (      N~ref~ + N~restr~ - N~param~     )

Y~obs~   = the observed coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) Y~calc~  = the calculated coefficients (see _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef) w        = the least-squares reflection weight [1/(e.s.d. squared)]

P~calc~  = the calculated restraint values P~targ~  = the target restraint values w~r~     = the restraint weight

N~ref~   = the number of reflections used in the refinement (see _refine.ls_number_reflns_obs) N~restr~ = the number of restraints (see _refine.ls_number_restraints) N~param~ = the number of refined parameters (see _refine.ls_number_parameters)

sum  is taken over the specified reflections sumr is taken over the restraints

_refine.ls_shift_over_esd_max.name: _refine.ls_shift_over_esd_max
_refine.ls_shift_over_esd_max.description: The largest ratio of the final least-squares parameter shift to the final standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation).

_refine.ls_shift_over_esd_mean.name: _refine.ls_shift_over_esd_mean
_refine.ls_shift_over_esd_mean.description: The average ratio of the final least-squares parameter shift to the final standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation).

_refine.ls_structure_factor_coef.name: _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef
_refine.ls_structure_factor_coef.description: Structure-factor coefficient |F|, F^2^ or I used in the least- squares refinement process.

_refine.ls_weighting_details.name: _refine.ls_weighting_details
_refine.ls_weighting_details.description: A description of special aspects of the weighting scheme used in least-squares refinement. Used to describe the weighting when the value of _refine.ls_weighting_scheme is specified as 'calc'.
examples:
Sigdel model of Konnert-Hendrickson:
 Sigdel =
 Afsig +  Bfsig*(sin(theta)/lambda-1/6)
 Afsig = 22.0, Bfsig = 150.0
   at the beginning of refinement.
 Afsig = 16.0, Bfsig =  60.0
   at the end of refinement.

_refine.ls_weighting_scheme.name: _refine.ls_weighting_scheme
_refine.ls_weighting_scheme.description: The weighting scheme applied in the least-squares process. The standard code may be followed by a description of the weight (but see _refine.ls_weighting_details for a preferred approach).

_refine.ls_wR_factor_all.name: _refine.ls_wR_factor_all
_refine.ls_wR_factor_all.description:
Weighted residual factor wR for all reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low.

( sum|w |Y~obs~ - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ wR = ( ---------------------------- ) (        sum|w Y~obs~^2^|      )

Y~obs~  = the observed amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef Y~calc~ = the calculated amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef w       = the least-squares weight

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_wR_factor_obs.name: _refine.ls_wR_factor_obs
_refine.ls_wR_factor_obs.description:
Weighted residual factor wR for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion.

( sum|w |Y~obs~ - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ wR = ( ---------------------------- ) (        sum|w Y~obs~^2^|      )

Y~obs~  = the observed amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef Y~calc~ = the calculated amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef w       = the least-squares weight

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_wR_factor_R_free.name: _refine.ls_wR_factor_R_free
_refine.ls_wR_factor_R_free.description:
Weighted residual factor wR for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were used as the test reflections (i.e. were excluded from the refinement) when the refinement included the calculation of a 'free' R factor. Details of how reflections were assigned to the working and test sets are given in _reflns.R_free_details.

( sum|w |Y~obs~ - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ wR = ( ---------------------------- ) (        sum|w Y~obs~^2^|      )

Y~obs~  = the observed amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef Y~calc~ = the calculated amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef w       = the least-squares weight

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_wR_factor_R_work.name: _refine.ls_wR_factor_R_work
_refine.ls_wR_factor_R_work.description:
Weighted residual factor wR for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were used as the working reflections (i.e. were included in the refinement) when the refinement included the calculation of a 'free' R factor. Details of how reflections were assigned to the working and test sets are given in _reflns.R_free_details.

( sum|w |Y~obs~ - Y~calc~|^2^| )^1/2^ wR = ( ---------------------------- ) (        sum|w Y~obs~^2^|      )

Y~obs~  = the observed amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef Y~calc~ = the calculated amplitude specified by _refine.ls_structure_factor_coef w       = the least-squares weight

sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.occupancy_max.name: _refine.occupancy_max
_refine.occupancy_max.description: The maximum value for occupancy found in the coordinate set.

_refine.occupancy_min.name: _refine.occupancy_min
_refine.occupancy_min.description: The minimum value for occupancy found in the coordinate set.

_refine.solvent_model_details.name: _refine.solvent_model_details
_refine.solvent_model_details.description: Special aspects of the solvent model used during refinement.

_refine.solvent_model_param_bsol.name: _refine.solvent_model_param_bsol
_refine.solvent_model_param_bsol.description:
The value of the BSOL solvent-model parameter describing the average isotropic displacement parameter of disordered solvent atoms.

This is one of the two parameters (the other is _refine.solvent_model_param_ksol) in Tronrud's method of modelling the contribution of bulk solvent to the scattering. The standard scale factor is modified according to the expression

k0 exp(-B0 * s^2^)[1-KSOL * exp(-BSOL * s^2^)]

where k0 and B0 are the scale factors for the protein.

Ref: Tronrud, D. E. (1997). Methods Enzymol. 277, 243-268.

_refine.solvent_model_param_ksol.name: _refine.solvent_model_param_ksol
_refine.solvent_model_param_ksol.description:
The value of the KSOL solvent-model parameter describing the ratio of the electron density in the bulk solvent to the electron density in the molecular solute.

This is one of the two parameters (the other is _refine.solvent_model_param_bsol) in Tronrud's method of modelling the contribution of bulk solvent to the scattering. The standard scale factor is modified according to the expression

k0 exp(-B0 * s^2^)[1-KSOL * exp(-BSOL * s^2^)]

where k0 and B0 are the scale factors for the protein.

Ref: Tronrud, D. E. (1997). Methods Enzymol. 277, 243-268.

_refine.correlation_coeff_I_to_Fcsqd_wor.name: _refine.correlation_coeff_I_to_Fcsqd_work
_refine.correlation_coeff_I_to_Fcsqd_wor.description:
The Pearson correlation coefficient between the observed (merged) intensities and the intensities calculated from a model for reflections included in the refinement (work reflections).

The correlation coefficient is scale-independent and gives an idea of the quality of the refined model.

<Io Ic> - <Io> <Ic> Corr = ---------------------------------------------- SQRT{<Io^2^>-<Io>^2^} SQRT{<Ic^2^>-<Ic>^2^}

Io = observed intensities Ic = |Fc|^2^ = calculated intensities <> denotes the average value, where the average is taken over the reflections included in the refinement (work reflections)

_refine.correlation_coeff_I_to_Fcsqd_fre.name: _refine.correlation_coeff_I_to_Fcsqd_free
_refine.correlation_coeff_I_to_Fcsqd_fre.description:
The Pearson correlation coefficient between the observed (merged) intensities and the intensities calculated from a model for reflections not included in the refinement (free reflections).

The correlation coefficient is scale-independent and gives an idea of the quality of the refined model.

<Io Ic> - <Io> <Ic> Corr = ---------------------------------------------- SQRT{<Io^2^>-<Io>^2^} SQRT{<Ic^2^>-<Ic>^2^}

Io = observed intensities Ic = |Fc|^2^ = calculated intensities <> denotes the average value, where the average is taken over the reflections not included in the refinement (free reflections)

_refine.pdbx_R_complete.name: _refine.pdbx_R_complete
_refine.pdbx_R_complete.description:
The crystallographic reliability index Rcomplete for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion

Ref: Luebben, J., Gruene, T., (2015). Proc.Nat.Acad.Sci. 112(29) 8999-9003

_refine.ls_R_factor_gt.name: _refine.ls_R_factor_gt
_refine.ls_R_factor_gt.description:
Residual factor for the reflections (with number given by _reflns.number_gt) judged significantly intense (i.e. satisfying the threshold specified by _reflns.threshold_expression) and included in the refinement. The reflections also satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low. This is the conventional R factor. See also _refine.ls_wR_factor_ definitions.

sum | F(obs) - F(calc) | R = ------------------------ sum | F(obs) |

F(obs)  = the observed structure-factor amplitudes F(calc) = the calculated structure-factor amplitudes

and the sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_gt.name: _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_gt
_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_gt.description:
The least-squares goodness-of-fit parameter S for significantly intense reflections (see _reflns.threshold_expression) after the final cycle of refinement. Ideally, account should be taken of parameters restrained in the least-squares refinement. See also _refine.ls_restrained_S_ definitions.

{  sum { w [ Y(obs) - Y(calc) ]^2^ }  }^1/2^ S = { ----------------------------------- } {            Nref - Nparam            }

Y(obs)  = the observed coefficients (see _refine_ls_structure_factor_coef) Y(calc) = the calculated coefficients (see _refine_ls_structure_factor_coef) w       = the least-squares reflection weight [1/(u^2^)] u       = standard uncertainty

Nref   = the number of reflections used in the refinement Nparam = the number of refined parameters

and the sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_ref.name: _refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_ref
_refine.ls_goodness_of_fit_ref.description:
The least-squares goodness-of-fit parameter S for all reflections included in the refinement after the final cycle of refinement. Ideally, account should be taken of parameters restrained in the least-squares refinement. See also _refine_ls_restrained_S_ definitions.

{  sum | w | Y(obs) - Y(calc) |^2^ |  }^1/2^ S = { ----------------------------------- } {            Nref - Nparam            }

Y(obs)  = the observed coefficients (see _refine_ls_structure_factor_coef) Y(calc) = the calculated coefficients (see _refine_ls_structure_factor_coef) w       = the least-squares reflection weight [1/(u^2^)] u       = standard uncertainty

Nref   = the number of reflections used in the refinement Nparam = the number of refined parameters

and the sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.ls_shift_over_su_max.name: _refine.ls_shift_over_su_max
_refine.ls_shift_over_su_max.description: The largest ratio of the final least-squares parameter shift to the final standard uncertainty.

_refine.ls_shift_over_su_max_lt.name: _refine.ls_shift_over_su_max_lt
_refine.ls_shift_over_su_max_lt.description: An upper limit for the largest ratio of the final least-squares parameter shift to the final standard uncertainty.  This item is used when the largest value of the shift divided by the final standard uncertainty is too small to measure.

_refine.ls_shift_over_su_mean.name: _refine.ls_shift_over_su_mean
_refine.ls_shift_over_su_mean.description: The average ratio of the final least-squares parameter shift to the final standard uncertainty.

_refine.ls_shift_over_su_mean_lt.name: _refine.ls_shift_over_su_mean_lt
_refine.ls_shift_over_su_mean_lt.description: An upper limit for the average ratio of the final least-squares parameter shift to the final standard uncertainty.  This item is used when the average value of the shift divided by the final standard uncertainty is too small to measure.

_refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_high_absF.name: _refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_high_absF
_refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_high_absF.description: Value of F at "high end" of data cutoff.

_refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_high_rms_absF.name: _refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_high_rms_absF
_refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_high_rms_absF.description: Value of RMS |F| used as high data cutoff.

_refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_low_absF.name: _refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_low_absF
_refine.pdbx_data_cutoff_low_absF.description: Value of F at "low end" of data cutoff.

_refine.pdbx_isotropic_thermal_model.name: _refine.pdbx_isotropic_thermal_model
_refine.pdbx_isotropic_thermal_model.description: Whether the structure was refined with indvidual isotropic, anisotropic or overall temperature factor.

_refine.pdbx_ls_cross_valid_method.name: _refine.pdbx_ls_cross_valid_method
_refine.pdbx_ls_cross_valid_method.description: Whether the cross validataion method was used through out or only at the end.

_refine.pdbx_method_to_determine_struct.name: _refine.pdbx_method_to_determine_struct
_refine.pdbx_method_to_determine_struct.description: Method(s) used to determine the structure.

_refine.pdbx_starting_model.name: _refine.pdbx_starting_model
_refine.pdbx_starting_model.description: Starting model for refinement.  Starting model for molecular replacement should refer to a previous structure or experiment.

_refine.pdbx_stereochemistry_target_valu.name: _refine.pdbx_stereochemistry_target_values
_refine.pdbx_stereochemistry_target_valu.description: Stereochemistry target values used in refinement.

_refine.pdbx_R_Free_selection_details.name: _refine.pdbx_R_Free_selection_details
_refine.pdbx_R_Free_selection_details.description: Details of the manner in which the cross validation reflections were selected.

_refine.pdbx_stereochem_target_val_spec_.name: _refine.pdbx_stereochem_target_val_spec_case
_refine.pdbx_stereochem_target_val_spec_.description: Special case of stereochemistry target values used in SHELXL refinement.

_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_R.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_R
_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_R.description: Overall estimated standard uncertainties of positional parameters based on R value.

_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_R_Free.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_R_Free
_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_R_Free.description: Overall estimated standard uncertainties of positional parameters based on R free value.

_refine.pdbx_solvent_vdw_probe_radii.name: _refine.pdbx_solvent_vdw_probe_radii
_refine.pdbx_solvent_vdw_probe_radii.description: For bulk solvent mask calculation, the value by which the vdw radii of non-ion atoms (like carbon) are increased and used.

_refine.pdbx_solvent_ion_probe_radii.name: _refine.pdbx_solvent_ion_probe_radii
_refine.pdbx_solvent_ion_probe_radii.description: For bulk solvent mask calculation, the amount that the ionic radii of atoms, which can be ions, are increased used.

_refine.pdbx_solvent_shrinkage_radii.name: _refine.pdbx_solvent_shrinkage_radii
_refine.pdbx_solvent_shrinkage_radii.description: For bulk solvent mask calculation, amount mask is shrunk after taking away atoms with new radii and a constant value assigned to this new region.

_refine.pdbx_real_space_R.name: _refine.pdbx_real_space_R
_refine.pdbx_real_space_R.description:
Real space R factor of electron density for all atoms.

The real space R factor is calculated by the equation

R_real = [Sum~i (|Dobs - Dcal|)]/[Sum~i (|Dobs + Dcal|)]

Where: Dobs is the observed  electron density, Dcal is the calculated  electron density, summation is for all the grid points

Ref: Branden, C.I. & Jones, T.A. (1990).  Nature, 343, 687-689

_refine.pdbx_density_correlation.name: _refine.pdbx_density_correlation
_refine.pdbx_density_correlation.description:
The density correlation coefficient is calculated from atomic densities of (2Fobs-Fcalc) map - "Robs" and the model map (Fcalc) - "Rcalc" :

D_corr =  <Robs><Rcalc>/sqrt(<Robs**2><Rcalc**2>)

where <Robs> is the mean of "observed" densities of all atoms

<Rcalc> is the mean of "calculated" densities of all atoms.

The value of density for some atom from map R(x) is:

sum_i ( R(xi) * Ratom(xi - xa) ) Dens =  ---------------------------------- sum_i ( Ratom(xi - xa) )

where  Ratom(x) is atomic electron density for the x-th grid point. xa - vector of the centre of atom. xi - vector of the i-th point of grid. Sum is taken over all grid points which have distance from the center of the atom less than the Radius_limit. For all atoms Radius_limit = 2.5 A.

Ref: Vaguine, A.A., Richelle, J. & Wodak, S.J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55,199-205

_refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_powder_pattern.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_powder_patterns
_refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_powder_pattern.description: The total number of powder patterns used.

_refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_points.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_points
_refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_points.description: The total number of data points in the processed diffractogram.

_refine.pdbx_pd_meas_number_of_points.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_meas_number_of_points
_refine.pdbx_pd_meas_number_of_points.description: The total number of points in the measured diffractogram.

_refine.pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_R_factor.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_R_factor
_refine.pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_R_factor.description: Rietveld/Profile fit R factors. Note that the R factor computed for Rietveld refinements using the extracted reflection intensity values (often called the Rietveld or Bragg R factor, R~B~) is not properly a profile R factor. pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_R_factor, often called R~p~, is an unweighted fitness metric for the agreement between the observed and computed diffraction patterns R~p~ = sum~i~ | I~obs~(i) - I~calc~(i) | / sum~i~ ( I~obs~(i) ) Note that in the above equations, w(i) is the weight for the ith data point I~obs~(i) is the observed intensity for the ith data point, sometimes referred to as y~i~(obs) or y~oi~. I~calc~(i) is the computed intensity for the ith data point with background and other corrections applied to match the scale of the observed dataset, sometimes referred to as y~i~(calc) or y~ci~. n is the total number of data points (see _refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_points) less the number of data points excluded from the refinement. p is the total number of refined parameters.

_refine.pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_wR_factor.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_wR_factor
_refine.pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_wR_factor.description: Rietveld/Profile fit R factors. Note that the R factor computed for Rietveld refinements using the extracted reflection intensity values (often called the Rietveld or Bragg R factor, R~B~) is not properly a profile R factor. pdbx_pd_proc_ls_prof_wR_factor often called R~wp~, is a weighted fitness metric for the agreement between the observed and computed diffraction patterns R~wp~ = SQRT { sum~i~ ( w(i) [ I~obs~(i) - I~calc~(i) ]^2^ ) / sum~i~ ( w(i) [I~obs~(i)]^2^ ) } Note that in the above equations, w(i) is the weight for the ith data point I~obs~(i) is the observed intensity for the ith data point, sometimes referred to as y~i~(obs) or y~oi~. I~calc~(i) is the computed intensity for the ith data point with background and other corrections applied to match the scale of the observed dataset, sometimes referred to as y~i~(calc) or y~ci~. n is the total number of data points (see _refine.pdbx_pd_number_of_points) less the number of data points excluded from the refinement. p is the total number of refined parameters.

_refine.pdbx_pd_Marquardt_correlation_co.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_Marquardt_correlation_coeff
_refine.pdbx_pd_Marquardt_correlation_co.description: The correlation coefficient between the observed and calculated structure factors for reflections included in the refinement. This correlation factor is found in the fitting using the Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm to search for the minimum value of chisquare. Almost all computer codes for Rietveld refinement employ the Gauss-Newton algorithm to find parameters which minimize the weighted sum of squares of the residuals. A description of the equations is given on http://www.water.hut.fi/~tkarvone/fr_org_s.htm

_refine.pdbx_pd_Fsqrd_R_factor.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_Fsqrd_R_factor
_refine.pdbx_pd_Fsqrd_R_factor.description: Residual factor R for reflections that satisfy the resolution limits established by _refine.ls_d_res_high and _refine.ls_d_res_low and the observation limit established by _reflns.observed_criterion. sum|F~obs~**2 - F~calc~**2| R = --------------------- sum|F~obs~**2| F~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitudes F~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitudes sum is taken over the specified reflections

_refine.pdbx_pd_ls_matrix_band_width.name: _refine.pdbx_pd_ls_matrix_band_width
_refine.pdbx_pd_ls_matrix_band_width.description: The least squares refinement "band matrix" approximation to the full matrix.

_refine.pdbx_overall_phase_error.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_phase_error
_refine.pdbx_overall_phase_error.description: The overall phase error for all reflections after refinement using the current refinement target.

_refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_free_Cruicksha.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_free_Cruickshank_DPI
_refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_free_Cruicksha.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of the displacement parameters based on the crystallographic R-free value, expressed in a formalism known as the dispersion precision indicator (DPI).

Ref: Cruickshank, D. W. J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55, 583-601.

_refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_free_Blow_DPI.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_free_Blow_DPI
_refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_free_Blow_DPI.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of the displacement parameters based on the crystallographic R-free value, expressed in a formalism known as the dispersion precision indicator (DPI).

Ref: Blow, D (2002) Acta Cryst. D58, 792-797

_refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_Blow_DPI.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_Blow_DPI
_refine.pdbx_overall_SU_R_Blow_DPI.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of the displacement parameters based on the crystallographic R value, expressed in a formalism known as the dispersion precision indicator (DPI).

Ref: Blow, D (2002) Acta Cryst. D58, 792-797

_refine.pdbx_TLS_residual_ADP_flag.name: _refine.pdbx_TLS_residual_ADP_flag
_refine.pdbx_TLS_residual_ADP_flag.description: A flag for TLS refinements identifying the type of atomic displacement parameters stored in _atom_site.B_iso_or_equiv.
enums: Residual B values  (excluding TLS contributions) are likely specified for some or all atom sites

_refine.pdbx_diffrn_id.name: _refine.pdbx_diffrn_id
_refine.pdbx_diffrn_id.description:
An identifier for the diffraction data set used in this refinement.

Multiple diffraction data sets specified as a comma separated list.

_refine.overall_SU_B.name: _refine.overall_SU_B
_refine.overall_SU_B.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of the displacement parameters based on a maximum-likelihood residual.
